title: M3a: UiBridge in-memory impl + SSE fan-out
description:
**Goal:** Default UiBridge impl. File: `src/server/ui-bridge/createInMemoryBridge.ts` (~100 LOC).

**Shape:**
```ts
export function createInMemoryUiBridge(): UiBridge {
  const state = new Map<string, UiState>()           // keyed by workspaceId (single entry in v1)
  const queue = new Map<string, Array<UiCommand & {seq: number}>>()
  const subscribers = new Map<string, Set<Handler>>()
  let nextSeq = 1
  return {
    async getState(wsId) { return state.get(wsId) ?? null },
    async setState(wsId, s) { state.set(wsId, s) },
    async postCommand(wsId, cmd) {
      const seq = nextSeq++
      const annotated = { ...cmd, seq }
      const q = queue.get(wsId) ?? []
      q.push(annotated); queue.set(wsId, q)
      subscribers.get(wsId)?.forEach(h => h(annotated))
      return { seq, status: 'ok' }
    },
    subscribeCommands(wsId, h) { /* returns unsubscribe */ },
  }
}
```

**Invariants:**
- Commands dropped from queue AFTER delivery (pull via subscribe OR poll via `?poll=true`).
- No persistence across backend restarts (accepted risk posture — matches spec §Risks).
- Monotonic `seq` per workspace (NOT global — workspace-scoped ordering).

**Total agent-visible tools in v1: 6** (bash, read, write, edit, get_ui_state, exec_ui). Plus the conditional `execute_isolated_code` if a VM sandbox with `'isolated-code'` capability is wired (M6+ future).

**Tests:**
- postCommand increments seq monotonically per workspaceId.
- Subscribers receive commands in order.
- Unsubscribe stops delivery (no leaked closures).
- setState + getState roundtrip.
- Multiple subscribers on same workspaceId all receive each command.
- Commands dropped after delivery (queue empty after flush).
- Backend restart = in-memory reset (no persistence).

**Logging:** `[bridge]` prefix. Log setState (level=debug with state keys only, not values — privacy). Log postCommand seq + kind. Log subscribe/unsubscribe lifecycle events at level=debug.

## Acceptance Criteria

Interface satisfied: all 4 methods work. Monotonic seq per workspace. Subscribers deliver in order. Unsubscribe clean. State roundtrip. No cross-workspace leakage. Memory bounded (commands dropped after delivery; subscribers cleaned on unsubscribe).
acceptance_criteria: postCommand increments seq. Subscribers receive commands. Unsubscribe stops delivery. setState+getState roundtrip.
close_reason: shipped

title: Phase 1: Zustand Store + Persistence Engine
description:
## Goal
Single Zustand store (store/index.ts) with partitioned persist middleware. Two localStorage keys: layout (per-workspace) and preferences (global). Bridge state is ephemeral (not persisted).

## Background (tenth pass decision)
One useWorkspaceStore(). Persist middleware uses partialize to route: layout→localStorage, preferences→localStorage, bridge state→ephemeral. store/index.ts is the single file. persistence/ and bridge/ are thin modules reading/writing this store.

## What to implement

### store/index.ts — the single Zustand store
- NOT exported from public API (enforced by API design)
- State shape:
  - hydrationComplete: boolean
  - layout: SerializedDockviewLayout | null
  - sidebar: { collapsed: boolean, width: number }
  - panelSizes: Record<string, number>
  - preferences: { theme: 'light' | 'dark' }
  - Bridge state (ephemeral, NOT persisted):
    - panels: PanelState[]
    - activePanel: string | null
    - activeFile: string | null
    - visibleFiles: string[]
    - dirtyFiles: Map<string, { panelId: string, savedAt: number | null }>
    - notifications: Notification[]
- Actions: openFile, openPanel, closePanel, showNotification, navigateToLine, markDirty, markClean, setHydrationComplete, etc.

### store/selectors.ts — atomic hooks (the ONLY public store API)
- useActiveFile() = useWorkspaceStore(s => s.activeFile)
- useActivePanel() = useWorkspaceStore(s => s.activePanel)
- useSidebarState() = useWorkspaceStore(s => s.sidebar)
- useOpenPanels() = useWorkspaceStore(s => s.panels)
- useDirtyFiles() = useWorkspaceStore(s => s.dirtyFiles)
- These are the ONLY exported hooks. useWorkspaceStore is internal.

### Persistence middleware configuration
- Two partitions via partialize:
  - boring-ui-v2:layout:{workspaceId} — layout, sidebar, panelSizes (reset on schema change)
  - boring-ui-v2:preferences — theme (persists forever, never reset)
- workspaceId falls back to omitted (key = boring-ui-v2:layout) if not provided
- Version field: '2.0' (string). Mismatch → call onLayoutVersionMismatch() callback (default: reset)

### Persistence hardening
- Zod schema validation on restore: reject negative dimensions, path separators in panel IDs, strings >1KB
- QuotaExceededError handling: try/catch on localStorage.setItem(). On quota error, clear layout key and retry once. If still failing, disable persistence for session + toast.
- Cross-tab: listen to window.addEventListener('storage') for layout key changes. Do NOT auto-apply (last-writer-wins, documented). Just log.
- Size budget: target <50KB serialized. Log warning if >100KB.

### persistence/useLayoutPersistence.ts
- Hook that wires dockview onDidLayoutChange → store
- Debounced 300ms (onDidLayoutChange fires per-pixel during sash drags)
- Flush on beforeunload
- Auto-restore on mount via Zustand onRehydrateStorage

### Hydration gate (Risk 7 — CRITICAL)
- onRehydrateStorage fires → set hydrationComplete = true
- DockviewShell gates onReady behind this flag (see dockview bead)
- If not hydrated yet → queue onReady, execute after hydration
- Show LoadingSkeleton until hydrated

Hydration gate pseudo-code (from plan Risk 7 mitigation):

```typescript
// store/index.ts — single store, partitioned persist (tenth pass)
export const useWorkspaceStore = create<WorkspaceStoreState>()(
  persist(
    (set, get) => ({
      hydrationComplete: false,
      layout: null,
      // ... other state
    }),
    {
      name: 'boring-ui-v2:layout',
      onRehydrateStorage: () => (state) => {
        // Fires AFTER hydration completes
        state?.setHydrationComplete(true)
      },
    }
  )
)
```

DockviewShell integration (consuming the hydration gate):

```typescript
// layouts/DockviewShell.tsx
function DockviewShell(props: DockviewShellProps) {
  const hydrationComplete = useWorkspaceStore(s => s.hydrationComplete)
  const pendingOnReady = useRef<DockviewReadyEvent | null>(null)

  const handleReady = useCallback((event: DockviewReadyEvent) => {
    if (!hydrationComplete) {
      pendingOnReady.current = event
      return
    }
    initializeDockview(event)
  }, [hydrationComplete])

  // Execute queued onReady when hydration completes
  useEffect(() => {
    if (hydrationComplete && pendingOnReady.current) {
      initializeDockview(pendingOnReady.current)
      pendingOnReady.current = null
    }
  }, [hydrationComplete])

  if (!hydrationComplete) return <LoadingSkeleton />

  return <DockviewReact onReady={handleReady} ... />
}
```

### Zod schema validation specifics
- Layout partition restore validation:
  - All dimension numbers (width, height, minWidth, etc.) must be >= 0
  - Panel IDs must be alphanumeric + dash, max 64 chars (no path separators, no null bytes)
  - String fields max 1KB (prevents oversized data injection)
  - layout field must be valid DockviewSerializedLayout shape (groups array, panels map)
  - sidebar.width must be positive integer
  - theme must be 'light' | 'dark' (reject unknown strings)
- On validation failure: log error, return null (triggers default state), toast "Layout restored with defaults"

## localStorage key inventory (namespace collision avoidance)
v1 scatters state across 12+ keys. v2 consolidates to two keys:

| v1 key pattern | v2 status |
|----------------|-----------|
| boring-ui:{prefix}:{projectRoot}:layout | → boring-ui-v2:layout:{workspaceId} |
| boring-ui:{prefix}:{projectRoot}:tabs | Dropped (derived from layout) |
| boring-ui:{prefix}:{projectRoot}:lastKnownGoodLayout | Dropped |
| boring-ui-theme / kurt-web-theme | → boring-ui-v2:preferences |
| boring-ui:chat-sessions:v1 | Agent package responsibility |
| boring-ui:terminal-* | Dropped (terminal → agent) |

## v1 references
- LayoutManager.js (618 lines) — save/restore, toJSON serialization, validation
- DROP: version migration system, lastKnownGoodLayout backup, multi-key storage
- KEEP: toJSON/fromJSON round-trip, validation pattern

## Risk mitigations embedded
- **Risk 4** (Persistence resets during development): Schema changes are rare after Phase 1 stabilizes. Simple version check (version !== current → reset) is already in place. No migration system needed.
- **Risk 7** (Zustand hydration race — CRITICAL): See hydration gate pseudo-code above. Zustand persist middleware onRehydrateStorage fires AFTER hydration. DockviewShell gates onReady behind hydrationComplete flag. Cleaner than v1's ref-based approach.
- **Risk 12** (Persisted layout shadows new LayoutConfig): LayoutConfig includes version field. Persisted layout stores the config version. On restore, versions differ → call onLayoutVersionMismatch() callback (defaults to reset). Apps can provide custom migration.

## Unit tests (store/persistence.test.ts)

### Store initialization
- Test: store initializes with correct default shape (all fields present, hydrationComplete=false)
- Test: bridge state fields start as empty (panels=[], activeFile=null, dirtyFiles empty)

### Persistence middleware
- Test: layout partition writes to localStorage under correct key (boring-ui-v2:layout)
- Test: preferences partition writes to localStorage under correct key (boring-ui-v2:preferences)
- Test: workspaceId scoping: different workspaceId → different layout key
- Test: bridge state (panels, activeFile, activePanel) is NOT written to localStorage
- Test: theme persists independently of layout (changing layout key doesn't affect theme)

### Zod validation on restore
- Test: valid layout JSON → restores correctly
- Test: negative dimensions → rejected, defaults applied
- Test: panel IDs with path separators (../../) → rejected
- Test: strings >1KB → rejected
- Test: missing required fields → rejected, defaults applied
- Test: corrupted JSON (not valid JSON) → rejected, defaults applied

### QuotaExceededError handling
- Test: mock localStorage.setItem throwing QuotaExceededError → clears key, retries
- Test: second QuotaExceededError → disables persistence for session
- Test: toast notification fires on persistent quota error

### Hydration gate
- Test: hydrationComplete starts false
- Test: onRehydrateStorage → hydrationComplete becomes true
- Test: subscribers notified when hydrationComplete changes

### Cross-tab storage events
- Test: storage event on layout key from other tab → logged (not applied)
- Test: storage event on unrelated key → ignored

### Size budget
- Test: serialized state >100KB → warning logged
- Test: serialized state <50KB → no warning

### Atomic selector hooks
- Test: useActiveFile returns correct value
- Test: useActivePanel returns correct value
- Test: useSidebarState returns correct value
- Test: useOpenPanels returns correct value
- Test: useDirtyFiles returns correct value
- Test: selectors only re-render when their specific slice changes

## Success criteria
- Zustand store initializes with correct shape
- Persist middleware writes to correct localStorage keys
- workspaceId scoping works (different keys per workspace)
- Hydration gate blocks DockviewShell until store is ready
- Zod validation rejects corrupted state gracefully (fallback to defaults)
- QuotaExceededError handled (disable persistence + toast, not crash)
- Atomic selector hooks return correct slices
- useWorkspaceStore is NOT importable from package public API
close_reason: Zustand store with partitioned persistence (layout per-workspace, preferences global), Zod validation, QuotaExceededError handling with notification, hydration gate, 7 selector hooks, cleanup(). 56 tests, 3 rounds of cross-review. Commit 862e7f5.

title: M2: vercel-sandbox RuntimeModeAdapter
description:
**Goal:** Self-contained `vercel-sandbox` mode module. File: `src/server/runtime/modes/vercel-sandbox.ts`.

**Shape:**
```ts
export const vercelSandboxMode: RuntimeModeAdapter = {
  id: 'vercel-sandbox',
  async create(ctx) {
    const sandbox = await resolveSandboxHandle(ctx.workspaceId, store, vercelClient)
    const workspace = createVercelSandboxWorkspace(sandbox)
    const exec = createVercelSandboxExec(sandbox)
    await exec.init({ workspace, sessionId: ctx.sessionId })
    return { workspace, sandbox: exec, fileSearch: createServerFileSearch(workspace, exec), uiBridge: ctx.uiBridge }
  },
}
```

**Config (required for this mode):**
- `VERCEL_OIDC_TOKEN` — auth token (short-lived; see `risk.oidc-refresh`)
- `VERCEL_TEAM_ID` — billing scope
- `BORING_AGENT_MODE=vercel-sandbox` — explicit opt-in

**Invariants:**
- Pairing via construction (same handle passed to workspace + exec).
- `await exec.init({workspace, sessionId})` — init hook for any one-time bootstrap (e.g., cd to /vercel/sandbox, set git config).

**Cold start + pricing reference (from spec §Vercel Sandbox):**
- **Cold start:** 2–10s for `Sandbox.create({source:git})` without a snapshot; sub-second with snapshot. See `research.vercel-cold-start-measure` for real numbers.
- **Hibernation:** VMs hibernate after 5 min idle; resume is ~1–2s.
- **Pricing model:** billed per compute-second (active) + snapshot storage (GB-hour). Idle hibernated VMs are essentially free.
- **API calls:** metered but cheap.
- **Container images:** `node24`, `node22`, `python3.13` available; changeable via `[sandbox.vercel].runtime`.

**Why Vercel vs Cloudflare Sandbox (spec §Future modes):** Vercel ships Firecracker + fs APIs. Cloudflare Sandbox is a potential parallel adapter; not in v1.

**Tests:**
- Mode creates a working bundle against a mocked VercelSandbox.
- Env var check failures produce helpful error messages (`VERCEL_OIDC_TOKEN is required for vercel-sandbox mode`).
- Pairing: write via workspace, read via exec — same content.

**Logging:** `[vercel-sandbox:mode]` prefix; log each handle create/resume with sandboxId + snapshotId.

## Acceptance Criteria

Mode creates working bundle against mocked VercelSandbox. Missing env vars produce helpful errors. Pairing verified: write via workspace → read via exec same content.
acceptance_criteria: Mode creates working bundle against mocked VercelSandbox. Missing env vars produce helpful errors. Pairing verified: write via workspace → read via exec same content.
close_reason: shipped — reviewed by claude: ship

title: Phase 2: Markdown Editor Pane (Tiptap)
description:
## Goal
Port v1's tiptap editor with 10 extensions (reduced from 16). Standalone MarkdownEditor + dockview pane wrapper.

## What to implement

### MarkdownEditor (src/components/MarkdownEditor.tsx) — STANDALONE, Tier 3
- Props-only, zero context dependency (tenth pass)
- Props: content: string, onChange: (content: string) => void, readOnly?: boolean, className?: string
- Tiptap with 10 extensions (see full inventory below)
- DROPPED: Table suite (4 pkgs), ImageResize (third-party), DiffExtension (deferred with diff mode)
- Toolbar: Bold, Italic, Underline, Strikethrough, H1/H2/H3, Bullet/Ordered/Task list, Quote, Code block, Link, Horizontal rule, Highlight, Image (URL prompt)
- Single mode: normal edit (no diff modes)
- Restyle with shadcn/tailwind (replace v1's custom CSS classes)

### MarkdownEditorPane (src/panes/MarkdownEditorPane.tsx) — dockview wrapper
- Reads/writes via DataProvider
- Uses useEditorLifecycle hook for dirty tracking + auto-save (1000ms)
- Tab dirty indicator
- External file change detection (same as code editor)
- Lazy-loaded (~300KB gz with tiptap bundles)

### Tiptap extension inventory (Appendix J — detailed)

| # | Extension | Package | v2 status |
|---|-----------|---------|-----------|
| 1 | StarterKit | @tiptap/starter-kit | KEEP |
| 2 | Underline | @tiptap/extension-underline | KEEP |
| 3 | Link | @tiptap/extension-link | KEEP |
| 4 | Placeholder | @tiptap/extension-placeholder | KEEP |
| 5 | TaskList + TaskItem | @tiptap/extension-task-list, @tiptap/extension-task-item | KEEP |
| 6 | TextAlign | @tiptap/extension-text-align | KEEP |
| 7 | Highlight | @tiptap/extension-highlight | KEEP |
| 8 | Image | @tiptap/extension-image | REPLACE (official, no resize handles) |
| 9 | CodeBlockLowlight | @tiptap/extension-code-block-lowlight | KEEP |
| 10 | Markdown | @tiptap/markdown (or tiptap-markdown) | KEEP |

**Dropped extensions (6 packages removed):**
| Extension | Package | Reason |
|-----------|---------|--------|
| Table | @tiptap/extension-table | Table suite = 4 packages, rarely used |
| TableRow | @tiptap/extension-table-row | Part of table suite |
| TableCell | @tiptap/extension-table-cell | Part of table suite |
| TableHeader | @tiptap/extension-table-header | Part of table suite |
| ImageResize | tiptap-extension-resize-image | Third-party, unreliable |
| DiffExtension | Inline in Editor.jsx | DEFERRED — returns when git diff viewing ships (v1.x) |

Result: 10 extensions (down from 16). Dropped 6 packages (including DiffExtension — deferred with diff mode).

### Risk 8: Keybinding isolation (shared with Code Editor)
- tiptap: editor.setEditable(false) on blur/deactivate, true on focus/activate
- Managed by useEditorFocusManagement hook (see Code Editor bead for full pseudo-code)
- Workspace-level shortcuts (Cmd+P, Cmd+B sidebar) always win

### HTML paste sanitization (SECURITY — CSP compatibility)
- Configure HTMLSanitizer to strip:
  - <script> tags
  - on* event handler attributes
  - javascript: URLs
  - <iframe> tags
- Default tiptap does NOT sanitize HTML paste — must configure explicitly
- CSP: style-src 'unsafe-inline' required by some tiptap extensions

### Performance budgets
| Metric | Target | Measured by |
|--------|--------|-------------|
| First tiptap render | <500ms | Playwright timer |
| Chunk size (tiptap bundles) | ~300KB gz | vite build analysis |
| Total all-loaded ceiling share | Must fit within 800KB total workspace budget | |

## v1 references
- Editor.jsx (912 lines) — PORT directly, restyle with shadcn/tailwind
- Keep: toolbar, task lists, code blocks with syntax highlighting
- Drop: FrontmatterEditor, Table suite, ImageResize, DiffExtension

## Considerations
- Tiptap HTML paste must be sanitized (security-critical)
- CSP: style-src 'unsafe-inline' required by some tiptap extensions
- Both editors share useEditorLifecycle hook for dirty/auto-save (Risk 16 mitigation)

## Unit tests (components/MarkdownEditor.test.tsx)

### Standalone MarkdownEditor
- Test: renders with content prop
- Test: onChange fires when content edited
- Test: readOnly mode prevents editing
- Test: all 10 extensions load without errors

### Toolbar functionality
- Test: Bold button toggles bold formatting
- Test: Italic button toggles italic
- Test: Heading buttons (H1/H2/H3) apply correct heading level
- Test: Bullet list / Ordered list / Task list creation
- Test: Code block creation with syntax highlighting
- Test: Link insertion (URL prompt)
- Test: Image insertion (URL prompt)
- Test: Highlight toggle
- Test: Horizontal rule insertion

### HTML paste sanitization (SECURITY)
- Test: pasted HTML with <script> tags → scripts stripped
- Test: pasted HTML with onclick handlers → on* attributes stripped
- Test: pasted HTML with javascript: URLs → URLs stripped
- Test: pasted HTML with <iframe> → iframes stripped
- Test: clean HTML paste works correctly (formatting preserved)

### MarkdownEditorPane
- Test: loads file content via DataProvider
- Test: saves via useFileWrite on auto-save trigger
- Test: dirty indicator shows when content modified
- Test: external file change auto-syncs when not dirty
- Test: lazy-loaded via React.lazy

### Keybinding isolation
- Test: inactive tiptap editor does not capture keystrokes (setEditable(false) on deactivate)
- Test: Cmd+P always triggers workspace command palette, not tiptap

## Success criteria
- All 10 extensions work correctly
- Toolbar buttons toggle formatting
- Standalone MarkdownEditor works without WorkspaceProvider
- Pane loads/saves via DataProvider, tracks dirty state
- HTML paste sanitized (no XSS vectors)
close_reason: done

title: Research: Vercel Sandbox cold-start + snapshot UX measurement
description:
**Goal:** Measure actual Vercel Sandbox cold-start times + snapshot benefits. Spec §Open research #9. Informs `risk.vercel-cold-start-ux` mitigation design.

**Why it matters:** design decisions about async provisioning, "preparing workspace…" UX, and snapshot bake frequency depend on real numbers.

**Methodology:**

**Phase 1 — benchmark setup:**
1. Dedicated Vercel team/project for benchmarking (avoid polluting prod billing).
2. Test script `scripts/bench-vercel-cold-start.ts` that:
   - Creates a fresh sandbox 10 times back-to-back (cold).
   - Measures: time to `Sandbox.create()` resolving; time to first successful `runCommand('echo hi')` (fully ready).
   - Tears down each sandbox after measuring.
3. Three scenarios per run:
   - `source: 'empty'` — baseline.
   - `source: { tarball: <small-url> }` — small template (~100KB).
   - `source: { snapshotId }` — pre-baked snapshot (from `m2.snapshot-bake`).

**Phase 2 — data collection:**
- Run benchmark script 3 times (morning, afternoon, evening — Vercel load varies).
- Record: p50, p95, p99 latencies per scenario.
- Note: region is `iad1` per spec; EU/APAC users will see +100–200ms per fs op.

**Phase 3 — analysis + recommendation:**
- Compare: does snapshot really win 10x as spec suggests?
- UX threshold: if cold > 3s, we NEED the async polling UX + "preparing workspace…" card (triggers `risk.vercel-cold-start-ux` implementation).
- If cold < 1s: simple spinner suffices; no polling needed.

**Deliverable (format):** `docs/PERFORMANCE.md` new section with:
```
## Vercel Sandbox cold-start benchmark (2026-XX-YY)

| Scenario | p50 | p95 | p99 |
|---|---|---|---|
| source: empty | 420 ms | 710 ms | 980 ms |
| source: tarball (~100KB) | 1.8 s | 3.2 s | 4.1 s |
| source: snapshot | 230 ms | 380 ms | 540 ms |

**Conclusion:** snapshot-based is the production pattern (matches spec line 1185).
**UX impact:** tarball cold-start > 3s p95 — async polling UX required (risk.vercel-cold-start-ux).
```

**Follow-up bead trigger:** if numbers differ materially from spec estimates, update `m2.vercel-mode` bead + consider design changes.

**Logging:** `[bench]` prefix. CSV output to `bench-results/vercel-cold-start-<date>.csv` for archival.

## Acceptance Criteria

Benchmark script runs reproducibly. Results published in `docs/PERFORMANCE.md`. Numbers inform design: async-polling UX implemented if cold > 3s p95, simple spinner otherwise. Any significant deviation from spec numbers → follow-up bead filed.
close_reason: Implemented Vercel cold-start benchmark harness (scripts/bench-vercel-cold-start.ts), published CSV artifacts under packages/agent/bench-results, and documented results + UX thresholds in packages/agent/docs/PERFORMANCE.md. Cross-review via claude -p: SHIP (non-blocking notes only).

title: M3a: UI bridge routes (/api/v1/ui/*) — SSE + poll
description:
**Goal:** HTTP surface for the bridge. File: `src/server/http/routes/ui.ts` (~80 LOC).

**Routes:**
- `GET /api/v1/ui/state` → `bridge.getState()`.
- `PUT /api/v1/ui/state` body `{state, causedBy: 'user'|'agent'|'restore'}` → `bridge.setState(body.state)`. Ignore echoes based on causedBy.
- `GET /api/v1/ui/state/latest` → cached snapshot (same as GET /state; separate route for polling clients).
- `POST /api/v1/ui/commands` body `{kind, params}` → `bridge.postCommand(cmd)` → returns `{seq, status}`.
- `GET /api/v1/ui/commands/next` (SSE) → emits `event: command` for each bridge event. `?poll=true` → returns batch JSON `{commands: [...]}` and clears queue.

**Zod schemas** for command kinds (openFile, openPanel, showNotification, navigateToLine, expandToFile + extensible fallback).

**Tests:** state PUT+GET roundtrip; POST command → SSE subscriber receives; poll fallback returns queue batch + clears.

## Acceptance Criteria

state PUT+GET roundtrip. POST command → SSE subscriber receives. `?poll=true` returns queue batch + clears.
acceptance_criteria: state PUT+GET roundtrip. POST command → SSE subscriber receives. `?poll=true` returns queue batch + clears.
close_reason: shipped

title: M2-Risk: OIDC token refresh for Vercel SDK
description:
**Risk class:** Requires real mitigation (spec §Risks > Risks requiring real mitigation in v1).

**Problem:** Vercel Sandbox SDK authenticates via `VERCEL_OIDC_TOKEN`. OIDC tokens have short lifetimes (minutes to an hour). If the token expires mid-agent-session, every subsequent API call 401s → agent errors → user sees cryptic failures.

**Mitigation:**
- Intercept Vercel SDK 401/403 responses.
- Trigger a token refresh (exchange long-lived credentials or CI credentials for a new OIDC token). Reference: Vercel Sandbox docs on OIDC federation.
- Retry the failed call once with the fresh token.
- If refresh itself fails → surface a clear error to the user ("Vercel auth expired; restart with fresh `VERCEL_OIDC_TOKEN`").

**File:** `src/server/sandbox/vercel-sandbox/oidcRefresh.ts` (~40 LOC), injected into circuit breaker middleware.

**Tests:**
- Mock 401 on first call → refresh fires → second call succeeds.
- Mock sustained 401 → refresh fails → user-facing error with stable code (`OIDC_REFRESH_FAILED`).
- Token cached; second call within TTL doesn't re-refresh.

**Logging:** `[oidc]` prefix on refresh events; log token expiry + next-refresh-at timestamp. Redact actual token bytes.

**Acceptance:** sustained 30-min agent session succeeds even when each OIDC token lasts only 5 min.

## Acceptance Criteria

Sustained 30-min session succeeds even when OIDC tokens last only 5 min. Refresh-fail error has stable code OIDC_REFRESH_FAILED.
close_reason: shipped — reviewed by claude: ship. OIDC refresh helper + circuit-breaker auth-retry middleware landed (plus follow-up logger/comment polish) with passing scoped vercel-sandbox tests.

title: M1: /health + /ready endpoints (port validation + monitoring)
description:
**Goal:** Minimal health + readiness endpoints. File: `src/server/http/routes/health.ts` (~40 LOC).

**Endpoints:**

### `GET /health`
- Returns 200 `{status: 'ok', version: '<x.y.z>', uptime: <seconds>}` if the Fastify server is up.
- Does NOT check downstream (sandbox, pi, etc.) — this is a liveness probe, not readiness.
- No auth required (safe; no secrets in response).
- Used by: CLI port-picker (to confirm server boot), load balancers, ops monitoring.

### `GET /ready`
- Returns 200 `{status: 'ready'}` if the sandbox handle is initialized AND pi is loaded.
- Returns 503 `{status: 'provisioning', retryAfter: N}` during Vercel cold start (see `risk.vercel-cold-start-ux`).
- Returns 503 `{status: 'degraded', reason: '<code>'}` if circuit breaker is open.
- No auth required.
- Used by: frontend on mount (show "preparing workspace…" card if not ready), load balancers that distinguish liveness vs readiness.

### CLI port-picker usage (spec §CLI startup > step 6):
```ts
async function tryPort(port: number): Promise<boolean> {
  try { await fetch(`http://localhost:${port}/health`, {signal: AbortSignal.timeout(200)}); return true }
  catch { return false }
}
// Increment port until /health responds with our version string (not someone else's server).
```

**Security:** `/health` exposes version. Don't expose more (no build hash, no env, no uptime-in-nanoseconds — just coarse uptime).

**Tests:**
- `/health`: 200 + valid JSON shape with version + uptime.
- `/ready` during cold start (mocked): 503 with retryAfter.
- `/ready` after init: 200.
- `/ready` with circuit open: 503 with reason.
- No auth required (hit without token).

**Logging:** minimal — `[health]` on state transitions of `/ready` only. Don't log every `/health` poll (would spam).

## Acceptance Criteria

`GET /health` returns 200 + version + uptime with no auth. `GET /ready` distinguishes liveness vs readiness (200/503). CLI port-picker uses `/health` to disambiguate "server I started" vs "someone else on this port". Load balancers can wire `/health` (liveness) + `/ready` (rolling deploys).
close_reason: shipped — /health + /ready with auth bypass, 7 tests, codex ship round 2

title: M1 — Local mode MVP
description:
**~1 week.** Smallest end-to-end thing where a user types a message in the standalone app, agent runs `bash` once against a local workspace, response streams back. **No remote adapters yet.**

**Deliverables:**
- `createNodeWorkspace()` + ported path helpers.
- `createDirectSandbox()` + `createBwrapSandbox()` + bwrap args builder.
- `resolveMode(mode)` factory: auto-detect Linux+bwrap → `local`, else → `direct`. `vercel-sandbox` opt-in.
- `createPiCodingAgentHarness()` + pi → UIMessage stream adapter.
- Standard catalog (4 tools: bash, read, write, edit).
- Fastify routes: files (GET/POST/DELETE), tree, stat, search, move, dirs, agent/chat, agent/sessions.
- `<ChatPanel />` raw `useChat`, bare-bones. `<Composer />` with `<ModelPicker />` + `<ThinkingToggle />`.
- Standalone app wired (Vite + Fastify).
- CLI `bin/boring-agent`: flag parser, API-key prompt + persistence, port picker, SSH/headless browser-open, auto-gitignore, graceful shutdown, `--logout`/`--reset-key`/`--no-open`/`--no-gitignore` flags.
- Build pipeline: `pnpm build` → `dist/frontend/` + `dist/bin/boring-agent.js`.

**Gate:** user can say "list files in /tmp" and get a streamed reply with a `bash` tool call card + result. File tree route returns correct listings. No polish, no session persistence, no styling.

**Reference files from old boring-ui:**
- `packages/workspace/src/server/workspace/paths.ts` (validatePath helpers)
- `packages/workspace/src/server/adapters/bwrap.ts` (buildBwrapArgs — flag order security-critical)
- `packages/agent/src/server/harnesses/pi/runtime.ts` (adapt for pi-coding-agent)
- `packages/workspace/src/server/http/fileRoutes.ts` (port + adapt to Workspace interface)

## Success Criteria

User types 'list files in /tmp' in standalone app, receives streamed reply with a `bash` tool call card + result. File tree route returns correct listings. No polish, no session persistence, no styling.
acceptance_criteria: User types 'list files in /tmp' in standalone app, receives streamed reply with a `bash` tool call card + result. File tree route returns correct listings. No polish, no session persistence, no styling.
close_reason: M1 complete — all deliverables landed: path helpers, NodeWorkspace, DirectSandbox, BwrapSandbox+args, RuntimeModeAdapter, PiCodingAgentHarness, stream adapter, 4 tool factories, standardCatalog, all HTTP routes (files, tree, stat, search, chat, sessions), auth middleware, ChatPanel, Composer, useAgentChat, standalone app, CLI bin/boring-agent. Chat works end-to-end.

title: M4 — Plugin compatibility
description:
**~2–3 days.** Pi plugin ecosystem compat. Local mode only (plugins are Node-native; irrelevant for remote VM).

**Deliverables:**
- Wire pi's extension discovery through `createAgentSession` options (`~/.pi/agent/extensions/`, `.pi/extensions/`, npm, git).
- Surface plugin-registered tools in the catalog UI via fallback renderer.
- Resolve name-collision precedence (last-registered wins in v1; document).
- Smoke-test 2–3 community pi extensions.
- Document: in `vercel-sandbox` mode, plugins are not auto-loaded (user can still drop tools into `/vercel/sandbox/.pi/extensions/` manually).

**Gate:** dropping a file in `~/.pi/agent/extensions/hello.ts` surfaces a working tool in the chat (local mode only).

**Notes:** Plugin tools bypass our Sandbox by design — they run in the Node host process (node:fs, pi.exec). Our sandbox knob only governs default tools. Accepted trade-off: plugins are user-chosen code.

## Success Criteria

Dropping a file in `~/.pi/agent/extensions/hello.ts` surfaces a working tool in the chat (local mode only).
acceptance_criteria: Dropping a file in `~/.pi/agent/extensions/hello.ts` surfaces a working tool in the chat (local mode only).
close_reason: plugin compatibility verified post-refactors in 8c627cb; cross-reviewed by claude: ship; added real local plugin file smoke through loadPlugins + createAgentApp catalog wiring

title: M0: Interface — Workspace + Entry + Stat
description:
**Goal:** Lock the Layer-1 workspace contract. File: `src/shared/workspace.ts`.

**Interface:**
```ts
export interface Workspace {
  readonly root: string                           // absolute path or opaque URI (browser)
  readFile(relPath: string): Promise<string>
  writeFile(relPath: string, data: string): Promise<void>
  unlink(relPath: string): Promise<void>          // removes a file OR an empty directory
  readdir(relPath: string): Promise<Entry[]>
  stat(relPath: string): Promise<Stat>
  mkdir(relPath: string, opts?: { recursive?: boolean }): Promise<void>
  rename(fromRelPath: string, toRelPath: string): Promise<void>   // handles drag-to-move AND in-place rename
}
export interface Entry { name: string; kind: 'file' | 'dir' }
export interface Stat { size: number; mtimeMs: number; kind: 'file' | 'dir' }
```

**Notes:**
- Platform-agnostic so future OPFS/IndexedDB browser impl satisfies it.
- Every impl MUST reject paths that escape workspace root (via realpath check, not string manipulation). Security-critical.
- `rename` unifies in-place rename and drag-and-drop move — same op, path change only.
- `unlink` removes files AND empty directories (matches `node:fs/promises.rm` single-entry semantics).

**Tests:** Type-level stub. A shared conformance test suite lands in the infra epic (see `infra.conformance-tests`).

## Acceptance Criteria

`src/shared/workspace.ts` exports `Workspace`, `Entry`, `Stat`. Includes all 7 methods: readFile/writeFile/unlink/readdir/stat/mkdir/rename. Type-level stub passes.
acceptance_criteria: `src/shared/workspace.ts` exports `Workspace`, `Entry`, `Stat`. Includes all 7 methods: readFile/writeFile/unlink/readdir/stat/mkdir/rename. Type-level stub passes.
close_reason: shipped — cross-reviewed by claude: ship

title: M3b: Background session title generation
description:
**Goal:** After the first assistant reply, generate a short title for the session. Fallback: `"New chat {date}"`.

**Flow:** on first turn complete, kick off a background request to LLM (cheap model: haiku) with prompt: `"Summarize this conversation in 5 words or less for a session title: {first user message} / {first assistant reply}"`. Write title back via SessionStore.

**Defer if it complicates M3b:** fallback is already good UX.

**Tests:** first turn → title populates within ~5s; falls back gracefully if LLM call fails.

## Acceptance Criteria

First turn → title populates within ~5s. LLM failure → fallback title. Title flows to SessionStore.
acceptance_criteria: First turn → title populates within ~5s. LLM failure → fallback title. Title flows to SessionStore.
close_reason: shipped — reviewed by claude: ship. session-title scheduler/tests landed in f2dc62e; final env-fallback hardening in ae3107b.

title: M2-Risk: Vercel cold-start UX (async provisioning + polling)
description:
**Risk class:** Requires real mitigation. Spec §Risks warns: first `Sandbox.create({source:'git'})` takes 2–10s; agents/users experience this as a frozen UI.

**Problem in agent v1 (no `/workspaces` route):** workspaces are bound to the server process via env. But first POST /api/v1/agent/chat to a newly-booted backend will lazily resolve the sandbox handle, hitting cold start inline — user's first message appears to hang.

**Mitigation (agent-v1 scope — simplified, since we dropped the state-machine route):**
- On backend boot in `vercel-sandbox` mode: kick off `resolveSandboxHandle(BORING_AGENT_WORKSPACE_ID)` asynchronously. Emit explicit status events (`restoring workspace`, `ready`) via a new SSE topic `/api/v1/ready-status`.
- Frontend subscribes to this on mount; shows a "Preparing workspace…" card above ChatPanel until status is `ready`.
- Chat route returns 503 with `{retryAfter: 2}` while status is not ready.

**Why not the full async-provisioning state machine:** agent v1 is single-workspace-per-backend; workspace CRUD belongs to `@boring/cloud`. The simpler pattern above achieves the UX without a full state machine.

**Tests:**
- Mocked cold-start: first chat before ready → 503. After ready event → 200 stream.
- Warm start (snapshot cached): ready event fires <500ms.
- E2E with real Vercel: cold start UX shows the "Preparing" card then disappears.

**Logging:** `[ready-status]` prefix; log each state transition with timestamp.

## Acceptance Criteria

Cold start: chat route returns 503 while sandbox not ready; 'Preparing workspace' card shown. After ready event: 200 stream. Warm start: ready event <500ms.

title: M0: Interface — FileSearch (decoupled from Workspace)
description:
**Goal:** Extract file search from Workspace — different backends use `find` (exec) or fs-walk (browser). File: `src/shared/file-search.ts`.

**Interface:**
```ts
export interface FileSearch {
  search(glob: string, limit?: number): Promise<string[]>
}
```

**Why separate from Workspace:**
- Server mode impl uses `sandbox.exec('find . -name …')` — needs both Workspace AND Sandbox refs.
- Browser mode impl does fs-walk over the Workspace interface directly.
- Extracting keeps Workspace pure (path-bound fs primitives) and lets FileSearch compose.

**Used by:** `/api/v1/files/search` route + potential future `grep`/`glob` agent tools.

## Acceptance Criteria

`src/shared/file-search.ts` exports `FileSearch { search(glob, limit?) }`. Type-level stub passes. Decoupled from Workspace.
acceptance_criteria: `src/shared/file-search.ts` exports `FileSearch { search(glob, limit?) }`. Type-level stub passes. Decoupled from Workspace.
close_reason: shipped — cross-reviewed by claude: ship

title: Migration: boring-macro — ai-sdk → pi + template transfer
description:
**Goal:** Migrate boring-macro to v2 agent. **Current:** bwrap backend + ai-sdk harness. **Target:** bwrap + pi-coding-agent. **HIGH STAKES** — boring-macro is live production.

**Step-by-step migration checklist:**

1. **Branch + parity env** — work on a `v2-migration` branch; spin up a staging Fly app to validate before prod cutover.

2. **Package deps:**
   - Remove: `ai-sdk` direct deps in boring-macro; any custom harness.
   - Add: `@boring/agent@workspace:*` (once published).

3. **Harness rewrite (the bulk of the work):**
   - Identify all tool definitions in boring-macro's current ai-sdk harness (grep for `tool({` or `defineTool`).
   - For each: translate to `AgentTool` shape: `{name, description, parameters (JSON Schema), execute(params, ctx)}`. Most tools port mechanically.
   - System prompt: boring-macro has a macro-specific system prompt (skills, style, context). Inject via pi's system-prompt API in `createAgentSession({ systemPrompt })`.

4. **Template transfer (skills, config files, READMEs):**
   - Locate boring-macro's template dir (check `apps/boring-macro/templates/` or similar).
   - Set `BORING_AGENT_TEMPLATE_PATH=/app/templates/default` in Fly secrets.
   - `copyTemplate` will seed new workspaces on first boot.

5. **Dockerfile — preserve bwrap install** (spec §Reference files > boring-macro's Fly Dockerfile):
   - Keep: `RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y bubblewrap` (spec line 1726).
   - Add: `COPY` step for `BORING_AGENT_TEMPLATE_PATH` contents if baking the template into the image.

6. **Env vars + Fly secrets (reuse existing Vault wiring):**
   - `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` (existing).
   - NEW: `BORING_AGENT_MODE=local`, `BORING_AGENT_WORKSPACE_ROOT=/app/workspace`, `BORING_AGENT_WORKSPACE_ID=<tenant-id>`.
   - Keep: existing session-secret / auth configs if applicable.

7. **Two-tier Python venv (local-libs-preinstall):**
   - Migrate boring-macro's `pip install pandas numpy scipy ...` Dockerfile line into `[sandbox].python_packages` TOML config.
   - OR: keep Dockerfile install path — both are supported.
   - Verify Python scripts from boring-macro's skills still run via the agent's `bash` tool.

8. **Auth / session cookies:** if boring-macro uses session cookies for multi-tenant auth (per memory file `memory/session-cookie-interop.md`), ensure v2 agent's `BORING_AGENT_AUTH_TOKEN` path compat.

9. **Parity testing:**
   - Unit: ported tool outputs match reference snapshots.
   - Integration: agent answers a known macro query with equivalent output.
   - E2E: full user flow (signup → workspace → chat → generated artifact).

10. **Canary deployment:**
   - Deploy to staging Fly app.
   - Internal team uses for 1 week.
   - Monitor for regressions (error rate, latency, user reports).

11. **Cutover:**
   - DNS / Fly alias switch.
   - Revert plan: previous ai-sdk version pinned and ready for rollback.

**Risks specific to this migration:**
- ai-sdk vs pi-coding-agent behavior differences (pi may be stricter about tool shapes).
- Session JSONL format change → historical sessions may need one-time conversion (or accept loss for fresh install).
- Model selection semantics — verify thinking levels match.

**Estimated effort:** 2–3 days coding + 1 week canary.

## Acceptance Criteria

boring-macro runs on Fly with v2 agent in local mode. All existing tools work. Template seeding produces expected skills/READMEs in fresh workspaces. Python-heavy workflows work. No user-visible regressions after 1-week canary. Rollback path documented + tested.

title: M3b: useSessions() headless hook (list/create/switch/delete)
description:
**Goal:** Expose `useSessions()` as a first-class headless primitive for Tier-3 consumers. File: `src/front/hooks/useSessions.ts` (~60 LOC).

**Why first-class (spec §UI export pattern):** every user-facing piece follows the "default + primitives + headless hook" contract. `<SessionToolbar />` (default), `<NewChatButton />` (primitive), `useSessions()` (headless) must ALL ship — not just the default. Workspace package consumes the hook directly to build custom session sidebars.

**Shape:**
```ts
export interface UseSessionsResult {
  sessions: SessionSummary[]
  activeSession: SessionSummary | undefined
  activeSessionId: string | undefined
  loading: boolean
  error: Error | undefined
  create: (init?: {title?: string}) => Promise<SessionSummary>
  switch: (id: string) => void              // sets active in local state + updates URL/localStorage
  delete: (id: string) => Promise<void>     // optimistic remove + DELETE /api/v1/agent/sessions/:id
  // rename — NOT in v1, deferred to workspace package
}

export function useSessions(opts?: { workspaceId?: string }): UseSessionsResult
```

**Contract from spec:** matches workspace plan's expected signature:
```ts
const { sessions, activeSession, create, switch: switchTo, delete: remove } = useSessions()
```

**Implementation notes:**
- Uses TanStack Query (React Query) for `GET /api/v1/agent/sessions` + cache invalidation on create/delete.
- Local state for `activeSessionId` (persisted to localStorage keyed by workspaceId).
- `create()` optimistic: adds session to cache immediately, POST fires in background; on failure, reverts + surfaces error.
- `delete()` optimistic: removes from cache, DELETE fires; on failure, re-adds + surfaces error.
- **No rename** in v1 — intentional. Workspace package can add rename via a separate hook/endpoint.

**Tests (vitest + React Testing Library):**
- `renderHook(() => useSessions())` against mock backend → returns empty list initially.
- `create()` adds a new session; second call creates another.
- `switch('id-x')` updates activeSessionId; persists to localStorage.
- `delete('id-x')` removes from list; second delete of same id → no-op (cache already clean).
- Network error during create → hook exposes `error`; list unchanged.

**Logging:** `[useSessions]` prefix on dev-mode console.debug for state transitions (create/switch/delete); no logs in prod builds.

## Acceptance Criteria

Hook exports the exact shape workspace plan expects. All 4 ops (list, create, switch, delete) work against a running backend. Optimistic updates roll back on error. localStorage persists activeSessionId per-workspace. Tests cover happy path + error recovery. Ships as named export from `@boring/agent` top-level barrel (browser-safe, no Node deps).

title: M3a: Local mode library pre-install (shared /opt/venv + workspace overlay)
description:
**Goal:** Two-tier Python/system package pre-install for local/bwrap mode. File: `src/server/sandbox/bwrap/libsPreinstall.ts` (~80 LOC). Spec section: §Environment provisioning > Libraries — local mode.

**Why needed:** production boring-macro currently manages Python deps via Dockerfile `RUN pip install …`. v2 needs to preserve that UX without locking users into writing custom Dockerfiles. Matches the spec's two-tier design that pairs local mode with remote mode (both have Tier 1 pre-baked + Tier 2 agent-driven overlay).

**Tier 1 — shared pre-installed venv:**
- Location: `/opt/venv` on the host container, bwrap bind-mounts read-only.
- Build paths:
  - **Dockerfile-built (fastest):** `python3 -m venv /opt/venv && /opt/venv/bin/pip install <packages>` in Dockerfile layer. No runtime cost.
  - **Runtime-built on first boot:** if `[sandbox].python_packages` set and `/opt/venv` missing → run `uv pip install …` at startup into `/var/cache/boring-agent/venvs/<hash>`, bwrap binds that path at `/opt/venv`. Cached by hash of package list — second boot with same config is instant.

**Tier 2 — per-workspace overlay:**
- Location: `${workspaceRoot}/.venv` created with `--system-site-packages` (so it inherits Tier 1).
- Created lazily on first agent `pip install <extra>` command.
- Persists across sessions (same workspace root → same overlay).

**Bwrap integration:**
- `buildBwrapArgs()` (see m1.bwrap-args) adds `--ro-bind /opt/venv /opt/venv` when venv exists.
- Python resolution: `PYTHONPATH=/opt/venv/lib/python3.12/site-packages:${workspace}/.venv/lib/python3.12/site-packages`.
- `PATH` includes `/opt/venv/bin:${workspace}/.venv/bin` so `python3` + `pip` resolve correctly.

**Config (TOML):**
```toml
[sandbox]
python_packages = ["pandas", "numpy", "scipy"]
system_packages = ["ripgrep", "jq"]            # apt-get install (local); dnf install (vercel bake)
```

**System packages:** on local mode, `system_packages` are assumed to be installed at image-build time (Dockerfile) — runtime `apt-get install` requires root. If not present at startup and `system_packages` configured, log warning + continue (agent will error at runtime if needed).

**Tests:**
- Hash-based cache: same package list → same cache dir → second boot skips install.
- Different package list → new cache dir → fresh install.
- Tier 2 overlay: `pip install requests` via bash tool → installs into `${workspace}/.venv` → second `python -c 'import requests'` succeeds.
- Missing `/opt/venv` + packages not configured → log info; agent can still run, `pip` errors at use.

**Logging:** `[libs-preinstall]` prefix. Log hash, cache path, package count, install duration.

## Acceptance Criteria

Boring-macro-style Python workflow works out of the box in local mode. Tier 1 pre-install works both via Dockerfile AND runtime-built path. Tier 2 overlay persists across sessions. Hash-based cache prevents redundant installs. `BORING_AGENT_PYTHON_PACKAGES` env override supported for quick dev workflows.
close_reason: shipped — two-tier venv preinstall (tier-1 /opt/venv hash-cached, tier-2 workspace overlay), buildBwrapArgs extraArgs, BORING_AGENT_PYTHON_PACKAGES env, 22 unit tests

title: M1: Tree route (GET /api/v1/tree)
description:
**Goal:** `GET /api/v1/tree?path=<dir>&recursive=<bool>` → `workspace.readdir(dir)` with optional shallow recursion. File: `src/server/http/routes/tree.ts`.

**Behavior:**
- Default: single-level listing of `path` (default: root).
- `recursive=true`: breadth-first traversal, max depth 10, max 5000 entries (safety cap).
- Returns `{ entries: [{name, kind, path}] }`.

**Tests:** root listing matches fs; recursive listing depth-capped; path traversal rejected.

**Reference:** Old `fileRoutes.ts` tree endpoint section.

## Acceptance Criteria

GET /api/v1/tree returns {entries:[{name,kind,path}]}. recursive=true depth-capped at 10; entry cap 5000. Path traversal rejected.
acceptance_criteria: GET /api/v1/tree returns {entries:[{name,kind,path}]}. recursive=true depth-capped at 10; entry cap 5000. Path traversal rejected.
close_reason: shipped — tree route with BFS recursion, depth/entry caps, traversal/ENOENT handling, 11 tests, codex ship round 3

title: Workspace Phase 4: Polish + Testing
description: Polish, testing, and deliverables for @boring/workspace v2. Theme system, command palette, error handling, accessibility, responsive layout, CSP, perf budgets, test harness, Playwright E2E, Storybook visual regression, Bombadil property testing, public API barrel, sample playground app.

title: M3a: PiSessionStore (JSONL-backed)
description:
**Goal:** Replace M1's in-memory store with a pi-JSONL-backed `SessionStore`. File: `src/server/harness/pi-coding-agent/sessions.ts` (~120 LOC).

**Backing store:** pi-coding-agent writes sessions to `${workspaceRoot}/.pi/sessions/<sessionId>.jsonl`. We read over that.

**Shape:**
```ts
export class PiSessionStore implements SessionStore {
  constructor(private workspaceRoot: string) {}
  async list(ctx) { /* scan .pi/sessions/*.jsonl → SessionSummary[] (parse first + last line for metadata) */ }
  async create(ctx, { title }) { /* generate uuid, create empty JSONL, return summary */ }
  async load(ctx, id) { /* stream JSONL → SessionEntry[] → UIMessage[] (conformance-tested mapping) */ }
  async delete(ctx, id) { /* fs.rm(path); remove pi's in-memory session if cached */ }
}
```

**Deliberately NO `append()` method** (spec §Session management): the harness writes messages as they stream; appends go through pi's loop, not through `SessionStore`. Rationale:
- Keeps the interface platform-agnostic (no "write a pi event" leak).
- Avoids concurrency footguns (two writers racing on the same session).
- Makes SessionStore a pure read+lifecycle interface.

**Read conversion pi SessionEntry → UIMessage:** called out as an "Open research" item (spec §Open research #6). Uses the same mapping as the live stream adapter (`m1.stream-adapter`). Conformance test in `risk.pi-session-mapping-conformance`.

**v1 defers (spec line 710-714):**
- Multi-user scoping (`SessionCtx.userId` ignored in v1).
- LLM-generated titles (default `"New chat {timestamp}"`; M3b background generation).
- Rename session flow (workspace package territory; delete ships).

**Tests:**
- Create+load roundtrip.
- List ordering by lastModified desc.
- Delete removes JSONL file.
- Malformed JSONL entries skipped with warning log (don't crash on corrupt files).
- Concurrent reads during pi write: assert list/load return consistent snapshots.
- Empty `.pi/sessions/` dir → list returns [].

**Logging:** `[session]` prefix. Log list-call count, create/delete events. Never log message contents.

## Acceptance Criteria

Implements all 4 SessionStore methods. Create+load roundtrip works. List ordered by lastModified desc. Delete removes JSONL. Malformed entries skipped with warning (no crash). Pi event → UIMessage mapping matches stream adapter conformance. No `append()` method. `userId` ignored but accepted in signature.
acceptance_criteria: create+load roundtrip. list ordered by updatedAt. delete removes JSONL. Malformed entries skipped with warning log.
close_reason: shipped

title: Phase 4: Accessibility
description:
## Goal
WCAG 2.1 AA target. Keyboard nav, focus management, ARIA labels, axe-core scanning.

## What to implement

### Keyboard navigation
- Tab through panels (panel focus order follows dockview group order)
- Escape to close dialogs/menus, close active modal
- Arrow keys in file tree (react-arborist provides this)
- Focus trap in modals (shadcn Dialog already handles this)

### Focus management
- Focus ring on active panel (2px solid var(--ring), offset 2px)
- Active panel receives focus on tab click
- On panel close, focus moves to next panel (or previous if closing last)
- Dockview's onDidActivePanelChange drives focus updates

### Screen reader support
- ARIA labels on major regions:
  - Sidebar: aria-label="File explorer"
  - Editor area: aria-label="Editor", role="tabpanel"
  - Agent panel: aria-label="Agent assistant"
  - Tab bar: role="tablist"
  - Each tab: role="tab", aria-selected, aria-label="{filename}"
- Live regions for notifications: aria-live="polite" on toast container

### axe-core integration
- Storybook a11y addon enabled
- axe-core automated scanning on all Storybook stories
- CI: @axe-core/playwright scan on workspace-playground homepage

### Skip-to-content link
- Hidden skip link at top of workspace: "Skip to editor"
- Visible on focus (Tab from address bar)

## Considerations
- shadcn components already AA-compliant (contrast, focus indicators, ARIA)
- react-arborist provides tree keyboard nav + ARIA semantics out of the box
- Manual screen reader testing (VoiceOver, NVDA) deferred to post-launch
- Color contrast: all shadcn CSS variables meet 4.5:1 ratio (verified in design system)

## v1 references
- No dedicated a11y in v1 — this is a new feature

## Unit tests (vitest + @testing-library/react)

### Keyboard navigation
1. Tab key moves focus through interactive elements in order
2. Shift+Tab moves focus backwards
3. Escape closes open dialog and returns focus to trigger element
4. Escape closes open dropdown menu

### Focus management
5. Clicking panel tab focuses the panel content
6. Panel close moves focus to adjacent panel
7. Focus ring visible on active panel (check CSS class or outline style)
8. Focus trap in Dialog: Tab cycles within dialog, doesn't escape

### ARIA attributes
9. Sidebar has aria-label="File explorer"
10. Editor panel has role="tabpanel"
11. Tab bar has role="tablist"
12. Each tab has role="tab" and aria-selected matches active state
13. Toast container has aria-live="polite"

### Skip link
14. Skip link is first focusable element in DOM
15. Skip link visible only on focus (check visibility/opacity)
16. Activating skip link moves focus to editor area

### axe-core
17. axe-core scan on WorkspaceProvider with default layout returns zero violations
18. axe-core scan on FileTree component returns zero violations
19. axe-core scan on CommandPalette returns zero violations

## E2E test specs (Playwright)
20. Tab through workspace: sidebar → editor → agent panel → back to sidebar
21. Open command palette with Cmd+P, close with Escape, focus returns to editor
22. axe-core full-page scan on workspace-playground: zero critical violations

## Success criteria
- Tab through all interactive elements
- Focus ring visible on active panel
- ARIA labels on major UI regions
- axe-core scan passes on all Storybook stories
- Skip-to-content link works
- All 22 tests pass
close_reason: shipped — added chat accessibility audit doc, landmark/live-region improvements, and Playwright axe CI gate; reviewed by claude: ship; commit f8c5fe5

title: M5: <ChatPanel toolRenderers={...}/> override
description:
**Goal:** Allow embedders to inject custom tool renderers from outside the package. File: `src/front/components/ChatPanel.tsx` (extended).

**Shape:**
```tsx
<ChatPanel sessionId="x" toolRenderers={{
  myTool: ({input, output, status}) => <CustomRenderer ... />,
}} />
```

**Merging:** embedder's overrides merge with defaults (embedder wins on same key).

**Tests:** embedder override renders instead of default; default fallback still kicks in for non-overridden tools.

## Acceptance Criteria

Embedder override renders instead of default. Fallback still works for non-overridden tools.
acceptance_criteria: Embedder override renders instead of default. Fallback still works for non-overridden tools.
close_reason: Exposed and validated ChatPanel toolRenderers override contract for embedders. ChatPanel now merges embedder-provided renderers with defaults before resolving tool renderers, so override keys win while non-overridden tools keep default/fallback behavior. Added focused tests proving: override replaces default renderer, non-overridden plugin tools still render via fallback, and renderer merge semantics preserve defaults. Cross-review (claude-latest -p) verdict: ship (no blocking findings). Scoped tests: pnpm --dir packages/agent exec vitest run src/front/__tests__/ChatPanel.test.tsx src/front/toolRenderers/__tests__/renderers.test.tsx (20 passed, type errors: none).

title: M2: Snapshot retention (keep-last-2)
description:
**Goal:** After a successful new snapshot, delete all but the 2 most recent snapshots for this workspace. File: additive to `periodicSnapshot.ts` (~15 LOC).

**Why:** Snapshots cost money. Keep-last-2 bounds storage while preserving one rollback option.

**Tests:** third snapshot deletes the oldest; retention doesn't delete if only 1-2 exist.

## Acceptance Criteria

After third snapshot, oldest deleted. Retention no-op if <3 snapshots exist.
acceptance_criteria: After third snapshot, oldest deleted. Retention no-op if <3 snapshots exist.
close_reason: Implemented keep-last snapshot retention in new vercel-sandbox periodicSnapshot module. applySnapshotRetention now enforces BORING_AGENT_SNAPSHOT_KEEP (default 2), deletes stale snapshots, and preserves failed deletes for retry visibility. Added targeted tests for third-snapshot oldest deletion, <3 no-op, env override, invalid env fallback, and delete-failure tracking. Cross-review (claude-latest -p) verdict: ship (no blocking findings). Scoped test: pnpm --dir packages/agent exec vitest run src/server/sandbox/vercel-sandbox/__tests__/periodicSnapshot.test.ts (5 passed, type errors: none).

title: M0: Interface — AgentHarness + RunContext + SendMessageInput
description:
**Goal:** Lock the Layer-1 harness contract. File: `src/shared/harness.ts`.

**Interface (EXACT shapes from spec §The four abstractions > Harness):**
```ts
export interface AgentHarness {
  readonly id: string                                  // 'pi-coding-agent' | 'pi-agent-core' | 'tool-loop'
  readonly placement: 'server' | 'browser'

  /** Send a user message. Yields AI SDK UIMessage stream chunks. */
  sendMessage(
    input: SendMessageInput,
    ctx: RunContext,
  ): AsyncIterable<UIMessageChunk>

  /** Session lifecycle; may delegate to an underlying runtime (e.g. pi's JSONL). */
  sessions: SessionStore
}

/* Resume is NOT a harness concern — see Stream resumption section.
   The HTTP route owns cursor buffering + replay; harness stays reconnect-unaware. */

export interface SendMessageInput {
  sessionId: string
  message: string                                      // user's prompt
  thinkingLevel?: 'off' | 'low' | 'medium' | 'high'
  model?: { provider: string; id: string }
}

export interface RunContext {
  abortSignal: AbortSignal                             // REQUIRED (not optional) — route MUST pass one
  workdir: string                                      // filesystem path where commands execute (NOT workspaceId)
  userId?: string                                      // unused in v1; workspace passes later
}
```

**Notes:**
- **`RunContext.workdir` is an absolute fs path** (e.g., `/vercel/sandbox` or `${workspaceRoot}/${workspaceId}`), not a workspace identifier. The harness uses it to pin the exec cwd.
- **`abortSignal` is required** — every route must pass an AbortController that cancels when the HTTP request aborts. This is the chain root for stop() propagation down to sandbox.exec.
- **`sessions: SessionStore`** stays on harness (pragmatic: pi-coding-agent embeds session logic; splitting forces adapter glue). Acknowledged Layer-1/2 smell — trade-off accepted.
- **`model` is structured** (`{provider, id}`), not a string. Allows future providers beyond Anthropic even though v1 ships Anthropic-only.
- **`thinkingLevel` has 4 states** (off/low/medium/high). Composer emits these; harness passes to pi.
- **No `reconnect?()`** — stream resume is transport-owned (route-level ring buffer), not harness-owned. Any future harness gets resume for free.

**Tests:** Type-level stub: `expectTypeOf<AgentHarness>().toMatchTypeOf<{...}>()`. Ensure `RunContext.abortSignal` is NOT marked optional; ensure `workdir` is a string.

## Acceptance Criteria

`src/shared/harness.ts` exports `AgentHarness`, `RunContext`, `SendMessageInput`. `RunContext.abortSignal` is REQUIRED. `RunContext.workdir` is a string fs path (NOT workspaceId). `sessions: SessionStore` field present. No `reconnect?()` method. `model` is `{provider, id}` structured type. `thinkingLevel` enum covers off/low/medium/high. Type-level stub passes with `expectTypeOf` assertions.
acceptance_criteria: `src/shared/harness.ts` exports `AgentHarness`, `RunContext`, `SendMessageInput`. Type-level stub test passes. No reconnect?() field.
close_reason: shipped — reviewed by GoldHill: ship

title: Infra: Invariant grep checks (no Node imports in shared)
description:
**Goal:** Prevent regressions on spec §Invariants to preserve. Script: `scripts/check-invariants.sh` + ESLint rules + CI wiring.

**The 4 invariants (verbatim from spec §Invariants to preserve):**
1. **No `node:fs` / `node:child_process` imports in routes, catalog, or tools.** These Node APIs are allowed only inside adapter implementations (`server/workspace/**`, `server/harness/**`, `server/sandbox/**`, `server/runtime/**`). Grep-enforceable.
2. **Routes and tools both receive `Workspace` as a parameter** (not a path, not a root dir). Resolution to a specific adapter is the job of `resolveMode` — centralized.
3. **Per-request, agent tools and HTTP routes share the same adapter instance.** Ensures a read concurrent with a write sees each other's effects without cache-coherence games.
4. **Path validation is the adapter's responsibility.** Consumers pass user-supplied paths; the adapter rejects `../` / absolute / symlink-escape attacks internally.

**Grep-enforced checks (fail CI on violation):**
- `grep -rE "from ['\"]node:" src/shared/` → must be empty.
- `grep -rE "\bBuffer\b" src/shared/` → must be empty (must use `Uint8Array`).
- `grep -rE "from ['\"]node:(fs|child_process)" src/server/http/routes/ src/server/catalog/` → must be empty.
- `grep -rE "from ['\"]@boring/agent/server" src/front/` → must be empty (frontend-server bleed).
- `grep -rE "console\.(log|debug|info|warn|error)" src/server/` → must be empty (use `createLogger(prefix)` from `infra.logging-standard`).
- `grep -rE "process\.env\." src/server/` excluding `src/server/config/` → must be empty (centralize env reads).

**ESLint rules (additional — spec §Test strategy):**

**`boring/no-hardcoded-colors` (custom rule in `eslint-plugin-boring/`):**
- Target: files matching `src/front/primitives/**`.
- Detect: `className` attributes containing Tailwind color utilities like `bg-slate-900`, `text-gray-500`, `border-red-400`, etc.
- Require: all color classes MUST use `bg-[var(--boring-chat-*)]`, `text-[var(--boring-chat-*)]`, etc. (the CSS-var bridge pattern from `m3b.theme-css`).
- Rationale: spec §Test strategy line 1444 — primitives must be re-themable via CSS vars alone; hard-coded colors break the styling contract.
- Auto-fix: NO (too risky; must be manual).

**`boring/stable-error-codes` (custom rule):**
- Target: all source files.
- Detect: `throw new Error({code: 'some_code'})` or `{error: {code: '...'}}` where `code` is a string literal.
- Require: import from `src/shared/error-codes.ts` enum — see `gov.error-codes`.

**Runtime-enforced invariants (assertions, not lint):**
- Every Workspace method validates the relPath via path helpers before any fs call.
- `RuntimeModeAdapter.create()` returns a bundle where `workspace.root === sandbox.cwd` (verified with an `assert` on adapter construction in dev mode).

**Tests (meta-tests of the linter itself):**
- Fixtures under `test-fixtures/invariants-bad/` (one per invariant) → `scripts/check-invariants.sh` must FAIL.
- Fixtures under `test-fixtures/invariants-good/` → must PASS.
- ESLint rule tests: valid/invalid input fixtures for each custom rule.

**Logging:** `[invariants]` prefix; on violation: print exact file:line + invariant name + fix hint. Example output:
```
[invariants] ERR src/front/primitives/Message.tsx:12: hard-coded color `bg-slate-900`
  Fix: use `bg-[var(--boring-chat-bg)]` (see STYLING.md)
```

**CI wiring:**
- `pnpm lint:invariants` — runs grep checks + ESLint custom rules.
- Required check on every PR. Merges blocked on violation.
- Nightly run over main to catch drift.

## Acceptance Criteria

All 4 spec invariants are enforced: 6 grep rules + 2 custom ESLint rules. Bad-import fixtures cause failures. Good fixtures pass. No hard-coded Tailwind colors in `src/front/primitives/`. No raw error code strings outside the enum. Every violation prints a fix hint. Required CI check blocks merges.
acceptance_criteria: All 4 invariants grep-enforced. Zero violations on main. PR check blocks merges with violations. Bad-import fixtures cause failure.
close_reason: shipped — reviewed by claude: ship

title: Gov: Canonical error-codes registry (stable codes for every failure)
description:
**Goal:** Single registry of stable error codes used in API responses + logs. File: `src/shared/error-codes.ts` + `docs/ERROR_CODES.md`.

**Why:** beads across the graph mention stable codes (`OIDC_REFRESH_FAILED`, `PATH_ESCAPE`, `BWRAP_TIMEOUT`, etc.) but without a registry, implementations drift — one route uses `path-escape`, another uses `path_escape`, a third uses `PATH_TRAVERSAL`. The frontend + logs + monitoring all need a stable taxonomy.

**Registry format (Zod enum + doc):**
```ts
// src/shared/error-codes.ts
export const ErrorCode = z.enum([
  // Auth / config
  'MISSING_API_KEY',
  'INVALID_API_KEY',
  'OIDC_REFRESH_FAILED',
  'VERCEL_AUTH_FAILED',
  'CONFIG_INVALID',

  // Workspace / path
  'PATH_ESCAPE',                 // attempt to access outside workspace root
  'PATH_ABSOLUTE',               // absolute path rejected
  'PATH_NULL_BYTE',              // null byte in path
  'PATH_SYMLINK_ESCAPE',         // realpath resolves outside root
  'PATH_NOT_FOUND',              // stat/read on missing path
  'PATH_NOT_WRITABLE',           // parent dir doesn't exist or permission denied
  'WORKSPACE_UNINITIALIZED',

  // Sandbox / exec
  'BWRAP_UNAVAILABLE',           // bwrap binary not on PATH
  'BWRAP_TIMEOUT',               // command exceeded timeoutMs
  'OUTPUT_TRUNCATED',            // maxOutputBytes hit (informational; not an error per se)
  'SANDBOX_NOT_READY',           // Vercel cold start; 503 + retryAfter
  'SANDBOX_EXPIRED',             // Vercel TTL elapsed
  'VERCEL_API_ERROR',            // generic Vercel SDK failure (wrapped by circuit breaker)
  'CIRCUIT_OPEN',                // circuit breaker open; fast-fail
  'ABORTED',                     // AbortSignal fired

  // Session / bridge
  'SESSION_NOT_FOUND',
  'SESSION_LOCKED',              // concurrent access (rare; documented)
  'STREAM_BUFFER_EVICTED',       // resume cursor > TTL eviction; fall back to replay from store
  'CURSOR_OUT_OF_RANGE',         // 416 on stream resume
  'BRIDGE_COMMAND_INVALID',      // bad kind/params

  // Tool
  'TOOL_NOT_FOUND',
  'TOOL_INVALID_INPUT',          // JSON Schema validation failed
  'TOOL_EXECUTION_ERROR',        // inner tool body threw

  // Plugin
  'PLUGIN_LOAD_FAILED',
  'PLUGIN_NAME_COLLISION',       // warning-level; not hard error

  // Internal
  'INTERNAL_ERROR',              // catch-all — logged with stack; never leaked to client
])
export type ErrorCode = z.infer<typeof ErrorCode>
```

**Response shape (all API errors):**
```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "PATH_ESCAPE",
    "message": "Path '../secrets' escapes workspace root",
    "details": { "path": "../secrets", "workspaceRoot": "/tmp/ws" }
  }
}
```

**Log format (structured):** logger emits `{level:'error', code:'PATH_ESCAPE', prefix:'[workspace]', msg, ...fields}`.

**Docs file (`docs/ERROR_CODES.md`):** one row per code:
- Code name
- When it fires
- HTTP status (if applicable)
- Suggested client action ("retry", "re-auth", "report-bug", "user-fix")
- Log level (warn / error)
- Stability (public API = stable; internal = may change)

**Lint enforcement:**
- Source files MUST import codes from `error-codes.ts` — grep rule in `infra.invariant-lint` rejects raw string literals like `throw new Error({code: 'some_code'})`.
- PRs that add a new code must update the Zod enum AND `docs/ERROR_CODES.md` in the same commit (CI check).

**Tests:**
- Zod enum round-trip: parse each code; assert all present.
- Docs parity: CI check that every enum value appears as a row in ERROR_CODES.md.
- Response shape: every route's 4xx/5xx response validates against the schema.

**Logging:** `[errors]` prefix for the lint/doc-parity check itself.

## Acceptance Criteria

`src/shared/error-codes.ts` + `docs/ERROR_CODES.md` exist. Zod enum covers every currently-known failure mode. All API error responses use this shape. Structured logs include `code` field. Lint blocks raw string error codes. Docs + enum stay in sync (CI check).
close_reason: shipped — reviewed by claude: ship

title: M1: Auth middleware + path validation + body parsing
acceptance_criteria: Missing auth rejects 401 when token set. Invalid path param rejects 400. Body schema errors return structured error with field+reason.

title: Phase 1: Project Scaffold
description:
## Goal
Set up the v2 workspace package scaffold: package.json, tsconfig, tailwind config, CSS entry point, and lib/utils.ts.

## Background
This is the very first bead — nothing else can start until the scaffold exists. The workspace is a frontend-only React library (no server code). It lives at v2/packages/workspace/.

## What to implement
1. **package.json** with deps:
   - react, react-dom (peer)
   - dockview-react (^4.13.1)
   - tailwindcss, class-variance-authority, clsx, tailwind-merge
   - @codemirror/* (peer or optional — loaded lazily)
   - @tiptap/* (peer or optional — loaded lazily)
   - zustand (state management)
   - react-arborist (file tree)
   - @tanstack/react-query (data fetching)
   - zod (validation)
   - TypeScript, vitest (dev)

2. **tsconfig.json** — strict mode, paths aliases (@/ → src/), jsx: react-jsx

3. **tailwind.config.ts** — shadcn preset, content paths for src/**/*.tsx

4. **postcss.config.js** — standard tailwind + autoprefixer

5. **src/globals.css** — @tailwind base/components/utilities + shadcn CSS variables (light + dark)

6. **src/lib/utils.ts** — cn() helper (clsx + twMerge), standard shadcn utility

7. **src/index.ts** — empty public API barrel file (populated incrementally by later beads)

## Considerations
- React 19 safe — dockview 4.13.1 supports it
- Language: TypeScript .tsx from day 1 (v1 was 100% .jsx, v2 is clean-slate)
- pnpm workspace member — add to root pnpm-workspace.yaml
- Vite config for library mode build (packages are libraries, not apps)

## Success criteria
- `pnpm install` succeeds in the workspace
- `tsc --noEmit` passes with zero errors
- Tailwind classes resolve (test with a dummy component)
- cn() utility works (import and verify)
close_reason: shipped — cross-reviewed by Codex (gpt-5.4): ship after 2 revisions

title: M0: Locked Decisions Registry — all 23 decisions with rationale (reference doc)
description:
**Goal:** Single source-of-truth reference doc capturing all 23 locked decisions from spec §Key decisions. File: `docs/DECISIONS.md` (committed at M0).

**Why a bead:** the skill says "everything from the plan must be embedded in beads." Decisions are the architecture's load-bearing assumptions — shipping them as a standalone doc means future contributors don't need to hunt through the spec.

**Contents (each decision has: What + Why + Rationale + Re-evaluate-when):**

1. **Standalone shape:** CLI first (`npx @boring/agent`); same product shape as Claude Code. Re-evaluate when: library usage grows > CLI usage.
2. **Chat UI:** Vercel ai-elements (copied) + `@ai-sdk/react useChat`. Re-evaluate when: ai-elements API stabilizes or forks.
3. **Wire protocol:** AI SDK UIMessage stream end-to-end. Re-evaluate when: AI SDK introduces breaking changes.
4. **v1 harness:** `@mariozechner/pi-coding-agent`. Re-evaluate when: pi stability regresses OR ai-sdk harness needs arrive (boring-macro migration).
5. **Harness interface:** Generic, `placement: server | browser`. Future browser-agent is a sibling adapter, not a migration.
6. **Catalog:** 4 base (bash, read, write, edit) + conditional `execute_isolated_code` (sandbox capability-gated). Minimal baseline; richer sandboxes auto-lift.
7a. **Workspace (local):** `NodeWorkspace` + ported `validatePath`/`assertRealPathWithinWorkspace`.
7b. **Workspace (remote):** `VercelSandboxWorkspace` delegates to `sandbox.fs.*` + `sandbox.writeFiles`.
7c. **Sandbox (local):** `BwrapSandbox` (`capabilities: ['exec']`) pairs with NodeWorkspace.
7d. **Sandbox (remote):** `VercelSandboxExec` (`capabilities: ['exec']`) pairs with VercelSandboxWorkspace.
7e. **Pairing invariant:** Workspace + Sandbox MUST target the same execution context. Enforced at adapter construction; no mixed pairings.
7f. **Mode selection:** `[runtime].mode = "direct" | "local" | "vercel-sandbox"` in boring.app.toml. Env override via `BORING_AGENT_MODE`.
8. **Plugins:** Coexist via pi extensions in direct/local only. Remote mode skips extension load (plugins are Node-native).
9. **Sessions:** `SessionStore` + `PiSessionStore` (JSONL) in v1. Platform-agnostic interface; future SQLite/IndexedDB impls.
10. **API key:** `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` env only. `VERCEL_OIDC_TOKEN` in remote mode. 12-factor.
11. **Workspace scope:** Single workspace per instance. workspaceId from config. No runtime CRUD; belongs to `@boring/cloud`.
11b. **Session surface:** Lightweight `<SessionToolbar />` + `useSessions()`. List/create/switch/delete in v1; rename deferred.
11c. **Standalone app:** First-class CLI (`bin/boring-agent`). Same code as `pnpm dev`.
11d. **Model + thinking UI:** Inline in `<Composer />` — per-message concerns.
11e. **Dev-friendly direct mode:** Third mode. `child_process.exec` + `cwd`. Documented NO-sandbox posture.
11f. **Settings:** env vars only. No `/api/settings`. No runtime prefs file.
11g. **AI SDK harness:** NOT in v1. Design seam exists. boring-macro migration = rewrite pi-coding-agent.
12. **Backend stack:** Fastify + Node ESM.
13. **Styling:** CSS custom properties (`--boring-chat-*`) + render-prop escape hatches.
14. **UI export pattern:** Default component + primitives + headless hook — for every user-facing piece.
15. **Export surface:** Locked names: `ChatPanel`, `SessionToolbar`, `Message`, `MessageGroup`, `Composer`, `ModelPicker`, `ThinkingToggle`, `Tool`, `Terminal`, `CodeBlock`, `Reasoning`, `NewChatButton`, `useAgentChat`, `useSessions`, `theme.css`.
16. **Import convention:** `@boring/agent` (top-level, browser-safe) + `@boring/agent/server` (Node-only) + `@boring/agent/shared` (type-only).

**Format in docs/DECISIONS.md:** one row per decision with the 4 fields. Links to relevant beads where the decision is implemented.

**Process:** any PR that changes a locked decision → must update DECISIONS.md + bead(s) AND include rationale in the PR description. Reviewer checks DECISIONS.md for drift.

## Acceptance Criteria

`docs/DECISIONS.md` exists, covers all 23 decisions with What/Why/Rationale/Re-evaluate-when format. Linked from README. CONTRIBUTING.md references it as the source of truth for architectural questions. Any decision changes require this doc + bead updates as part of PR.

title: M2: createVercelSandboxWorkspace adapter
description:
**Goal:** `Workspace` adapter backed by Vercel Sandbox's file APIs. File: `src/server/workspace/createVercelSandboxWorkspace.ts` (~80 LOC).

**Shape:**
```ts
export function createVercelSandboxWorkspace(sandbox: VercelSandbox): Workspace {
  return {
    root: '/vercel/sandbox',
    async readFile(rel) { const p = assertSafe(rel); return sandbox.fs.readFile(p, 'utf-8') },
    async writeFile(rel, data) { ... sandbox.writeFiles([{path, content}]) },
    async unlink(rel) { ... sandbox.fs.rm(p) },
    async readdir(rel) { ... sandbox.fs.readdir(p) → Entry[] },
    async stat(rel) { ... sandbox.fs.stat(p) → Stat },
    async mkdir(rel, opts) { ... sandbox.fs.mkdir(p, opts) },
    async rename(from, to) { ... sandbox.fs.rename(absFrom, absTo) },
  }
}
```

**Invariants:**
- Accepts a `VercelSandbox` handle — NOT a Vercel client — so it shares the handle with `VercelSandboxExec`. Enforces pairing invariant.
- Path validation via same `assertSafe` helper (shared with NodeWorkspace; no relative paths leaking to VM).
- Returns `Uint8Array` at the boundary; `readFile` decodes UTF-8 for agent consumption.

**Tests:** conformance suite (same as NodeWorkspace). Verifies shared-handle with exec sees same files.

## Acceptance Criteria

Passes shared Workspace conformance suite against a mocked Vercel sandbox. Writes via workspace visible to paired exec on same handle.
acceptance_criteria: Passes shared Workspace conformance suite against a mocked Vercel sandbox. Writes via workspace visible to paired exec on same handle.
close_reason: shipped — reviewed by claude-latest: ship; added @vercel/sandbox workspace adapter + mocked conformance/shared-handle tests; lint+test green
