title: Coord: Lock @boring/workspace integration contract
description:
**Goal:** Ensure the contracts the workspace package expects are delivered AND vice versa. Tracks cross-plan alignment. **Integration pattern: app-shell composition, NOT direct workspace imports.**

**Integration pattern (reconciled 2026-04-23):**
- Workspace package has **ZERO imports** from `@boring/agent`.
- App shell (final application) imports components from BOTH `@boring/agent` AND `@boring/workspace`, then wires them via `WorkspaceProvider`'s `panels` prop.
- Avoids circular-dep risk + keeps workspace tree-shakable (chat is opt-in).
- Bridge types (`UiCommand`, `CommandResult`, etc.) live in `@boring/core` — agent imports from core, workspace implements from core. Neither package imports from the other.

**Contracts this package owes to workspace (agent APIs workspace consumes):**

1. **HTTP routes** — agent hosts these, workspace frontend calls them:
   - Files: `/api/v1/files` (GET/POST/DELETE), `/api/v1/files/move` (POST — handles drag + rename), `/api/v1/dirs` (POST — mkdir), `/api/v1/tree` (GET), `/api/v1/stat` (GET), `/api/v1/files/search` (GET)
   - Agent: `/api/v1/agent/chat` (POST stream), `/api/v1/agent/chat/:id/:turn` (SSE resume), `/api/v1/agent/sessions` (CRUD minus rename), `/api/v1/agent/sessions/:id/changes` (GET)
   - UI bridge: `/api/v1/ui/state` (GET/PUT), `/api/v1/ui/state/latest` (GET), `/api/v1/ui/commands` (POST), `/api/v1/ui/commands/next` (SSE or `?poll=true`)
   - Health: `/health` (liveness), `/ready` (readiness, 503 during cold start)
   - All routes use error codes from `gov.error-codes`.

2. **Component exports — for APP SHELL to pass into workspace** (NOT for workspace to import):
   - `ChatPanel` — app passes via `WorkspaceProvider panels={[{id:'agent', component: ChatPanel, …}]}`.
   - `SessionToolbar`, `Composer`, `Message`, `NewChatButton` — workspace doesn't need these; app shell composes custom chat shells if desired.

3. **Hooks — for TIER-3 headless consumers** (rare — app shells building custom session UI):
   - `useAgentChat()` — Tier-3 custom chat shells.
   - `useSessions()` — custom session sidebars beyond our `SessionToolbar`.
   - Workspace v1 package itself does NOT use these hooks.

4. **Styling:** `theme.css` + CSS custom properties (`--boring-chat-*`). Workspace can override at any scope — per-panel via `data-boring-chat` attribute.

5. **UiBridge semantics:** command-first dispatch (single source = `UiBridge.postCommand`), state-sourced-from-workspace (workspace PUTs state after applying), same `seq` in SSE + display-only `data-ui-command` parts.

6. **CommandResult shape:** `{seq: number, status: 'ok'|'error', error?: {code, message}}`. Matches workspace plan's bridge contract exactly.

7. **Ready-status SSE** (for cold-start UX — see `risk.vercel-cold-start-ux`): agent emits on `/ready` or via a dedicated `/api/v1/ready-status` SSE; frontend shows "preparing workspace…" card until ready.

**Contracts workspace owes to us:**

1. `PUT /api/v1/ui/state` with `causedBy: 'user'|'agent'|'restore'` field (prevents echo loops).
2. Subscribe to `/api/v1/ui/commands/next` (SSE default, `?poll=true` fallback for restricted environments).
3. Consume `data-ui-command` parts as **display-only** — never dispatch from these; SSE is the dispatch channel.
4. Pass `panels` config including `{id:'agent', component: ChatPanel}` when chat is desired.
5. Implement the `UiCommand` kinds workspace cares about (`openFile`, `openPanel`, etc.) + extend enum for workspace-specific kinds.

**Verification E2E test (deliverable of this bead):**
- Playwright test that boots BOTH packages in an app-shell scenario.
- App imports `ChatPanel` from agent + passes to `WorkspaceProvider`.
- Test: agent calls `exec_ui({kind:'openFile'})` → SSE delivers → workspace Zustand store opens file → IdeLayout's Editor panel renders it.
- Test: workspace `PUT /api/v1/ui/state` with `causedBy:'user'` → agent tool `get_ui_state` returns that state.
- Test: workspace doesn't import `@boring/agent` at runtime (verify via bundle analyzer — workspace dist has no agent exports).

**Cross-plan consistency check (one-shot script `scripts/check-plan-sync.sh`):**
- Every route in agent's HTTP API table appears in workspace plan's usage list (no orphan routes).
- Every workspace-expected route appears in agent's table (no phantom references).
- CommandResult shape identical across both plans.

**Gate:** `docs/WORKSPACE_CONTRACT.md` exists with full contract table + app-shell wiring example. E2E test passes. `scripts/check-plan-sync.sh` passes in CI. Workspace package's bundle analyzer confirms zero agent imports.

## Acceptance Criteria

`docs/WORKSPACE_CONTRACT.md` has full contract table + app-shell composition example. E2E test proves agent↔workspace integration via app-level injection (not workspace import). `scripts/check-plan-sync.sh` validates both plans stay aligned. Workspace package bundle has zero agent imports (bundle analyzer check). Workspace plan (lines 113, 649) + agent plan (Component exports section) both describe the same app-shell-composition pattern.

title: Phase 1: Vendor shadcn UI Components
description:
## Goal
Vendor the 17 shadcn components needed by workspace phases 1-4 into src/components/ui/.

## Background
shadcn components are copy-pasted (vendored), not installed as npm packages. This gives full control over styling and tree-shaking. Workspace vendors its own set; apps can also use @boring/core/ui for shared components (Avatar, Switch, Textarea).

## Components to vendor (17 total across all phases)
### Phase 1 — 10 components (vendor now):
- Button, Tabs, Tooltip, DropdownMenu, Sheet, ScrollArea, Input, Badge, Separator, Dialog

### Phase 2 — 3 components (vendor now, used by panes):
- Card, Checkbox, AlertDialog

### Phase 4 — 4 components (vendor now, used by command palette + polish):
- Command/CommandDialog, Popover, Select, Label

## What to implement
1. Run shadcn CLI or manually copy each component into src/components/ui/
2. Each component is a .tsx file using tailwind classes only
3. All components use the cn() utility from lib/utils.ts
4. Ensure all components respect shadcn CSS variables (--primary, --background, --border, etc.)
5. No custom CSS classes — tailwind only
6. Create barrel export: src/components/ui/index.ts exporting all 17 components

## Considerations
- Do NOT re-vendor components available from @boring/core/ui (Avatar, Switch, Textarea)
- All 17 components are needed across phases — vendor them all now to avoid repeated setup
- shadcn components are already WCAG AA-compliant (accessibility built-in)
- Tree-shakeable by default (each is a separate file)
- ResizablePanel from shadcn is NOT included — dockview handles all panel resizing natively
- The plan's Phase 1.2 text mentions ResizablePanel but Section F (authoritative inventory) excludes it

## Unit tests (vitest)
1. Each component renders without crashing (smoke test per component)
2. Button: renders variants (default, destructive, outline, secondary, ghost, link), sizes (default, sm, lg, icon), disabled state
3. Dialog: opens on trigger, closes on overlay click, closes on Escape, renders title/description, traps focus
4. AlertDialog: renders action/cancel buttons, calls onAction/onCancel callbacks, blocks background interaction
5. DropdownMenu: opens on trigger, renders items, calls onSelect, supports sub-menus, closes on outside click
6. Tabs: switches content on tab click, respects defaultValue, calls onValueChange, keyboard navigation (arrow keys)
7. Command/CommandDialog: filters items on type, selects with Enter, groups render correctly, empty state shown
8. Sheet: opens from correct side (top/right/bottom/left), overlay dismissal, focus trap, ARIA attributes
9. Select: opens dropdown, selects item, shows placeholder, disabled state, keyboard navigation
10. Input: renders value, calls onChange, placeholder shown, disabled/readonly states
11. ScrollArea: renders scrollbar when content overflows, no scrollbar when content fits
12. Tooltip: shows on hover after delay, hides on mouse leave, accessible via aria-describedby
13. Badge: renders variants (default, secondary, destructive, outline)
14. Popover: opens on trigger, positions correctly, closes on outside click
15. Checkbox: toggles checked state, indeterminate state, disabled state, calls onCheckedChange
16. Card: renders header, content, footer slots
17. Separator: renders horizontal/vertical orientation
18. Label: renders with htmlFor association

## Success criteria
- All 17 components importable from src/components/ui/
- Each component renders correctly in light and dark themes
- No external CSS dependencies (tailwind only)
- tsc --noEmit passes
- All unit tests pass
close_reason: done

title: Migration: apps/chat → v2
description:
**Goal:** Port apps/chat to v2 workspace + agent packages. This is the simplest full-stack migration because chat already uses ChatCenteredWorkspace (v1) which maps directly to ChatLayout (v2).

## Current v1 structure (apps/chat/src/front/App.jsx)
- QueryClientProvider + DataContext.Provider wrapping
- createHttpProvider() for file operations
- useChatControllerAdapter() from @boring/agent — provides controller, agentMode, thinkingLevel, model selection
- ChatCenteredWorkspace from workspace layouts/chat/
- Props: controller, agentMode, thinkingLevel, setThinkingLevel, selectedModel, setModel, availableModels, renderMessage, onSaveApiKey

## Step-by-step migration checklist

1. **Dependency swap** — update apps/chat/package.json:
   - Remove: direct imports from ../../../../packages/workspace/..., ../../../../packages/agent/...
   - Add: @boring/workspace, @boring/agent as workspace deps

2. **App.jsx rewrite** — replace entire file:
   ```tsx
   // v2 — apps/chat/src/front/App.tsx
   import { WorkspaceProvider, ChatLayout } from "@boring/workspace"
   import { ChatPanel } from "@boring/agent"

   export default function App() {
     return (
       <WorkspaceProvider
         layout="chat"
         panels={[{ id: "chat", component: ChatPanel }]}
         apiBaseUrl="/api/v1"
       >
         <ChatLayout />
       </WorkspaceProvider>
     )
   }
   ```

3. **Remove boilerplate** — v2 WorkspaceProvider handles:
   - QueryClientProvider (internal)
   - DataProvider (internal, configured via apiBaseUrl)
   - ThemeProvider (internal)
   - The v1 app manually assembled 3 providers — v2 needs 1

4. **Chat controller** — useChatControllerAdapter() moves into ChatPanel (agent package owns this). App shell no longer handles controller, model selection, or thinking level.

5. **File extension** — rename .jsx → .tsx (v2 is TypeScript)

6. **Vite config** — update to use v2 package aliases (pnpm workspace:* resolves automatically)

7. **Test:** `pnpm --filter apps/chat dev` → chat loads, can send messages, artifacts open in surface, session switching works

## Acceptance criteria
- apps/chat boots with v2 packages
- Chat messages flow correctly
- Artifacts open in surface panel
- Session list renders and switches
- Theme toggle works
- App.tsx is <20 lines (all complexity in WorkspaceProvider + ChatLayout)
- No direct ../../../../ package imports remain

## Estimated effort: ~0.5 day
notes: Deferred by operator on 2026-04-24; migration beads paused for now.

title: M3a: E2E — session CRUD + resume + bridge reliability
description:
**Goal:** M3a gate test. Exercises the load-bearing contracts.

**Scenarios (Playwright + mock HTTP client):**
1. Create session → list → load → delete. Assert CRUD via JSON + reload shows only remaining sessions.
2. Start chat → disconnect after 2s → reconnect with `experimental_resume: true` → receives remaining chunks. Total output matches single-connection baseline.
3. Agent calls `exec_ui({kind:'openFile', params:{path:'foo.md'}})` → workspace SSE subscriber receives command with matching `seq` → workspace state updated. Assert state.openFiles includes 'foo.md'.
4. Workspace `PUT /api/v1/ui/state` → agent tool `get_ui_state` returns that state.
5. Bridge short-poll fallback: subscribe via `?poll=true`, post commands from another client, poll returns batch.

**Logging:** `[m3a-e2e]` tag. Log seq numbers, reconnect cursors, state transitions for CI debuggability.

**Done when:** all 5 scenarios pass in both `direct` and `local` modes.

## Acceptance Criteria

All 5 scenarios pass in both direct and local modes (session CRUD, disconnect+resume, bridge dispatch, state roundtrip, poll fallback).
acceptance_criteria: All 5 scenarios pass in both direct and local modes (session CRUD, disconnect+resume, bridge dispatch, state roundtrip, poll fallback).

title: Research: Vercel Sandbox latency on tight fs loops
description:
**Question:** How slow is Vercel Sandbox for agent patterns like "list 100 files" or "grep across 50 files"? Each `exec` has per-call overhead.

**Investigation:**
1. Benchmark: 100-file tree listing via `sandbox.exec('find . -type f')`.
2. Benchmark: 50-file grep via `sandbox.exec('grep -r pattern .')`.
3. Compare vs local bwrap same commands.

**If painful (>10s for common operations):**
- Add sandbox-side `grep` tool that batches reads.
- Cache directory structure in-process.
- Introduce a `find_files` tool over the FileSearch abstraction instead of `bash find`.

**Acceptance:** benchmark numbers documented; mitigations beaded if >2x slower than local.

## Acceptance Criteria

Benchmark numbers in docs/PERFORMANCE.md. Mitigations beaded if >2x slower than local.
close_reason: shipped — reviewed by claude: ship; documented p50/p95/p99 in docs/PERFORMANCE.md and opened mitigations boring-ui-v2-jru.1/.2/.3 for >2x slowdowns

title: Phase 4: Error Handling + Recovery
description:
## Goal
2-tier error recovery (pane boundary + full reset), network error handling, corrupted state handling.

## What to implement

### Network errors
- HTTP fetch wrapper: auto-retry 3x with exponential backoff for 5xx / network errors
- 401/403: fire onAuthError callback (passed via WorkspaceProvider props)
- Timeout: 10s default, configurable per route, toast on timeout
- Offline detection: navigator.onLine + fetch probe, show banner, disable writes

### Panel crashes (Tier 1)
- React error boundary per pane (src/components/PanelErrorBoundary.tsx)
- Crashed pane shows error + retry button, doesn't kill workspace
- Error logged with panel ID and stack trace
- Bridge notified: bridge.emit('pane:error', { panelId, error })

### Corrupted state (Tier 2)
- Zod validate layout JSON on rehydration
- Invalid layout (missing fields, negative dimensions): reset to defaults + toast
- Corrupted panel references (non-existent component): strip unknown panels, keep valid
- Reset button in UI: user can force-reset layout from workspace menu

### Recovery hierarchy (2-tier)
1. Retry (panel error boundary — per-pane)
2. Full reset (corrupted layout → default layout, lose customization)

### Bridge disconnect handling
- Bridge exposes onDisconnect / onReconnect callbacks for app-level handling
- On SSE disconnect: workspace shows subtle "reconnecting..." indicator
- On reconnect: workspace PUTs full state to re-sync (reconciliation per Section D)
- Correlated failure handling (e.g., bridge disconnect + dirty file + network error simultaneously) is DEFERRED to post-launch — v2 handles each failure independently, which is sufficient for typical single-failure scenarios

## v1 references
- PanelErrorBoundary.jsx (58 lines) — port and style with shadcn

## Unit tests — PanelErrorBoundary (vitest + @testing-library/react)

### Basic error catching
1. Renders children normally when no error thrown
2. Catches render error in child component, shows error UI
3. Error UI shows "Something went wrong" message with panel ID
4. Error UI shows "Retry" button
5. Clicking "Retry" resets error boundary and re-renders children
6. If child throws again after retry, shows error UI again (no infinite loop)
7. Error boundary catches errors in nested children (not just direct children)
8. Multiple error boundaries are independent — one crashing doesn't affect siblings

### Error reporting
9. bridge.emit('pane:error', { panelId, error }) called when error caught
10. Error includes component stack trace
11. console.error called with panel ID and stack

### Scoping
12. Error in pane A does NOT crash pane B (sibling isolation)
13. Error in pane does NOT crash workspace shell
14. Error boundary does NOT catch event handler errors (React limitation — document this)

## Unit tests — Network error handling (vitest)

### Retry logic
15. 5xx response triggers automatic retry (up to 3 attempts)
16. Exponential backoff: 1s, 2s, 4s between retries
17. Successful retry returns data (no error surfaced)
18. All 3 retries fail → error propagated to caller
19. 4xx responses are NOT retried (client errors are permanent)
20. Network errors (fetch throws TypeError) trigger retry
21. AbortController / timeout (10s default) triggers retry

### Auth errors
22. 401 response fires onAuthError callback
23. 403 response fires onAuthError callback
24. onAuthError receives the HTTP response for inspection
25. No retry attempted for 401/403

### Timeout
26. Request exceeding 10s default is aborted
27. Custom timeout per route (e.g., file upload: 30s) is respected
28. Timeout shows toast notification with route context

### Offline handling
29. navigator.onLine=false shows offline banner
30. Write operations (POST, PUT, DELETE) are disabled when offline
31. Read operations continue to attempt (may use stale cache)
32. When navigator.onLine transitions to true, banner dismissed
33. Fetch probe confirms actual connectivity (navigator.onLine can lie)

## Unit tests — Corrupted state recovery (vitest)

### Zod validation on rehydration
34. Valid layout JSON passes validation, hydration succeeds
35. Missing required fields (e.g., no groups array) → reset to defaults
36. Negative dimension values → reset to defaults
37. Extra/unknown fields are stripped (Zod passthrough not used)
38. Corrupted JSON string (not parseable) → reset to defaults

### Panel reference validation
39. Panel referencing non-existent component ID → strip that panel, keep valid ones
40. All panels referencing non-existent components → full reset to defaults
41. Valid panels mixed with invalid → only invalid stripped

### Reset button
42. Reset button visible in workspace menu (UserMenu or similar)
43. Clicking reset clears persisted layout from localStorage
44. After reset, workspace re-renders with default layout
45. Toast notification confirms reset happened

## E2E test specs (Playwright)
46. Inject JS error into pane → pane shows error boundary, rest of workspace works
47. Click "Retry" in error boundary → pane recovers
48. Corrupt localStorage layout data → reload → workspace loads with defaults + toast
49. Simulate network offline → banner appears, write buttons disabled → go online → banner dismissed

## Success criteria
- Panel crash doesn't kill workspace
- 401 fires onAuthError callback
- Corrupted localStorage handled gracefully (reset to defaults)
- Network errors show appropriate feedback (toast, banner)
- Reset button works
- Bridge disconnect/reconnect shows indicator and re-syncs
- All 49 tests pass

title: M1: RuntimeModeAdapter + resolveMode factory
description:
**Goal:** Each execution mode (`direct` | `local` | `vercel-sandbox`) is a self-contained module producing a `RuntimeBundle`. File: `src/server/runtime/modes/{direct,local,vercel-sandbox}.ts` + `src/server/runtime/resolveMode.ts`.

**Contract:**
```ts
export interface RuntimeModeAdapter {
  readonly id: 'direct' | 'local' | 'vercel-sandbox'
  create(ctx: ModeContext): Promise<RuntimeBundle>
}
export interface RuntimeBundle {
  workspace: Workspace
  sandbox: Sandbox
  fileSearch: FileSearch
  uiBridge?: UiBridge                  // pass-through from app-level
}
export interface ModeContext {
  workspaceRoot: string
  sessionId: string
  uiBridge?: UiBridge
}
```

**v1 in-scope modes (this task):** `direct` + `local` only. `vercel-sandbox` lands in M2.

**Auto-detection:**
```ts
function autoDetectMode(): 'direct' | 'local' | 'vercel-sandbox' {
  if (process.env.BORING_AGENT_MODE) return process.env.BORING_AGENT_MODE as any
  if (process.platform === 'linux' && hasBwrap()) return 'local'
  return 'direct'                      // cross-platform fallback
}
```

**Pairing invariant:** each mode owns BOTH workspace and sandbox construction → impossible to build a mismatched pair. Shared fs substrate guaranteed by construction.

**Tests:**
- `resolveMode('direct')` returns a bundle where `workspace` is a NodeWorkspace and `sandbox` is a DirectSandbox.
- `resolveMode('local')` returns NodeWorkspace + BwrapSandbox. Gracefully errors on non-Linux.
- Writing via `bundle.workspace.writeFile` is visible via `bundle.sandbox.exec('cat …')` — proves shared substrate.

## Acceptance Criteria

resolveMode('direct') and resolveMode('local') produce RuntimeBundles where workspace.writeFile + sandbox.exec('cat') see same content. Auto-detection: Linux+bwrap→local, else→direct.
acceptance_criteria: resolveMode('direct') and resolveMode('local') produce RuntimeBundles where workspace.writeFile + sandbox.exec('cat') see same content. Auto-detection: Linux+bwrap→local, else→direct.
close_reason: shipped — reviewed by claude: ship; commit 8ed653e; targeted tests: pnpm exec vitest run src/server/runtime/__tests__/resolveMode.test.ts src/server/sandbox/bwrap/__tests__/createBwrapSandbox.test.ts src/server/sandbox/direct/__tests__/createDirectSandbox.test.ts

title: Phase 1: Layout Presets (IdeLayout + ChatLayout)
description:
## Goal Two thin preset layouts (~30 lines each) that build a LayoutConfig and pass it to DockviewShell. ## What to implement ### IdeLayout (src/layouts/IdeLayout.tsx) - Props: { sidebar?: string, center?: string, right?: string } — all panel IDs, overridable - Defaults: { sidebar: 'filetree', center: 'empty', right: undefined } - Builds LayoutConfig: - sidebar group: left, locked, collapsible (collapsedWidth: 0), constraints 200-400px - center group: center, dynamic, placeholder 'empty' - right group: optional, hideHeader, constraints minWidth 300px - Returns Full pseudo-code: ```typescript // layouts/IdeLayout.tsx — ~30 lines export function IdeLayout({ sidebar = 'filetree', center = 'empty', right }: IdeLayoutProps) { return } ``` ### ChatLayout (src/layouts/ChatLayout.tsx) - Props: { nav?: string, center?: string, surface?: string, sidebar?: string } - Defaults: { nav: 'session-list', center: 'chat', surface: undefined } - Builds LayoutConfig: - nav group: left, locked, hideHeader, fixed 60px width (minWidth=60, maxWidth=60) - center group: center - sidebar group: optional, left, collapsible (collapsedWidth: 0), constraints 200-350px - surface group: optional, right, dynamic, placeholder 'empty' - Returns - Note: surface group is where ArtifactSurfacePane renders a nested DockviewShell - ChatLayout supports an optional file tree sidebar (sidebar='filetree') Full pseudo-code: ```typescript // layouts/ChatLayout.tsx — ~30 lines export function ChatLayout({ nav = 'session-list', center = 'chat', surface, sidebar }: ChatLayoutProps) { return } ``` ### Layout types (src/layouts/types.ts) ```typescript interface IdeLayoutProps { sidebar?: string // panel ID for left sidebar (default: 'filetree') center?: string // panel ID for center area (default: 'empty') right?: string // panel ID for right rail (optional) } interface ChatLayoutProps { nav?: string // panel ID for nav rail (default: 'session-list') center?: string // panel ID for center area (default: 'chat') surface?: string // panel ID for artifact surface (optional) sidebar?: string // panel ID for optional sidebar (e.g., 'filetree') } ``` ### NavRail props contract (for ChatLayout) - { sessions, activeSessionId, onSwitch, onCreate, onNewChat, onToggleSurface, surfaceOpen } - App shell passes user menu separately (not workspace's concern) ### Shared hooks extraction (Risk 10 mitigation) Both presets use DockviewShell which handles all heavy logic internally: | Shared concern | Location | Used by | |----------------|----------|---------| | useSidebarLayout() | DockviewShell (wireCollapsible) | IdeLayout, ChatLayout | | usePanelSizing() | DockviewShell (constraint enforcement) | IdeLayout, ChatLayout | | usePanelConstraints() | DockviewShell (group constraints) | IdeLayout, ChatLayout | v2 architecture: DockviewShell handles groups, constraints, placeholders, collapse internally. Presets are ~30 lines each building LayoutConfig. No duplicated sidebar/sizing code. ## v1 references - IdeLayout.jsx (1718 lines) — study group arrangement, simplify to ~30 lines - ChatCenteredWorkspace.jsx (434 lines) — nav rail + chat + surface arrangement - SurfaceShell.jsx (544 lines) — artifact surface dockview (nested) - useDockLayout.js (337 lines) — sidebar discovery, collapse toggle ## Tier 1/2/3 composability examples ```tsx // TIER 1: Preset layout with slot overrides // TIER 2: Custom layout via DockviewShell // TIER 3: Standalone components (no dockview, no WorkspaceProvider, no context) import { FileTree, CodeEditor } from '@boring/workspace'
``` ## Considerations - Both are THIN — they only build a LayoutConfig. All heavy logic is in DockviewShell. - Apps that need custom layouts skip presets and use DockviewShell directly - Presets are not special — just convenience functions - Layout config includes version: '2.0' for persistence version tracking ## Unit tests (layouts/presets.test.ts) ### IdeLayout - Test: IdeLayout renders with default props (sidebar=filetree, center=empty) - Test: IdeLayout accepts sidebar override (custom panel ID) - Test: IdeLayout with right prop adds right rail group - Test: IdeLayout without right prop has no right rail group - Test: sidebar group is locked and collapsible - Test: center group is dynamic with placeholder 'empty' ### ChatLayout - Test: ChatLayout renders with default props (nav=session-list, center=chat) - Test: ChatLayout with surface prop adds surface group (dynamic, placeholder) - Test: ChatLayout with sidebar prop adds collapsible sidebar - Test: ChatLayout without surface or sidebar → just nav + center - Test: nav group has fixed 60px width (minWidth=maxWidth=60) - Test: nav group is locked with hidden header ### Both presets - Test: preset output is a valid LayoutConfig object - Test: all panel IDs in LayoutConfig exist in registry (integration with Risk 11 validation) - Test: presets are <50 lines each (lint check) ## Success criteria - IdeLayout renders sidebar + center + optional right rail - ChatLayout renders nav + center + optional sidebar + optional surface - All slot overrides work (custom panel IDs) - Presets are <50 lines each (thin wrappers) - Can be skipped entirely in favor of direct DockviewShell usage

title: Migration: apps/minimal → v2
description:
**Goal:** Port apps/minimal to v2 workspace package. Minimal is the simplest app — just FileTree + Editor with no agent, no chat, no auth.

## Current v1 structure (apps/minimal/src/front/App.jsx)
- QueryClientProvider + DataContext.Provider wrapping
- createHttpProvider() for file operations
- Manual flexbox layout: sidebar (240px FileTree) + main (Editor)
- useState for selectedFile
- Components: FileTree (onSelectFile), Editor (path)
- No agent, no chat, no auth, no capabilities, no dockview

## Step-by-step migration checklist

1. **Dependency swap** — update apps/minimal/package.json:
   - Remove: direct ../../../../packages/ imports
   - Add: @boring/workspace as workspace dep

2. **App.jsx rewrite** — replace entire file:
   ```tsx
   // v2 — apps/minimal/src/front/App.tsx
   import { WorkspaceProvider, IdeLayout } from "@boring/workspace"

   export default function App() {
     return (
       <WorkspaceProvider
         layout="ide"
         apiBaseUrl="/api/v1"
         capabilities={["workspace.files"]}
       >
         <IdeLayout />
       </WorkspaceProvider>
     )
   }
   ```

3. **Layout upgrade** — v1 used manual CSS flexbox. v2 uses IdeLayout with dockview (resizable panels, tab management, layout persistence). This is a UX upgrade, not just a migration.

4. **Remove manual state** — v1 managed selectedFile via useState. v2 handles this via Zustand store + panel lifecycle. No app-level state needed.

5. **File extension** — rename .jsx → .tsx

6. **No agent panel** — IdeLayout without agent capabilities shows FileTree + Editor only (agent panel hidden via capability gating)

7. **Test:** `pnpm --filter apps/minimal dev` → file tree loads, clicking file opens editor tab, multiple tabs work, layout persists on reload

## Acceptance criteria
- apps/minimal boots with v2 packages
- FileTree renders and navigates files
- Clicking file opens in CodeMirror editor (tab)
- Multiple files can be open simultaneously (tab bar)
- Layout persists across reloads (Zustand persist)
- App.tsx is <15 lines
- No direct ../../../../packages/ imports remain
- No agent panel visible (capability gated)

## Estimated effort: ~0.25 day
notes: Deferred by operator on 2026-04-24; migration beads paused for now.

**Deliverables:**
- ai-elements copied into `src/front/primitives/` (Message, PromptInput→Composer, Tool, Terminal, CodeBlock, Reasoning). Provenance comment at top of each.
- Tool rendering: ai-elements `Tool` as generic renderer, `Terminal` for bash, `CodeBlock` for read. Custom `DiffView` only for `edit` (adapt from old `EditToolRenderer.jsx`).
- `<SessionToolbar />` — current-session + dropdown + "new chat" + per-session delete. Embedded in ChatPanel header.
- `theme.css` + CSS custom properties (`--boring-chat-*`). Default dark theme.
- Background title generation on first reply (fallback: `"New chat {date}"`).
- Slash commands infrastructure + 5 built-ins (`/clear`, `/reset`, `/model`, `/help`, `/cost`). `useAgentChat.registerCommand()` extension. ~80 LOC.
- Heartbeat events during long tool execution: synthetic `data-status` parts every 2s; `<Tool />` renders elapsed time. ~20 LOC.
- `/api/v1/agent/sessions/:id/changes` endpoint + per-session change tracker. ~80 LOC.

**Gate:** standalone app feels like a real chat in both modes; FileTree/Editor visibly update when agent writes files.

## Success Criteria

Standalone app feels like a real chat in both modes; FileTree and Editor visibly update when the agent writes files.
acceptance_criteria: Standalone app feels like a real chat in both modes; FileTree and Editor visibly update when the agent writes files.
close_reason: shipped — 3 UX improvements: auto-scroll to latest message, empty state welcome prompt, streaming status indicator with pulse animation, Composer auto-focus via forwardRef. All 787 tests pass.

title: M3a: SSE file_changed events for tree/file invalidation
description:
**Goal:** When the agent writes files (via writeTool/editTool/bash), the frontend refreshes FileTree + Editor. Files: chat route + `src/front/hooks/useFileChangeStream.ts` (~60 LOC total).

**Architecture:** embedded in the chat UIMessage stream as `data-file-changed` parts. No separate SSE channel — same ordering guarantees, one transport.

**Event shape (AI SDK custom data part):**
```ts
{
  type: 'data-file-changed',
  data: {
    op: 'write' | 'edit' | 'unlink' | 'rename' | 'mkdir',
    path: string,                  // workspace-relative
    oldPath?: string,              // for rename only
    toolCallId: string,            // which tool triggered the change (for UX attribution)
    timestamp: string,             // ISO 8601
    size?: number                  // for write/edit (bytes written)
  }
}
```

**Emission points (backend):**
- `writeTool.execute()` → emit after successful `workspace.writeFile()`.
- `editTool.execute()` → emit after successful `workspace.writeFile()`.
- `bashTool.execute()` → NOT emitted directly (bash writes are invisible to us). Alternative: if sandbox supports fs-watch (future), emit from that. For v1, bash-driven writes won't invalidate FileTree automatically — users may need to refresh. Documented limitation.
- UI tools don't touch fs → no emission.

**Client hook:**
```ts
// src/front/hooks/useFileChangeStream.ts
export function useFileChangeStream() {
  const queryClient = useQueryClient()
  return {
    onPart(part: UIMessagePart) {
      if (part.type !== 'data-file-changed') return
      const { op, path, oldPath } = part.data
      // Invalidate file-tree caches
      queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ['tree'] })
      // Invalidate specific file's cached content
      queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ['file', path] })
      if (oldPath) queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ['file', oldPath] })
    },
  }
}
```

**ChatPanel wiring:** subscribe to `useChat`'s `onData` or iterate `message.parts` after each streamed chunk.

**Batching:** if 10 files change in 100ms (e.g., npm install), emit all as individual events (don't batch). Client's React Query dedupes invalidations naturally.

**Tests:**
- writeTool → event emitted with op:'write' + correct path + size.
- editTool → op:'edit'.
- unlink → op:'unlink'.
- rename → op:'rename' + both paths.
- mkdir → op:'mkdir'.
- Client hook: event received → `invalidateQueries` called with correct keys.
- E2E: bash tool that writes a file → verify limitation (no auto-refresh); manual refresh picks up the file.

**Logging:** `[file-watch]` prefix. Log each emission with op + path + toolCallId (for correlating with chat transcript).

## Acceptance Criteria

All non-bash write paths emit `data-file-changed` events with the canonical shape. Frontend hook invalidates correct React Query keys. FileTree visibly updates after agent writes a file. Bash-driven writes don't auto-refresh (documented limitation; future fs-watch integration seam noted). Events correlate with tool calls via toolCallId.
acceptance_criteria: Bash tool that writes → chat stream contains `data-file-changed` part → FileTree refetches → new file visible.
close_reason: Implemented in-band file change events for chat stream: write/edit tools now attach fileChanges metadata; stream adapter emits data-file-changed parts (with toolCallId) before tool-output-available; frontend useAgentChat now wires useFileChangeStream invalidation for ['tree'] and ['file', path]/oldPath. Added targeted tests for tool metadata, stream emission ordering, and frontend invalidation hook behavior. Cross-review (claude-latest -p) verdict: ship (no blocking findings). Scoped tests: pnpm --dir packages/agent exec vitest run src/server/catalog/tools/__tests__/writeTool.test.ts src/server/catalog/tools/__tests__/editTool.test.ts src/server/harness/pi-coding-agent/__tests__/stream-adapter.test.ts src/front/hooks/__tests__/useAgentChat.test.ts src/front/hooks/__tests__/useFileChangeStream.test.tsx (46 passed, type errors: none). Bash-driven writes remain a documented limitation (no fs-watch emission path yet).

title: Gov: Review Decisions Registry (12 codex + 12 product/UX findings with adopt/defer rationale)
description:
**Goal:** Embedded registry of what was adopted vs deferred from two external reviews (spec §Review decisions). File: `docs/REVIEW_DECISIONS.md`.

**Why:** future contributors ask "why don't we have optimistic concurrency?" or "why no ChangeReviewDrawer?". Answer: we reviewed it and deferred — here's exactly what was considered and why. Beats re-litigating.

**External architecture review (Codex, 2026-04-22) — 12 findings:**

**Adopted (4):**
1. Workspace CRUD inconsistency → Removed `/api/v1/agent/workspaces` from HTTP surface; session routes trimmed.
2. Exec safety caps → `ExecOptions { timeoutMs, maxOutputBytes }`; `ExecResult` adds `durationMs`, `truncated`, Uint8Array-typed stdout/stderr.
6 (partial). Vercel API resilience → Circuit breaker wrapping the Vercel SDK client. Skipped lease/heartbeat/reconciler machinery (cloud-package territory).
11. Export surface consistency → Decision #15 rewritten. `SessionToolbar` instead of `SessionList`; no rename/delete dialogs; no `useRegisterTool` hook.

**Deferred (8)** — per "ship fast, accept known risk":
3. Optimistic write concurrency — accepted risk; silent last-write-wins on multi-tab (→ `risk.multi-tab-concurrency`).
4. `CanonicalSessionStore` (SQLite) — stay on pi JSONL. Drift risk accepted.
5. Performance budgets + benchmarks — no numbers until users complain.
6 (majority). Lease/heartbeat/reconciler — cloud-package territory.
7. Plugin name reservation + namespacing — last-registered wins (→ `research.plugin-name-collision`).
8. Control plane (policy/audit/telemetry) — retrofit when operationally needed.
9. `ChangeReviewDrawer` (review-before-apply) — workspace-package feature.
10. M0.5 invariant-tests milestone — invariants emerge via normal review.

**Product/UX review (internal, 2026-04-22) — 12 findings:**

**Adopted (11):**
1. Stream resumption in v1 (not deferred) → Session management + M3 milestone (→ `m3a.stream-resume`).
2. Uint8Array-typed exec output (binary-safe) → `ExecResult` interface.
3. Slash commands (`/clear`, `/reset`, `/model`, `/help`, `/cost`) → `m3b.slash-commands`.
4. CLI SSH/headless detection for browser-open → `m1.cli`.
5. Auto-gitignore for workspace artifacts → `m1.cli`.
6. Heartbeat events during long tool calls → `m3b.heartbeat-events`.
7. Vercel snapshot retention (keep-last-2) → `m2.snapshot-retention`.
8. CSS-var ↔ Tailwind contract worked example → `m3b.theme-css`.
9. `/api/v1/agent/sessions/:id/changes` endpoint → `m3b.changes-route`. (Originally flagged as rejected but upgraded to adopt for Claude Code parity.)
10. CLI `--logout` / `--reset-key` flags → `m1.cli`.
11. Windows-native support statement (WSL2 only) → `m1.direct-sandbox`.
12. Test strategy statement → `infra.*` beads.

**Considered and rejected (0):** all 12 findings landed.

**Total impact:** ~300 LOC added over pre-review baseline (net v1 ≈ 3,070 LOC).

**Cross-reference index:** this doc links each finding → its implementing bead(s), so readers can trace "review said X" → "bead Y implements it."

**Maintenance:** if an accepted-risk item bites (user complains), upgrade it from deferred → new bead + add entry to this doc's changelog.

## Acceptance Criteria

`docs/REVIEW_DECISIONS.md` exists with all 24 findings (12 codex + 12 product/UX) + adopt/defer reasons + cross-links to implementing beads. Linked from README + DECISIONS.md. Changelog section for future upgrades of deferred items.

title: Migration: apps/agent-backend — ai-sdk → pi-coding-agent rewrite
description:
**Goal:** Migrate apps/agent-backend to v2 agent. **Current:** bwrap backend + ai-sdk harness. **Target:** bwrap + pi-coding-agent.

**Step-by-step migration checklist:**

1. **Branch** off main; feature-flag via `V2_AGENT=1` env until cutover.

2. **Package deps:**
   - Remove: ai-sdk direct deps, custom harness code.
   - Add: `@boring/agent@workspace:*`.

3. **Harness rewrite:**
   - List current ai-sdk tools → port each to our `AgentTool` shape.
   - Port system prompt to pi's `createAgentSession({ systemPrompt })`.
   - Replace `/api/chat` handler with a redirect/alias to `/api/v1/agent/chat`.

4. **Frontend:**
   - Replace custom chat UI with `<ChatPanel />` from `@boring/agent`.
   - Swap useSWR/useChat imports to our `useAgentChat` hook.

5. **Config:**
   - `BORING_AGENT_MODE=local` (bwrap preserved).
   - `BORING_AGENT_WORKSPACE_ID` + `WORKSPACE_ROOT`.

6. **Tests:** run existing e2e smoke against v2 backend; fix any shape mismatches.

7. **Feature-flag cutover:** default `V2_AGENT=1` in staging → prod.

**Estimated effort:** 1–2 days.

**Blockers:** v2 agent package published (post-M1).

## Acceptance Criteria

apps/agent-backend runs with v2 agent in local mode. All existing e2e tests pass. Ported tools match pre-migration behavior. Feature flag allows fast rollback.
notes: Deferred by operator on 2026-04-24; migration beads paused for now.

title: M4: Pi extension discovery wiring
description:
**Goal:** Allow pi extensions to register tools alongside our defaults. File: `src/server/harness/pi-coding-agent/pluginLoader.ts`.

**Discovery:**
- `~/.pi/agent/extensions/*.{ts,js,mjs}` (global user)
- `.pi/extensions/*.{ts,js,mjs}` (workspace-local)
- npm modules prefixed `pi-plugin-*` (per pi convention)
- git URLs declared in `.pi/extensions.json`

**Wiring:** pass pi's extension list into `createAgentSession({ tools: [...ourTools, ...pluginTools] })`.

**Local mode only:** plugins run in the Node host process (they import `node:fs`, `pi.exec`). Irrelevant for remote VM mode.

**Tests:** drop a hello-world extension into `~/.pi/agent/extensions/`, assert its tool shows up in the catalog endpoint.

## Acceptance Criteria

title: M2 — Remote mode (Vercel Sandbox)
description:
**~1 week.** Prove the core architectural claim: swapping adapters flips from local to remote with zero changes elsewhere.

**Deliverables:**
- `createVercelSandboxWorkspace(sandbox)` — 30 LOC adapter against `sandbox.fs.*` + `sandbox.writeFiles` + path validation.
- `createVercelSandboxExec(sandbox)` — 30 LOC adapter wrapping `sandbox.runCommand`.
- `SandboxHandleStore` interface + `FileHandleStore` default impl at `~/.config/boring-agent/sandboxes.json`. Cloud package injects DB-backed impl. ~60 LOC.
- `resolveSandboxHandle(workspaceId)` — create-or-get-or-resume-from-snapshot + in-process cache, persists via SandboxHandleStore.
- `vercel-sandbox` `RuntimeModeAdapter` — workspace + sandbox share a single Sandbox handle.
- Snapshot-bake flow (src/server/sandbox/vercel-sandbox/bake.ts): creates seed sandbox + pip install + snapshot + caches snapshotId. One-time per `python_packages` hash. ~150 LOC.
- Periodic snapshot cron (every 10 min or on-idle).
- Circuit breaker around Vercel SDK (exponential backoff, fast-fail). ~30 LOC.
- Snapshot retention policy (keep-last-2). ~15 LOC.
- Config: `BORING_AGENT_MODE`, `VERCEL_OIDC_TOKEN`, `VERCEL_TEAM_ID`.

**Gate:** `BORING_AGENT_MODE=vercel-sandbox pnpm dev` — same chat UI, same tools, files now living in a Firecracker VM. No other code changes. Switching modes mid-development is an env-var flip.

## Success Criteria

`BORING_AGENT_MODE=vercel-sandbox pnpm dev` serves the same chat UI, same tools; agent files live in a Firecracker VM. Switching modes is an env-var flip — no code changes.
acceptance_criteria: `BORING_AGENT_MODE=vercel-sandbox pnpm dev` serves the same chat UI, same tools; agent files live in a Firecracker VM. Switching modes is an env-var flip — no code changes.
close_reason: M2 Remote Mode complete — all children shipped: jru.1 (find_files, f318c84), jru.2 (grep_files, b5898ff), jru.3 (tree/stat cache, 04db555). Plus n1o (mode-flip E2E be82e7d), 082 (tarball e640081), 4vl (pricing 790e5af), 96h (cold-start fcc7f65), f4f (fs latency baca373), xzr (leak monitor b5898ff).

title: M2: Add find_files tool over FileSearch (mitigate vercel fs latency)
description: Triggered by boring-ui-v2-f4f benchmark: vercel-exec find(100 files) p50 ~297ms vs local-bwrap ~26ms (>11x). Implement a first-class find_files tool that uses FileSearch abstraction instead of shelling out to find. Acceptance: chat can enumerate matching files without invoking bash find; docs note this as preferred path for vercel-sandbox mode.
close_reason: shipped in 1cb50cb; cross-reviewed by claude: ship; added find_files tool over FileSearch + catalog wiring + tests + vercel-sandbox docs note

title: M2: Add grep_files batched tool to avoid per-file read loops
description: Triggered by boring-ui-v2-f4f benchmark: vercel-exec grep(50 files) p50 ~292ms vs local-bwrap ~26ms (>11x), and per-file read/stat ops are 180-1000x slower than local node fs. Implement grep_files tool that executes one batched search in runtime adapter (prefer ripgrep where available) and returns structured matches. Acceptance: tool can search 50+ files with one invocation and includes file/path/line metadata for UI rendering.
close_reason: shipped — grep_files batched tool: sandbox.exec() with rg||grep fallback, structured match output, 14 unit tests passing

title: M2: Add short-lived tree/stat cache for vercel-sandbox workspace
description: Triggered by boring-ui-v2-f4f benchmark: vercel-fs stat p50 ~299ms vs local node stat ~0.3ms (~996x), readFile/writeFile/mkdir also 200x+ slower. Add bounded in-process cache for readdir/stat results in vercel-sandbox workspace adapter with invalidation on write/rename/unlink/mkdir. Acceptance: repeated stat/readdir in same turn avoid redundant remote calls; cache invalidation correctness covered by unit tests.
close_reason: shipped in 04db555; cross-reviewed by claude: ship; added short-lived stat/readdir cache invalidation hook for sandbox.exec + cache correctness tests

description:

close_reason: Work shipped in commits 987bf78 (barrel exports) + 6f49636 (26-test contract). All 3 upstream blockers now closed: tk3 (e42e2b7), z6u (571cca9), 0gl (9b41d0d). Formal close now unblocked.

title: M1: buildBwrapArgs (port — security-critical)
description:
**Goal:** Port the bwrap argument builder. File: `src/server/sandbox/bwrap/buildBwrapArgs.ts`.

**Source:** Old boring-ui `packages/workspace/src/server/adapters/bwrap.ts` lines ~22–58.

**SECURITY REVIEW REQUIRED:** This bead MUST be reviewed by a second agent or human before merge. It defines the sandbox boundary — subtle flag errors create sandbox escapes.

**Behavior:** Given `{workspaceRoot}`, returns `string[]` of `bwrap` flags creating a sandbox where:
- `/workspace` → bind-mounted from `workspaceRoot` (writable)
- Read-only binds: `/usr`, `/lib`, `/lib64`, `/bin`, `/etc/ssl`, `/etc/ca-certificates`, etc. (see `RO_BIND_DIRS`)
- `--unshare-all --share-net` (proc/user/ipc/uts isolated; keep network)
- `--die-with-parent` — so if our Node process dies, sandbox terminates.
- `--chdir /workspace`
- `--setenv HOME /workspace`
- `--proc /proc --dev /dev --tmpfs /tmp`

**Why flag order matters:** bwrap applies binds in order. Read-only binds must come BEFORE `--chdir`. Port verbatim where possible.

**Constants:**
- `BWRAP_TIMEOUT_SECONDS = 30`
- `KILL_GRACE_SECONDS = 5`
- `RO_BIND_DIRS` — list of host paths to bind read-only.

## Security invariants
- `--unshare-all` MUST always be present (no exception, no config to disable)
- `--die-with-parent` MUST always be present
- workspaceRoot bind is writable; ALL other binds are read-only
- No user-controlled string appears unescaped in the args array
- workspaceRoot is validated before reaching this function (realpath resolved, no symlink chasing outside allowed root)

## Unit tests (vitest)

### Snapshot + structure
1. Snapshot test: args array for known workspaceRoot matches expected output
2. `--unshare-all` present in every generated args array
3. `--share-net` present (network allowed)
4. `--die-with-parent` present
5. `--bind workspaceRoot /workspace` present exactly once
6. All RO_BIND_DIRS have `--ro-bind` entries
7. RO binds appear before `--chdir` in the array (ordering invariant)
8. `--chdir /workspace` present
9. `--setenv HOME /workspace` present

### Adversarial inputs (security-critical)
10. workspaceRoot with spaces: `/home/user/my project` — args correctly quoted/separated
11. workspaceRoot with shell metacharacters: `/tmp/$(whoami)` — treated as literal path, no shell expansion
12. workspaceRoot with newlines: `/tmp/foo\nbar` — rejected or escaped
13. workspaceRoot with null bytes: `/tmp/foo\x00bar` — rejected
14. workspaceRoot as symlink: should be pre-validated by caller (test that buildBwrapArgs does NOT resolve symlinks — that is tx7 responsibility)
15. workspaceRoot that does not exist: function still builds args (existence check is caller responsibility), but assert no crash
16. Empty workspaceRoot string: throws or returns error (not silent empty bind)
17. Very long workspaceRoot (>4096 chars): rejected

### Negative tests
18. Removing `--unshare-all` from RO_BIND_DIRS does NOT affect its presence (it is hardcoded, not derived)
19. No `--bind-try` in output (we want hard failures, not silent skips)

## Acceptance criteria
- Snapshot test for args array
- Required flags present (`--unshare-all`, `--die-with-parent`, `--share-net`)
- `--bind` for workspaceRoot present exactly once
- RO binds precede `--chdir`
- All 19 adversarial + structural tests pass
- Security review completed before merge
acceptance_criteria: Snapshot test for args array; required flags present; --bind for workspaceRoot present exactly once; RO binds precede --chdir.

title: Gov: Single source of truth for env + config schema
description:
**Goal:** One canonical Zod schema for all env vars + config keys. File: `src/shared/config-schema.ts`.

**Contents:**
```ts
export const ConfigSchema = z.object({
  // Runtime
  BORING_AGENT_MODE: z.enum(['direct','local','vercel-sandbox']).optional(),
  BORING_AGENT_WORKSPACE_ROOT: z.string().optional(),
  BORING_AGENT_WORKSPACE_ID: z.string().default('default'),
  BORING_AGENT_TEMPLATE_PATH: z.string().optional(),
  BORING_AGENT_AUTH_TOKEN: z.string().optional(),

  // Providers
  ANTHROPIC_API_KEY: z.string(),

  // Vercel mode
  VERCEL_OIDC_TOKEN: z.string().optional(),
  VERCEL_TEAM_ID: z.string().optional(),

  // TOML-sourced (runtime/sandbox sections)
  runtime: z.object({ mode: z.string().optional() }).optional(),
  workspace: z.object({ template_path: z.string().optional() }).optional(),
  sandbox: z.object({
    python_packages: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
    system_packages: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
    vercel: z.object({
      runtime: z.enum(['node24','node22','python3.13']).optional(),
      snapshot_id: z.string().optional(),
    }).optional(),
  }).optional(),
})
```

**Precedence rules:**
1. CLI flag
2. Env var
3. `boring.app.toml` / `.boring-agent/config.toml`
4. Default

**Documented in:** `docs/CONFIG.md` with full table.

**Acceptance:** all env/CLI/TOML reads funnel through `loadConfig()`; invalid configs fail fast with clear errors.

## Acceptance Criteria

All env/CLI/TOML reads funnel through `loadConfig()`. Invalid configs fail fast. `docs/CONFIG.md` has full table.

title: M1: createNodeWorkspace adapter
description:
**Goal:** Node fs-backed `Workspace` adapter. File: `src/server/workspace/createNodeWorkspace.ts`.

**Shape:**
```ts
export function createNodeWorkspace(root: string): Workspace {
  return {
    root,
    async readFile(rel) { const p = validatePath(root, rel); await assertRealPath...(root,p); return fs.readFile(p,'utf8') },
    async writeFile(rel, data) { ... ensureWritable ... fs.writeFile(p, data) },
    async unlink(rel) { ... fs.rm(p, { recursive: false }) },   // file OR empty dir
    async readdir(rel) { ... fs.readdir(p, { withFileTypes: true }) → Entry[] },
    async stat(rel) { ... fs.stat(p) → Stat },
    async mkdir(rel, opts) { ... fs.mkdir(p, opts) },
    async rename(from, to) { validate both; fs.rename(abs(from), abs(to)) },
  }
}
```

**Uses:** path helpers on every call (no exceptions).

**Shared by:** `direct` and `local` modes (both use same host fs).

**Tests:**
- Happy path for all 7 ops.
- Path traversal rejections on every op.
- `rename` within root (move file, rename file, rename dir).
- `unlink` on empty dir succeeds; on non-empty dir rejects.
- `readdir` returns `{name, kind}` — no extra fields.

**Done when:** Passes shared `Workspace` conformance suite (in infra epic).

## Acceptance Criteria

Passes shared Workspace conformance suite. All 7 methods work with traversal rejection. rename handles move AND in-place rename. unlink on empty dir succeeds; non-empty dir rejects.
acceptance_criteria: Passes shared Workspace conformance suite. All 7 methods work with traversal rejection. rename handles move AND in-place rename. unlink on empty dir succeeds; non-empty dir rejects.

title: Phase 4: Playwright E2E Tests
description:
## Goal
Golden-path E2E tests + bridge protocol E2E test. ~2 min CI.

## What to implement

### Golden paths (Playwright, workspace-playground app)

#### File workflow
1. Click file in FileTree → editor tab opens with correct content
2. Edit file in CodeMirror → dirty indicator (dot) appears on tab
3. Cmd+S → file saved (dirty indicator disappears)
4. Close tab → reopen same file → content matches saved version

#### Layout persistence
5. Drag sidebar wider → reload page → sidebar width restored
6. Open 3 tabs → close middle tab → reload → 2 tabs restored in correct order
7. Collapse sidebar → reload → sidebar still collapsed

#### Panel lifecycle
8. Open file → panel registers in tab bar
9. Close panel via X button → tab removed, panel unmounted
10. Open same file again → panel re-created (not cached)

#### Chat layout
11. Switch chat session → messages update
12. Open artifact from chat → artifact panel opens adjacent

#### Markdown workflow
13. Open .md file → tiptap editor renders rich content
14. Toggle bold → text formatted → save → reload → formatting preserved

### Bridge protocol E2E (~50 LOC, critical)
Proves the full command-based bridge flow end-to-end:

```
Test flow:
1. Playwright opens workspace-playground at localhost
2. Separate fetch() client simulates agent:
   - POST /api/v1/ui/commands { kind: "openFile", params: { path: "/src/main.ts" } }
3. Playwright asserts: new editor tab appears in workspace within 2s
4. Agent client: POST /api/v1/ui/commands { kind: "showNotification", params: { msg: "Hello", level: "info" } }
5. Playwright asserts: toast notification appears
6. Playwright: user closes file tab
7. Agent client: GET /api/v1/ui/state → verify openPanels no longer includes main.ts
8. Playwright: SSE event stream test — open EventSource, verify events arrive with v:1 protocol version
```

This test validates:
- Command POST → SSE delivery → workspace execution
- Workspace state → PUT to server → agent reads via GET
- Protocol version field present
- End-to-end latency <2s

### CI integration
- Run on every PR (~2 min target)
- Playwright config: 1 worker (sequential, avoids port conflicts)
- Full suite (chromium + firefox + webkit) on merge to main only
- Retry: 1 retry per test (flake protection)

## Implementation files
- tests/e2e/golden-path.spec.ts — scenarios 1-14
- tests/e2e/bridge-protocol.spec.ts — scenario 15 (bridge E2E)
- tests/e2e/playwright.config.ts — workspace-playground server setup

## Success criteria
- All 15 E2E scenarios pass
- Bridge E2E proves command flow works end-to-end
- No flaky tests (0 flakes over 10 consecutive runs)
- <2 min total runtime on CI
- Test output includes detailed step logging for debugging failures

title: v1 Migration Context + Implementation Reference
description:
## Goal
Reference document for the implementing agent. Contains ALL v1→v2 migration context, files NOT to port, per-app compatibility, and shared knowledge the implementer needs. This bead is read-only context — no code to write, but every other bead should be understood in the context of this information.

## v1 → v2 Reference Map

### Phase 1: Foundation
| v2 target | v1 reference | What to learn |
|-----------|-------------|---------------|
| DockviewShell.tsx | packages/workspace/src/front/layouts/ide/IdeLayout.jsx (1718 lines, focus lines 1–80, 400–500) | Dockview init: DockviewReact setup, onReady callback, component map registration, api.fromJSON() for restore. SIMPLIFY: v1 is 1718 lines — most is panel lifecycle that belongs in hooks. |
| PanelChrome.tsx | packages/workspace/src/front/components/DockTab.jsx (143 lines) | Tab rendering with file icons, close button, active state. Replace all custom CSS with shadcn/tailwind. |
| dockview-reset.css | packages/workspace/src/front/layouts/ide/IdeLayout.jsx — .dockview-* overrides in imported CSS | Identify which dockview classes need zeroing. Also check node_modules/dockview-react/dist/styles/dockview.css for full default theme. |
| store/index.ts | packages/workspace/src/front/persistence/LayoutManager.js (618 lines) | Layout save/restore: dockApi.toJSON() serialization, localStorage read/write, validation. DROP: version migration system, lastKnownGoodLayout backup, multi-key storage. KEEP: toJSON/fromJSON round-trip, validation. |
| PanelRegistry.ts | packages/workspace/src/front/registry/panes.jsx (484 lines) | Registration: register(id, { component, title, icon, placement, constraints, requires }). Lazy loading via React.lazy(). Capability gating. DROP: requiresRouters, requiresFeatures (legacy). KEEP: requiresCapabilities. |
| IdeLayout.tsx | packages/workspace/src/front/layouts/ide/IdeLayout.jsx (full file) | v1 is 1718 lines because layout logic, hooks, and panel lifecycle are inlined. v2: IdeLayout is ~30 lines building a LayoutConfig. Study for group arrangement (sidebar left locked, center tabs, right rail) and constraints. |
| ChatLayout.tsx | packages/workspace/src/front/layouts/chat/ChatCenteredWorkspace.jsx (434 lines) | Same simplification. Study group arrangement + SurfaceShell.jsx (544 lines) for artifact surface dockview. |
| useDockLayout.ts | packages/workspace/src/front/hooks/useDockLayout.js (337 lines) | Sidebar discovery (findSidebarGroup()), collapse toggle, center group tracking, panel activation, group constraints. |
| usePanelActions.ts | packages/workspace/src/front/hooks/usePanelActions.js (16 KB) | Open/close/activate panel. File-to-panel routing. Editor tab management. HEAVY — extract only core open/close/activate, leave file-specific logic to panes. |

### Phase 2: Built-in Panes
| v2 target | v1 reference | What to learn |
|-----------|-------------|---------------|
| FileTreePane.tsx | packages/workspace/src/front/components/FileTree.jsx (1129 lines) + panels/FileTreePanel.jsx (344 lines) | Tree rendering, expand/collapse, click-to-open, context menu. REWRITE with react-arborist + shadcn. |
| MarkdownEditor.tsx | packages/workspace/src/front/components/Editor.jsx (912 lines) | PORT directly. Keep tiptap. Restyle with shadcn/tailwind. Port toolbar, task lists, code blocks. |
| CodeEditorPane.tsx | packages/workspace/src/front/components/CodeEditor.jsx (176 lines, react-simple-code-editor + Prism) + panels/EditorPanel.jsx (417 lines) | File loading (useFileContent()), save (useFileWrite()), dirty state, external change detection. FULL REWRITE: react-simple-code-editor → CodeMirror 6. Port load/save/dirty pattern. |
| DataCatalogPane.tsx | packages/workspace/src/front/panels/DataCatalogPanel.jsx (87 lines) | Simple list. Port with shadcn Card/Badge. |
| EmptyPane.tsx | packages/workspace/src/front/panels/EmptyPanel.jsx (44 lines) | Placeholder. Trivial rewrite. |
| useFileData.ts | packages/workspace/src/front/providers/data/queries.js (10 KB) + providers/data/httpProvider.js (8.4 KB) | React Query hooks, HTTP client, auth header injection, 401 retry. DROP: useGitStatus, lightningFs, isomorphicGit, pyodide providers. |

### Phase 3: Agent Integration
| v2 target | v1 reference | What to learn |
|-----------|-------------|---------------|
| bridge/client.ts + bridge/commands.ts | packages/workspace/src/server/services/uiStateImpl.ts (201 lines) + packages/workspace/src/front/utils/frontendState.js (2.6 KB) | Current UI state bridge: panel snapshots, command queuing. v2: bridge reads/writes single Zustand store. |
| bridge/client.ts | packages/workspace/src/server/http/uiStateRoutes.ts (289 lines) | Current HTTP endpoints. REPLACE with command-based SSE+POST bridge. Port command vocabulary. |
| dynamicLoader.ts | No v1 equivalent | Reference: Vite import.meta.glob(), React lazy + Suspense from panes.jsx lines 20-50, PanelErrorBoundary.jsx (58 lines). |
| Agent pane slot | packages/workspace/src/front/registry/panes.jsx lines 440-470 | Agent panel registration with capability gating. Lazy loading. |
| Session list UI | packages/workspace/src/front/layouts/chat/ChatCenteredWorkspace.jsx lines 50-120 + layouts/chat/NavRail.jsx (133 lines) | Session switcher. PORT the UI, not state management (agent owns state in v2). |
| Tool → artifact routing | packages/workspace/src/front/layouts/chat/utils/toolArtifactBridge.js (6.4 KB) + hooks/useToolBridge.js (5.3 KB) | How tool results become panels. SIMPLIFY: v2 agent calls bridge.openPanel() explicitly. Keep as optional convenience. |

### Phase 4: Polish
| v2 target | v1 reference | What to learn |
|-----------|-------------|---------------|
| Theme system | packages/core/src/front/hooks/useTheme.jsx | Light/dark toggle, localStorage, system preference. Port, wire to shadcn CSS vars. |
| Keyboard shortcuts | packages/core/src/front/hooks/useKeyboardShortcuts.js | Shortcut registration, priority handling. Consider shadcn CommandDialog for Cmd+P. |
| Responsive layout | packages/workspace/src/front/hooks/useResponsiveSidebarCollapse.js (1.5 KB) | Auto-collapse sidebar below breakpoint. Port breakpoint logic, use shadcn Sheet for mobile. |
| Panel error boundary | packages/workspace/src/front/components/PanelErrorBoundary.jsx (58 lines) | Error boundary with retry button. Port, style with shadcn. |

## Files Explicitly NOT to Port
| v1 file | Reason |
|---------|--------|
| components/Terminal.jsx (578 lines) | Terminal/PTY no longer used in any app |
| panels/TerminalPanel.jsx (384 lines) | Same |
| panels/ShellTerminalPanel.jsx (298 lines) | Same |
| components/SyncStatusFooter.jsx (489 lines) | Git sync UI — agent responsibility |
| hooks/useAutoSync.js | Git auto-sync — agent responsibility |
| hooks/useLightningFsGitBootstrap.js (8.5 KB) | Browser git — dropped (HTTP only) |
| providers/data/lightningFsProvider.js | Browser FS — dropped |
| providers/data/isomorphicGitProvider.js | Browser git — dropped |
| providers/data/pyodideRunner.js | Browser Python — dropped |
| hooks/useGitHubConnection.js | Auth — agent/core responsibility |
| hooks/useFrontendStatePersist.js | Replaced by bridge in v2 |
| components/FrontmatterEditor.jsx | YAML editing — not in v2 scope |
| chrome/UserMenu.jsx | App chrome — app shell, not workspace |
| server/adapters/* (nodeFs, directBash, bwrap) | Moved to agent |
| server/http/fileRoutes.ts, execRoutes.ts, gitRoutes.ts | Moved to agent |
| server/jobs/execJob.ts | Moved to agent |
| server/workspace/paths.ts, helpers.ts | Moved to agent |
| All *.d.ts, *.d.ts.map, *.js build artifacts | v1 build artifacts, not source |

## Per-App v2 Compatibility
| App | Compat | Key gaps |
|-----|--------|----------|
| minimal | ~95% | FileTree needs prop-based variant (solved by Tier 3 standalone) |
| custom-layout | ~90% | Same — 3-column CSS grid + standalone components |
| chat | ~20% (by design) | 80% is agent code. Workspace provides layout shell + artifact surface. |
| agent-backend | ~15% (by design) | Layout toggle dropped (app implements). Capability fetching is app shell's job. |
| ide | ~5% (by design) | 95% is app-shell code. Auth, routing, cloud features not workspace's concern. |
| agent-frontend | DROPPED (permanent) | Offline mode permanently dropped. |

Low percentages are correct — they reflect auth, routing, cloud, and agent logic moving to proper owners.

## Custom Events Inventory (v1 → v2)
| v1 Event | v2 Replacement |
|----------|----------------|
| boring-ui:user-settings-open | App shell responsibility |
| boring-ui:sync-interval-changed | Dropped (agent owns sync) |
| boring-ui:agent-prompt | bridge.sendMessage() |
| boring-ui:shell-state | store.subscribe() |
| theme-toggle-request | useTheme() hook |
| bui:openFile | bridge.openFile() |
| PI session events (4) | Agent package responsibility |

## localStorage Key Inventory (Collision Avoidance)
| v1 key pattern | v2 status |
|----------------|-----------|
| boring-ui:{prefix}:{projectRoot}:layout | → boring-ui-v2:layout:{workspaceId} |
| boring-ui:{prefix}:{projectRoot}:tabs | Dropped (derived from layout) |
| boring-ui:{prefix}:{projectRoot}:lastKnownGoodLayout | Dropped |
| boring-ui-theme / kurt-web-theme | → boring-ui-v2:preferences |
| boring-ui:chat-sessions:v1 | Agent package responsibility |
| boring-ui:terminal-* | Dropped (terminal → agent) |

## CSS Breakpoints to Parameterize
| v1 breakpoint | v1 purpose | v2 approach |
|---------------|-----------|-------------|
| 56px | Sidebar collapse width | collapsedWidth in GroupConfig |
| 420px | Surface min-width | constraints.minWidth in GroupConfig |
| 1180px | Compact layout trigger | tailwind lg breakpoint |
| 960px | Mobile layout trigger | tailwind md breakpoint |

## IDE Boot Flow Boundary
| Step | v2 owner |
|------|----------|
| Capabilities fetch | App shell |
| Auth flow (OIDC, session) | App shell |
| Workspace routing | App shell |
| Data provider scope | Workspace (WorkspaceProvider) |
| Resolved capabilities | Workspace (registry filters) |
| Layout selection | App shell |
| Layout rendering | Workspace |

## @boring/core Dependency Contract
Every import workspace v2 makes from @boring/core:

### HTTP transport (@boring/core/front)
- apiFetchJson(url, opts), buildApiUrl(path), getHttpErrorDetail(error), routes constant

### UI utilities (@boring/core/front)
- cn() (tailwind class merger), copyTextToClipboard(text), formatShortcut(shortcut), useViewportBreakpoint()

### Keyboard shortcuts (@boring/core/front)
- useKeyboardShortcuts(shortcuts), DEFAULT_SHORTCUTS

### Accessibility (@boring/core/front)
- announceToScreenReader(message), trapFocus(containerEl), useReducedMotion()

### Theme (@boring/core/front)
- useTheme(), ThemeToggle

### Configuration (@boring/core/front)
- useConfig(), useCapabilities()

### Design tokens (@boring/core/front)
- ICON_SIZE_INLINE, ICON_SIZE_TOOLBAR, ICON_SIZE_ACTIVITY

### UI components (@boring/core/ui — 13 vendored)
- Button, Input, Badge, Separator, Tabs, Tooltip, DropdownMenu, Select, Dialog, Avatar, Switch, Label, Textarea
- v2 imports these, does NOT re-vendor

### Server (@boring/core/server)
- createAuthHook, ServerConfig type

### Shared (@boring/core/shared)
- CapabilitiesResponse type, Git types (GitStatus, GitChange, etc.)

## Deep Import Anti-Pattern (v1 → v2 fix)
v1 apps use 11+ deep imports bypassing the public API. v2's index.ts exports everything apps need — zero deep imports required. See Public API bead for full export list.
close_reason: Reference-only bead — migration context, v1 file map, per-app compatibility, CSS breakpoints, localStorage keys, and @boring/core dependency contract are all documented in bead description. No code deliverable. Context available for downstream beads (87n, vtt, 8qd, 0ld, shj).

title: M3b: /api/v1/agent/sessions/:id/changes + tracker
description:
**Goal:** Per-session change summary endpoint + server-side tracker. File: `src/server/http/routes/sessionChanges.ts` (~80 LOC).

**Tracker:**
- Hook writeTool / editTool / bash-with-write to record `{path, op, size}` per session.
- Store in-memory keyed by `sessionId` (persistent in M3b.polish or later).

**Route:** `GET /api/v1/agent/sessions/:id/changes` → `{files: [{path, op, size}, ...]}`.

**Why:** powers post-turn summary ("Claude modified 3 files") like Claude Code.

**Tests:** multiple writes + edits accumulate; deletes recorded; response shape stable.

## Acceptance Criteria

Multiple writes+edits accumulate. Deletes recorded. Response shape stable.
acceptance_criteria: Multiple writes+edits accumulate. Deletes recorded. Response shape stable.
close_reason: Implemented per-session file-ops tracker + endpoint: added in-memory SessionChangesTracker, wired chat route to record data-file-changed chunks by session, and added GET /api/v1/agent/sessions/:id/changes returning { files: [...] }. Hooked app wiring so chat + changes route share tracker. Added bash tool file-change inference for successful write/edit/unlink/rename/mkdir patterns (including delete tracking) so bash-originated mutations flow into tracker. Added focused tests for route accumulation/session isolation/deletes, tracker parsing+bounds, and bash inference edge-cases. Validation: vitest (49 tests across chat/sessionChanges/tracker/bash/createAgentApp), package typecheck, invariants pass. Cross-review (claude-latest) verdict: ship. Commit: 494e498.

title: Phase 4: Performance Budget Enforcement
description:
## Goal
CI enforcement of bundle budgets + performance benchmarks.

## Performance budgets (from plan Section 4.7)
| Metric | Target | Measurement |
|--------|--------|-------------|
| Initial JS (shell + registry) | <150KB gz | vite build + gz analysis |
| Total all-loaded ceiling | <800KB gz | vite build + gz analysis |
| Shell render (empty layout) | <500ms TTI | Playwright performance.mark() |
| File tree (10K files) | <120ms first view | Vitest benchmark (virtualized) |
| First CodeMirror render | <500ms | Playwright performance.mark() |
| First tiptap render | <500ms | Playwright performance.mark() |
| Layout persistence round-trip | <50ms | Vitest benchmark |
| Bridge command latency | <100ms (SSE), <2s (poll) | Vitest benchmark |

## What to implement

### Bundle size analysis (CI)
- Post-build script: vite build → analyze chunks with rollup-plugin-visualizer or custom script
- Check two numbers: initial chunk <150KB gz, total <800KB gz
- Output human-readable report to stdout
- CI step: run build, compare against budgets, fail PR if exceeded
- PR comment with bundle diff (optional, nice-to-have)

### Runtime performance benchmarks (CI)
- Vitest bench files for synchronous operations:
  - Zustand store hydration from localStorage (target: <50ms)
  - File tree rendering 10K entries (target: <120ms for visible viewport)
  - Bridge command serialization round-trip (target: <10ms)
- Playwright performance tests for browser operations:
  - Shell TTI with empty layout (target: <500ms)
  - First CodeMirror render with 1000-line file (target: <500ms)
  - First tiptap render with 500-word document (target: <500ms)

### Performance marks API
- usePerformanceMark(name) hook: calls performance.mark on mount
- Wrap DockviewShell mount, CodeMirror first render, tiptap first render
- Marks readable by Playwright: page.evaluate(() => performance.getEntriesByType('mark'))

### CI integration
- GitHub Actions step after build
- Exit code 1 if any budget exceeded
- Summary table in CI output showing metric / actual / budget / pass/fail

## Implementation files
- scripts/check-bundle-size.ts — post-build budget checker
- src/hooks/usePerformanceMark.ts — performance.mark wrapper
- tests/bench/ — vitest benchmark files
- tests/perf/ — playwright performance test files

## Unit tests (vitest)

### Bundle budget checker
1. Passes when initial chunk <150KB gz
2. Fails (exit 1) when initial chunk >150KB gz
3. Passes when total <800KB gz
4. Fails (exit 1) when total >800KB gz
5. Outputs human-readable table with metric names and sizes

### usePerformanceMark hook
6. Calls performance.mark(name) on mount
7. Does not call performance.mark on re-render (only once)
8. Cleans up mark on unmount (optional, verify no memory leak)

### Vitest benchmarks
9. Store hydration benchmark completes under 50ms (threshold assertion)
10. File tree 10K render benchmark completes under 120ms
11. Bridge command serialization under 10ms

## E2E test specs (Playwright)
12. Shell TTI: navigate to workspace-playground → measure time to first interactive → <500ms
13. CodeMirror render: open file tab → performance.mark('cm-first-render') fires → <500ms
14. Tiptap render: open markdown tab → performance.mark('tiptap-first-render') fires → <500ms

## Success criteria
- CI reports bundle sizes on every PR
- CI fails if initial >150KB or total >800KB
- Performance benchmarks run in CI
- All benchmarks pass within defined budgets
- All 14 tests pass
close_reason: shipped — reviewed by claude-cli: ship

title: Phase 2: HTTP Data Provider
description:
## Goal
Typed fetch client + React Query hooks for all agent-hosted HTTP endpoints. DataProvider context so panes access data without knowing transport.

## What to implement

### DataProvider (src/providers/DataProvider.tsx)
- React context wrapping React Query client + typed fetch
- Configured via WorkspaceProvider props: apiBaseUrl, authHeaders
- Provides hooks for panes to use
- Internal QueryClient instance (no need for apps to provide one)

### Typed fetch client (all endpoints are agent-hosted)
- GET /api/v1/tree?dir=<path> → FileEntry[]
- GET /api/v1/files?path=<path> → { content: string, mtime: number }
- POST /api/v1/files { path, content } → void (create/update file)
- DELETE /api/v1/files?path=<path> → void (delete file)
- GET /api/v1/stat?path=<path> → { isDir: boolean, size: number, mtime: number }
- GET /api/v1/files/search?q=<glob>&limit=<n> → string[]
- POST /api/v1/dirs { path } → void (create directory)
- POST /api/v1/files/move { from, to } → void (rename/move file or directory)
- No git routes in v1 (git UI dropped per decisions table)

### React Query hooks
- useFileContent(path): { data: { content, mtime }, isLoading, error, refetch }
- useFileList(dir): { data: FileEntry[], isLoading, error } — with refetchInterval: 3000ms
- useFileWrite(): useMutation for POST /api/v1/files — invalidates useFileContent cache on success
- useFileSearch(query, limit): search hook with debounce
- useStat(path): { data: { isDir, size, mtime }, isLoading, error }
- useCreateDir(): useMutation for POST /api/v1/dirs
- useMoveFile(): useMutation for POST /api/v1/files/move — invalidates tree + content caches
- useDeleteFile(): useMutation for DELETE /api/v1/files — invalidates tree cache

### Query key patterns
- ['files', path] for file content
- ['tree', dir] for directory listing
- ['stat', path] for file metadata
- ['search', query, limit] for search results
- Mutations invalidate related query keys on success

### Error handling
- 401/403 → fire onAuthError callback (app shell handles redirect to login)
- 404 → return null/undefined (component-level "file not found" UI)
- 5xx → auto-retry 3x with exponential backoff (React Query retry config)
- Network error → auto-retry 3x
- Timeout: 10s default, configurable per route. Show toast on timeout.
- Auth header injection: authHeaders from WorkspaceProvider props injected into every request

## v1 references
- queries.js (10KB) — React Query hooks, query key patterns
- httpProvider.js (8.4KB) — HTTP client, auth header injection, 401 retry
- DROP: useGitStatus, lightningFs, isomorphicGit, pyodide providers

## Considerations
- DataProvider ONLY used by pane wrappers (FileTreePane, CodeEditorPane, etc.)
- Standalone components (FileTree, CodeEditor) accept all data via props — NO DataProvider needed
- DataProvider is a React context wrapper for convenience, NOT an abstraction for swappable backends (decision: HTTP only, no DataProvider SPI)
- Polling paused during active edits (refetchInterval disabled when dirty)
- React Query staleTime: 0 for file content (always refetch), tree can be stale for 3s

## Unit tests (providers/DataProvider.test.ts)

### Typed fetch client
- Test: GET /api/v1/tree returns FileEntry array
- Test: GET /api/v1/files returns { content, mtime }
- Test: POST /api/v1/files sends { path, content }
- Test: DELETE /api/v1/files sends path param
- Test: POST /api/v1/dirs creates directory
- Test: POST /api/v1/files/move sends { from, to }
- Test: authHeaders injected into every request
- Test: apiBaseUrl prepended to all endpoint paths

### React Query hooks
- Test: useFileContent(path) fetches and returns content
- Test: useFileList(dir) returns directory listing
- Test: useFileList polling: refetchInterval fires every 3000ms
- Test: useFileWrite invalidates useFileContent cache on success
- Test: useMoveFile invalidates tree + content caches
- Test: useDeleteFile invalidates tree cache
- Test: useFileSearch debounces query

### Error handling
- Test: 401 response → onAuthError callback fired
- Test: 403 response → onAuthError callback fired
- Test: 404 response → no retry, returns error
- Test: 500 response → retries 3x with backoff
- Test: network error → retries 3x
- Test: timeout (10s) → shows toast notification

### Query key isolation
- Test: different paths produce different cache entries
- Test: mutations invalidate correct keys (not unrelated paths)

## Success criteria
- All hooks work with mock API responses
- Error handling: 401 fires callback, 5xx retries, timeout shows toast
- Query keys properly scoped (path-based cache invalidation)
- Hooks usable inside WorkspaceProvider context
- All file tree operations (create, rename, delete, move) have typed wrappers

Dependencies:
  -> boring-ui-v2-zwp (blocks) - Phase 1: WorkspaceProvider
  -> boring-ui-v2-dwe (blocks) - Phase 1: Project Scaffold

Dependents:
  <- boring-ui-v2-fds (blocks) - Phase 4: Error Handling + Recovery
  <- boring-ui-v2-8qd (blocks) - Phase 2: Markdown Editor Pane (Tiptap)
  <- boring-ui-v2-0ld (blocks) - Phase 2: Code Editor Pane (CodeMirror 6)
  <- boring-ui-v2-6el (blocks) - Phase 2: File Tree Pane

title: M2: E2E — mode flip works with zero code changes
description:
**Goal:** Prove the architectural claim. `BORING_AGENT_MODE=vercel-sandbox pnpm dev` serves the same UI, same tools; agent runs in a Firecracker VM instead of host/bwrap.

**Test approach (Playwright against a real or mocked Vercel account):**
1. Boot with `BORING_AGENT_MODE=vercel-sandbox`.
2. Send "list files in /" via chat.
3. Assert response mentions `/vercel/sandbox` or similar VM paths, NOT host paths.
4. Write a file via the UI's FileTree "new file" action.
5. Send "cat the new file" → assert contents match.

**Logging:** heavy test logs tagged `[m2-modeflip]` for CI debugging. Print sandbox ID, API call counts, cold-start duration.

**Done when:** flipping env vars works both ways; no code changes needed.

## Acceptance Criteria

`BORING_AGENT_MODE=vercel-sandbox pnpm dev` works same as local. Flipping env vars both ways works. No code changes.

title: Deferred: GitHub Connect + /api/v1/git/* routes (v1.x)
description:
**Scope:** Git UI dropped from agent v1 AND workspace v1. Neither package has a git consumer → `/api/v1/git/*` routes would be dead code. Agent runs git via the `bash` tool (`git status`, `git commit`, etc.).

**Design already documented (for future):**
- Git routes ~200 LOC of thin `sandbox.exec('git …')` wrappers with output parsing.
- Credentials in remote mode: token-in-URL for HTTPS (`https://x-access-token:$TOKEN@github.com/…`).
- SSE invalidation: `data-git-changed` events after write ops.
- VM persistence: git config (`user.name`, `user.email`) seeded on sandbox create.

**Trigger to implement:** any UI consumer emerges (agent v1.x status bar, or workspace reviving git badges).

**Acceptance:** stays deferred until consumer lands; design notes preserved in this bead.

## Acceptance Criteria

Stays deferred until UI consumer lands. Design notes preserved in this bead.
