title: Phase 4: Responsive Layout
description:
## Goal
Mobile sidebar as sheet, tablet auto-collapse, minimum panel sizes enforced.

## What to implement

### Mobile layout (<768px)
- Sidebar collapses to shadcn Sheet (slide-in overlay from left)
- Sheet triggered by hamburger menu button (top-left)
- Sheet dismisses on outside click or file selection
- Main content area takes full width when sidebar is sheet

### Tablet layout (<1024px)
- Auto-collapse sidebar below breakpoint
- Sidebar collapses to icon-only rail (40px wide)
- Click sidebar icon to expand temporarily (overlay mode)
- User can pin sidebar open (persisted to preferences)

### Minimum panel sizes
- Enforced via GroupConfig constraints (DockviewShell handleReady)
- Sidebar: min 200px, max 50% viewport
- Editor: min 300px
- Agent panel: min 250px
- Prevents panels from being dragged to zero width

### Breakpoint detection
- Use @boring/core's useViewportBreakpoint() hook
- Responsive changes are CSS-first where possible (media queries)
- JS-driven changes (Sheet vs inline sidebar) via hook

## v1 references
- useResponsiveSidebarCollapse.js (1.5KB) — breakpoint logic for auto-collapse

## Unit tests (vitest + @testing-library/react)

### Mobile layout
1. Viewport <768px: sidebar renders as Sheet (not inline)
2. Hamburger button visible at <768px, hidden at >=768px
3. Click hamburger → Sheet opens with file tree
4. Click file in Sheet → Sheet closes + file opens
5. Click outside Sheet → Sheet dismisses

### Tablet layout
6. Viewport <1024px: sidebar auto-collapses to icon rail
7. Collapsed sidebar shows icons only (40px width)
8. Click collapsed sidebar icon → expands as overlay
9. Pin button in expanded overlay → keeps sidebar open (persisted)

### Panel constraints
10. Sidebar cannot be resized below 200px (drag stops at minimum)
11. Sidebar cannot exceed 50% of viewport width
12. Editor panel maintains minimum 300px width
13. Agent panel maintains minimum 250px width

### Breakpoint transitions
14. Resize from desktop → tablet: sidebar auto-collapses (smooth transition)
15. Resize from tablet → mobile: sidebar switches to Sheet mode
16. Resize from mobile → desktop: sidebar returns to inline mode

## E2E test specs (Playwright)
17. Set viewport to 375x667 (mobile) → hamburger visible, sidebar is Sheet
18. Set viewport to 800x600 (tablet) → sidebar collapsed to rail
19. Set viewport to 1440x900 (desktop) → sidebar inline, full width
20. Drag panel divider → verify minimum size enforced

## Success criteria
- Sidebar becomes Sheet on mobile
- Sidebar auto-collapses on tablet
- Panel sizes never go below minimums
- Transitions are smooth (no layout flash)
- All 20 tests pass
close_reason: shipped — reviewed by claude-cli: ship

title: Risk: Multi-tab concurrency (document + observe)
description:
**Risk class:** Accepted risk (spec: silent last-write-wins on multi-tab file writes).

**Scope for v1:** DOCUMENT, don't mitigate.

**Deliverable:**
- `docs/KNOWN_LIMITATIONS.md` entry: "Opening two tabs and editing the same file simultaneously: the later write wins silently. The agent has no awareness of concurrent edits."
- Future mitigation seam: optimistic write with `If-Match` header + 409 on conflict.

**Why accept:** dev-tool posture; mitigations cost >> user exposure for v1.

**Trigger to re-evaluate:** first bug report where a user loses edits.

## Acceptance Criteria

Documented in `docs/KNOWN_LIMITATIONS.md`. Future mitigation seam identified.
close_reason: shipped — documented multi-tab last-write-wins behavior and added characterization test; reviewed by claude: ship; landed in commit 8042f80 (shared-worktree mixed commit)

title: M1: editTool factory (string-replace)
description:
**Goal:** `edit` tool — string-replace edits on existing files. File: `src/server/catalog/tools/editTool.ts`.

**Parameters:** `{ path: string, oldString: string, newString: string, replaceAll?: boolean }`.

**Behavior:**
- Reads file via workspace.readFile.
- If `oldString` not present → error (agent must use exact content).
- If `replaceAll: false` and `oldString` appears more than once → error ("ambiguous; add context or replaceAll").
- Replaces + writes back via workspace.writeFile.
- Returns `{ path, replacements: number }`.

**Tests:** unique match replace; ambiguous match rejects without replaceAll; replaceAll succeeds; no-match rejects.

**Why NOT a diff-based edit tool:** Claude Code pattern. Exact-string-replace is simpler for LLMs than unified-diff; fewer parse errors.

## Acceptance Criteria

Unique-match replace works. Ambiguous match rejects without replaceAll. replaceAll succeeds on multiples. No-match rejects.
acceptance_criteria: Unique-match replace works. Ambiguous match rejects without replaceAll. replaceAll succeeds on multiples. No-match rejects.

## Success Criteria

title: Risk: Pi SDK version pinning + release monitoring (no semver guarantee)
description:
**Goal:** Lock down pi-coding-agent version strategy + monitoring. Spec §Risks: "Pi SDK API instability (v0.68.x, no semver)."

**Strategy:**
1. **Pin exact version in package.json** — no caret, no tilde. `"@mariozechner/pi-coding-agent": "0.68.3"` (or whatever exact version we target at M1).
2. **Pin via `overrides` in workspace root** (pnpm-workspace.yaml or package.json overrides) so transitive deps can't bump it.
3. **Monitor upstream releases:** RSS feed / GitHub Releases subscription on `@mariozechner/pi-coding-agent`. Ops channel (email or Slack) on new release.
4. **Manual upgrade protocol:**
   - Read release notes before upgrading.
   - Run full e2e suite (including risk.pi-abort-propagation-test + risk.pi-session-mapping-conformance) on new version locally.
   - If tests pass: upgrade in a dedicated PR with before/after test output + CHANGELOG-style notes.
   - If tests fail: open issue + pin stays; consider `PiAgentCoreHarness` fallback.

**No semver guarantee = no auto-upgrades:**
- Dependabot/Renovate config: IGNORE pi-coding-agent (document rationale in comment).
- CI has a scheduled weekly job that tries the latest pi version against our test suite; reports to a quarantine channel if tests fail. **Does not auto-PR.**

**Fallback readiness:**
- Keep `PiAgentCoreHarness` as a design-seam in the Harness interface (already there).
- If a pi-coding-agent breaking change lands + we can't work around: implement PiAgentCoreHarness in ~400 LOC (lower-level pi-agent-core API, no plugin ecosystem).

**Rationale (why accept the risk):** pi-coding-agent is single-maintainer; rewriting the harness is expensive. Pinning is the conservative default. Monitoring catches breaks early.

**Tests:**
- `package.json` audit: assert `@mariozechner/pi-coding-agent` version is exact (no range specifier). Lint in CI.
- Weekly CI job: try latest pi; report failures separately from main CI.

**Logging:** `[pi-version]` prefix in startup log: print exact version for forensics (`pi-coding-agent@0.68.3 loaded`).

## Acceptance Criteria

Exact-version pin in package.json + workspace overrides. CI lints for the exact specifier. Weekly canary job exists + reports failures. CONTRIBUTING.md has upgrade protocol. Fallback plan (PiAgentCoreHarness) tracked as design seam.

title: M3b: Tool renderers — ai-elements generic + DiffView for edit
description:
**Goal:** Render bash/read/write with ai-elements generic `Tool` + `Terminal` + `CodeBlock`. **One custom renderer: `DiffView` for `edit`.** Files: `src/front/toolRenderers/*`.

**Note on spec discrepancy:** spec §Options we ship > LOC table (line 462) says "no DiffView in v1" but spec §M3b roadmap (line 1354) says "Only 1 specialized renderer authored: a unified-diff view for edit". Resolution: **DiffView IS in v1** — the LOC row was stale. DiffView adapted from old boring-ui is ~40 LOC; small enough to ship.

**Mapping:**
- `bash` → `<Tool>{<Terminal>{stdout, stderr, exitCode}</Terminal>}</Tool>` (input=command shown at top)
- `read` → `<Tool>{<CodeBlock language={guessFromPath}>{content}</CodeBlock></Tool>` — truncated view for long files with "show all" affordance
- `write` → `<Tool>` with summary `"Wrote N bytes to <path>"` (no content render — too much for writes that could be large; delegate to Editor if user wants to inspect)
- `edit` → `<DiffView oldString={input.oldString} newString={input.newString} path={input.path} replaceAll={input.replaceAll} />` — custom unified-diff UI.
- `get_ui_state` → `<Tool>` with pretty-printed JSON of returned state. Collapsible.
- `exec_ui` → `<Tool>` with a compact "→ openFile(foo.md)" style summary. Matches the `data-ui-command` display card pattern.

**DiffView implementation:**
- Reference: `packages/agent/src/front/components/chat/EditToolRenderer.jsx` from old boring-ui.
- Library: `diff` (jsdiff) for computing unified diff; render with our primitives + CSS vars.
- Shows: file path header, +/- lines with color coding (via `--boring-chat-diff-add`/`-remove` vars).
- Collapsed view for long diffs (>30 lines) with "expand" button.

**Override seam:** `<ChatPanel toolRenderers={{ my_custom_tool: MyRenderer }} />`. Default map inlined; override merges deep. Workspace package uses this for workspace-specific tools.

**Tests:**
- Each renderer renders a snapshot for representative input.
- DiffView: multi-line addition, multi-line removal, mixed changes, empty-file diff, identical strings (renders "no change").
- Override via prop: custom renderer takes precedence.
- Fallback: unknown tool name → generic `<Tool>` with JSON.stringify.

**Logging:** none at render time (UI perf). `[tool-renderer]` only for dev warnings (e.g., malformed tool output that doesn't match expected shape).

## Acceptance Criteria

All 6 shipped tools have a renderer: bash, read, write, edit (custom DiffView), get_ui_state, exec_ui. Override via `toolRenderers` prop works with deep merge. Fallback generic renderer renders unknown tools legibly. DiffView handles edge cases (empty, identical, very long). Visual snapshots captured for regression.
acceptance_criteria: Each built-in tool has a renderer snapshot. DiffView renders multi-line changes cleanly. Override via `toolRenderers` prop merges deep.

title: M4: Verify plugins bypass Sandbox as designed (local mode only)
description:
**Goal:** Automated verification of spec §Risks > "Plugin × sandbox mismatch" design-time mitigation: **plugin loading is local-mode-only; plugin tools run in Node host (bypass Sandbox by design).**

**Why this test matters:** a future refactor could accidentally wire plugins through the Sandbox, silently changing the security model. A test catches this.

**Scenarios:**

1. **Direct mode loads plugins:** set `BORING_AGENT_MODE=direct`, drop a test extension into `~/.pi/agent/extensions/testplugin.ts` that registers a tool calling `process.cwd()`. Invoke tool via chat. Assert the tool reports the BACKEND process cwd (plugin bypassed Sandbox ✓).

2. **Local mode loads plugins:** same as above but `BORING_AGENT_MODE=local`. Plugin still calls `process.cwd()` — reports backend cwd (NOT bwrapped). Bypass confirmed.

3. **Vercel-sandbox mode SKIPS plugin discovery:** set `BORING_AGENT_MODE=vercel-sandbox` with the same extension present. Invoke via chat — expect 404/not-found. Plugin discovery disabled in remote mode.

4. **Warning log on plugin load:** log should say `[plugin] Loaded testplugin (host-process; bypasses sandbox)` with level=warn. Verify in log capture.

5. **Plugin + our default name collision:** drop a plugin registering `bash` → assert last-registered wins, log warning, chat still functional (covered in m4.name-collision; cross-link).

**Implementation:** test file `src/server/harness/pi-coding-agent/__tests__/pluginSandboxBypass.test.ts`. Fixture `test-fixtures/plugins/testplugin.ts` — minimal pi extension.

**Tests ARE the enforcement** — this is a design-time invariant that only code guarantees.

**Logging:** capture `[plugin]` logs for each load/skip event.

## Acceptance Criteria

4 scenarios pass: plugins load in direct mode, plugins load in local mode, plugins DON'T load in vercel-sandbox mode, warning logged on each load. Test file documents the invariant with a large comment block. Regression = test fails = PR blocked.

title: fix(agent): history replay drops user messages — add JSON messages endpoint
description:
**Symptom (reported by user):** On page refresh, past message formatting changes — user bubbles vanish, only assistant text comes back.

**Root cause:** packages/agent/src/server/http/routes/chat.ts line 154 — the replay branch filters to `msg.role === 'assistant'` and emits chunks via AI SDK's createUIMessageStream. Two issues:

1. User messages are silently dropped (the `continue`).
2. AI SDK v6 UIMessageChunk stream is shaped for ONE assistant response; it has no native `type: 'user-message'` or cross-role equivalent. Multi-message history replay is a stretch of the stream protocol.

**Fix (idiomatic AI SDK v6):**
1. Add `GET /api/v1/agent/chat/:sessionId/messages` → returns `UIMessage[]` JSON (full history including user turns).
2. In `useAgentChat`, on mount, fetch the endpoint and call `setMessages(fetchedMessages)` to hydrate. Keep `resume: true` for in-flight stream recovery, but drop the stream-based history replay.
3. Delete the history-replay branch in chat.ts' stream endpoint — let it return 204 when no active stream exists.
4. Tests: verify refresh shows same user + assistant sequence as live stream.

**Scope:** packages/agent/src/server/http/routes/chat.ts + hooks/useAgentChat.ts + one new route + 1–2 unit tests.

**Why P1:** visible UX regression, found post-v1 close. Small scope, high impact.
close_reason: shipped — reviewed by claude: ship; refresh now hydrates full UIMessage history via /messages, resume endpoint is live-stream-only 204 fallback, and regression tests updated

title: Research: Tailwind v4 conflict with workspace styles
description:
**Question:** When `@boring/workspace` also uses Tailwind v4, do our utility classes and theirs compose cleanly, or do we get preflight resets conflicting with the workspace's global styles?

**Investigation:**
1. Set up a minimal workspace-like host that imports both packages.
2. Render ChatPanel inside a workspace layout that also has Tailwind v4.
3. Check DevTools for specificity conflicts, unexpected resets, class-name collisions.

**Expected issues:**
- Two preflight injections → double-reset.
- Scoped utility classes bleed.

**Mitigation candidates (pick one):**
- Use CSS Modules inside primitives (bypass Tailwind for agent-internal styling).
- Ship a scoping config (prefix `agt-` on all our classes).
- Document "don't enable Tailwind preflight in both packages" as a hard requirement.

**Acceptance:** integration test or documented constraint.

## Acceptance Criteria

Integration test OR documented 'don't enable preflight in both packages' constraint.
close_reason: shipped — research doc + 7 guard tests. Three conflicts found (CSS var collision, double @layer base, utility identity), all mitigated by current architecture. Documented constraints: don't import both globals.css files; only one @import tailwindcss per document.

title: M1: Build pipeline (tsup + vite)
description:
**Goal:** `pnpm build` produces shippable artifacts. Files: `tsup.config.ts`, `vite.config.ts`, `package.json` scripts.

**Outputs:**
- `dist/shared/index.js` + `.d.ts` (pure types + interfaces)
- `dist/server/index.js` + `.d.ts` (Node-only entrypoints)
- `dist/front/index.js` + `.d.ts` (React components + hooks)
- `dist/frontend/` (Vite-built SPA; served statically by CLI)
- `dist/bin/boring-agent.js` (bundled CLI with shebang)

**package.json:**
- `"bin": { "boring-agent": "./dist/bin/boring-agent.js" }`
- `"exports"`: `.` / `./shared` / `./server` / `./front`

**Tests:** CI runs `pnpm build` and asserts all dist outputs exist + parseable.

## Acceptance Criteria

CI runs `pnpm build` and asserts all dist outputs exist + parseable. `bin` field points at compiled CLI.
acceptance_criteria: CI runs `pnpm build` and asserts all dist outputs exist + parseable. `bin` field points at compiled CLI.
close_reason: shipped — reviewed by claude: ship; build pipeline + CI artifact assertions delivered in commit f2289d3 (shared-worktree commit also included unrelated workspace test files)

title: Research: Plugin tool name collision precedence
description:
**Question:** When a pi plugin registers `bash` and our default `bash` is also present, who wins?

**Investigation:**
1. Write a synthetic plugin that registers a tool named `bash` with behavior "return 'FROM PLUGIN'".
2. Load standard catalog + plugin → invoke `bash` via chat → observe output.
3. Vary registration order: ours-then-plugin vs plugin-then-ours.
4. Try pi's namespace mechanisms if any.

**Document:** exact precedence rule in `docs/PLUGINS.md`.

**v1 decision:** last-registered wins (plugins after defaults = plugin wins).

**Future:** namespace defaults as `boring.bash` — deferred unless collision reports pile up.

**Acceptance:** rule documented with reproducible test.

## Acceptance Criteria

Precedence rule documented with reproducible test.
close_reason: shipped — reviewed by claude: ship. Documented plugin-vs-plugin same-name precedence in PLUGINS.md and added mergeTools unit test proving later plugin wins with warning and no hard error.

title: Phase 3a: Agent Pane Slot
description:
## Goal
Register ChatPanel from @boring/agent as a panel in the workspace registry. Workspace does NOT import from agent — the app shell passes the component in.

## What to implement
- ChatPanel registered via WorkspaceProvider panels prop (app shell provides it)
- Default placement: 'right' (IdeLayout) or center slot (ChatLayout)
- Lazy-loaded with React.lazy + Suspense wrapper
- requiresCapabilities: ['agent.chat'] — only available when capability is set

## How it works (no circular dependency)
- App shell imports ChatPanel from @boring/agent
- App shell passes it to WorkspaceProvider panels prop
- Workspace never imports from @boring/agent
- Types (WorkspaceBridge, CommandResult, etc.) in @boring/core

## Unit tests (vitest + @testing-library/react)

### Registration
1. Panel registered via WorkspaceProvider panels prop appears in registry
2. Panel has correct default placement (IdeLayout: 'right', ChatLayout: 'center')
3. Panel component lazy-loads (Suspense fallback shown, then component renders)

### Capability gating
4. Panel visible when capabilities include 'agent.chat'
5. Panel hidden when capabilities do NOT include 'agent.chat'
6. Adding 'agent.chat' capability dynamically makes panel appear

### Isolation (no circular dependency)
7. WorkspaceProvider renders without @boring/agent imported (no hard dependency)
8. Passing null/undefined for panels prop works (no agent panel)
9. Custom panel component (not ChatPanel) can be registered the same way

### Placement
10. In IdeLayout: agent panel renders in right group
11. In ChatLayout: agent panel renders in center group

## Success criteria
- Agent pane renders inside IdeLayout right rail
- Agent pane renders inside ChatLayout center
- Pane lazy-loads correctly
- Capability gating works (hidden when 'agent.chat' not in capabilities)
- All 11 tests pass

title: M0: Interface — SandboxHandleStore (DB injection seam)
description:
**Goal:** Lock the sandbox-handle persistence interface. File: `src/shared/sandbox-handle-store.ts`.

**Interface:**
```ts
export interface SandboxHandleRecord {
  workspaceId: string
  sandboxId: string
  snapshotId?: string
  createdAt: string
  lastUsedAt: string
}
export interface SandboxHandleStore {
  get(workspaceId: string): Promise<SandboxHandleRecord | null>
  put(record: SandboxHandleRecord): Promise<void>
  delete(workspaceId: string): Promise<void>
  list(): Promise<SandboxHandleRecord[]>
}
```

**Why:** Agent has no DB dependency. Default impl (`FileHandleStore`) writes `~/.config/boring-agent/sandboxes.json`. Future `@boring/cloud` injects a DB-backed impl via `createAgentApp({ sandboxHandleStore })`. Same pattern as `SessionStore`.

**Used by:** `resolveSandboxHandle(workspaceId)` (M2).

## Acceptance Criteria

`src/shared/sandbox-handle-store.ts` exports `SandboxHandleStore` + `SandboxHandleRecord`. Type-level stub passes.

**Design note — no M1 impl by design:** Bwrap sandboxes in M1 are ephemeral (no handle to persist — each sandbox is a fresh process). First implementation is M2: FileHandleStore, which persists Vercel sandbox handles for snapshot resume.
acceptance_criteria: `src/shared/sandbox-handle-store.ts` exports `SandboxHandleStore` + `SandboxHandleRecord`. Type-level stub passes.

title: Phase 3a: Chat Layout Integration
description:
## Goal
Integrate ChatLayout with agent pane, artifact surface (nested DockviewShell), session list UI, and chat hooks.

## What to implement

### ArtifactSurfacePane (src/panes/ArtifactSurfacePane.tsx)
- Renders its own nested DockviewShell inside ChatLayout's surface group
- storageKey: 'boring-ui-v2:surface' (own persistence, independent of outer shell)
- allowedPanels: ['code-editor', 'markdown-editor', 'csv-viewer', 'empty'] (guard against outer-shell panels)
- Only rendered when artifacts exist (conditional mount)
- Sync suppression flag prevents feedback loops between outer and inner state

Nested shell pseudo-code (from plan Section E):
```typescript
// panes/ArtifactSurfacePane.tsx — renders inside ChatLayout's surface group
function ArtifactSurfacePane({ artifacts, onSelectArtifact, onCloseArtifact }) {
  return (
    <DockviewShell
      layout={{
        groups: [{ id: 'artifacts', position: 'center', dynamic: true, placeholder: 'empty' }]
      }}
      storageKey="boring-ui-v2:surface"    // own persistence, independent of outer shell
      allowedPanels={['code-editor', 'markdown-editor', 'csv-viewer', 'empty']}
    />
  )
}
// Filters outer panel registry to allowed IDs. Has own API, own state, own lifecycle.
```

### Nested DockviewShell — isolation model (ninth pass)
Nesting works naturally because dockview instances are independent. No formal isolation protocol needed for v2.

1. **Persistence**: Each DockviewShell with unique storageKey creates its own Zustand persist partition. Root uses boring-ui-v2:layout, nested surface uses boring-ui-v2:surface.
2. **API scoping**: useDockviewApi() returns the API for the nearest ancestor DockviewShell. Panes inside nested shell interact with the correct instance.
3. **No panel ID namespacing**: panel IDs are plain strings. If two shells have panels with same ID, they're independent (different dockview instances).
4. **No bridge routing**: bridge commands always target root shell. Nested shell panels managed directly by the component rendering the nested DockviewShell.

### Chat hooks (simplified from v1's 8 → 2)
- **useArtifactPanels()** (~50 lines): open/close/track artifact panels in surface dockview
- **useArtifactRouting()** (~30 lines): optional convenience mapping tool names to panel types

**Eliminated by bridge** (no longer needed — these were v1 coupling mechanisms):
| v1 hook | Replacement |
|---------|-------------|
| useShellStatePublisher | → store subscriptions |
| useShellPersistence | → Zustand persist |
| useSessionState/Store/ServerSessions | → agent owns via bridge props |
| useToolBridge | → agent calls bridge.openPanel() explicitly |

### Session list UI (workspace renders, agent owns state)

Session list ownership boundary (from plan Resolved Questions):
```
@boring/agent (owns state):
  sessions: Session[]
  activeSessionId: string
  switchSession(id: string): void
  createSession(): Session
  deleteSession(id: string): void

@boring/workspace (owns UI):
  <SessionList
    sessions={agent.sessions}
    activeId={agent.activeSessionId}
    onSwitch={agent.switchSession}
    onCreate={agent.createSession}
    onDelete={agent.deleteSession}
  />
```

- SessionList component: renders { id, title, updatedAt }[] from agent
- Calls agent's switchSession/createSession/deleteSession
- Active session indicator
- Context menu: rename, delete
- Workspace doesn't know what a "session" contains — just renders the list with titles and handles the chrome

### NavRail integration
- NavRail props: { sessions, activeSessionId, onSwitch, onCreate, onNewChat, onToggleSurface, surfaceOpen }
- App shell passes user menu separately (not workspace's concern)

### Tool → artifact routing simplification
v1 approach: toolArtifactBridge.js (6.4KB) + useToolBridge.js (5.3KB) — workspace infers panel type from tool results.
v2 approach: agent explicitly calls bridge.openPanel() — workspace no longer infers. useArtifactRouting() is an optional convenience (~30 lines) that maps common tool names to panel types for backward compatibility.

## v1 references
- ChatCenteredWorkspace.jsx (434 lines) — nav rail + chat + surface
- SurfaceShell.jsx (544 lines) — artifact surface dockview (nested)
- NavRail.jsx (133 lines) — session switcher
- toolArtifactBridge.js (6.4KB) + useToolBridge.js (5.3KB) — tool→artifact routing (simplified in v2)
- SurfaceDockview pattern: filtered registry via allowedPanels, gets layout via props, uses callbacks for state flow

## Considerations
- Nested DockviewShell: independent instance, own API, own state, own lifecycle
- useDockviewApi() inside nested shell returns nested shell's API (nearest ancestor)
- No panel ID namespacing needed (different dockview instances)
- Bridge commands target root shell only (no multi-shell routing in v1)
- If multi-shell routing needed post-launch (agent opening artifacts in surface from outside), add shell parameter to bridge commands then

## Unit tests (layouts/chat-integration.test.tsx)

### ArtifactSurfacePane
- Test: renders nested DockviewShell with storageKey="boring-ui-v2:surface"
- Test: allowedPanels filters registry (only code-editor, markdown-editor, csv-viewer, empty)
- Test: outer-shell panel IDs not available in nested shell
- Test: conditional mount — not rendered when no artifacts
- Test: sync suppression — inner state changes don't feedback to outer

### Chat hooks
- Test: useArtifactPanels() opens artifact panel in surface dockview
- Test: useArtifactPanels() closes artifact panel
- Test: useArtifactPanels() tracks open artifact panels
- Test: useArtifactRouting() maps tool name to correct panel type
- Test: useArtifactRouting() returns undefined for unknown tool

### Session list
- Test: SessionList renders list of { id, title, updatedAt }
- Test: clicking session calls onSwitch(id)
- Test: create button calls onCreate
- Test: context menu delete calls onDelete(id)
- Test: active session has indicator
- Test: empty session list shows "No sessions" state

### NavRail
- Test: NavRail renders with sessions list
- Test: onNewChat callback fires on button click
- Test: onToggleSurface toggles surface open state
- Test: surfaceOpen prop reflects in toggle button state

### Nested shell independence
- Test: useDockviewApi() inside nested shell returns nested shell's API
- Test: bridge commands target root shell, not nested
- Test: nested shell has independent persistence (different storageKey)

## E2E tests (Playwright)
- Test: ChatLayout session switch → correct session displayed
- Test: ChatLayout artifact open → surface appears with correct content
- Test: agent message in chat → artifact renders in surface
- Test: nested shell resize independent of outer shell

## Success criteria
- ChatLayout renders with agent chat in center, artifacts in surface
- Artifact surface opens/closes correctly (conditional mount)
- Session list displays and switches sessions
- Nested DockviewShell has independent persistence
- allowedPanels guard prevents outer panels from rendering in surface
- Two chat hooks work correctly (artifact panels + routing)
close_reason: done

title: M0: Interface — Sandbox + SandboxCapability + ExecResult
description:
**Goal:** Lock the Layer-1 sandbox contract. File: `src/shared/sandbox.ts`.

**Interface (exact shapes from spec §The four abstractions > Sandbox):**
```ts
export type SandboxCapability = 'exec' | 'isolated-code'

export interface Sandbox {
  readonly id: string                          // 'bwrap' | 'direct' | 'vercel-sandbox' | 'boxlite' | …
  readonly placement: 'server' | 'browser'
  readonly capabilities: readonly SandboxCapability[]

  init(ctx: { workspace: Workspace; sessionId: string }): Promise<void>

  // Always available (capability 'exec'):
  exec(cmd: string, opts?: ExecOptions): Promise<ExecResult>

  // Optional — present iff capabilities includes 'isolated-code':
  executeIsolatedCode?(input: IsolatedCodeInput): Promise<IsolatedCodeOutput>

  dispose?(): Promise<void>
}

export interface ExecOptions {
  cwd?: string
  env?: Record<string, string>
  signal?: AbortSignal
  timeoutMs?: number                           // hard timeout; kills the child process
  maxOutputBytes?: number                      // per-stream cap; output beyond this is truncated
  onHeartbeat?: (elapsedMs: number) => void    // called every 2s during active exec — for <Tool /> ticking UI
}

export interface ExecResult {
  stdout: Uint8Array                            // RAW BYTES, binary-safe — server adapter decodes per encoding
  stderr: Uint8Array
  exitCode: number
  durationMs: number
  truncated: boolean                            // true if maxOutputBytes was hit
  stdoutEncoding?: 'utf-8' | 'binary'           // best-effort classification for UI rendering
  stderrEncoding?: 'utf-8' | 'binary'
}

// ONLY meaningful when capabilities includes 'isolated-code' (future M6+):
export interface IsolatedCodeInput {
  code: string
  language: 'python' | 'shell'
  image?: string                                // e.g. 'python:3.12-slim' — sandbox-defined set
  packages?: string[]                           // installed before run (pip/npm/etc, sandbox decides)
  sandboxId?: string                            // reuse a prior pooled VM (TTL-based)
  vmSize?: 'xxs' | 'xs' | 's' | 'm' | 'l'
}

export interface IsolatedCodeOutput {
  sandboxId: string                             // for reuse
  stdout: string
  stderr: string
  exitCode: number
}
```

**Invariants:**
- `Uint8Array` (NOT `Buffer` — Node-only type leak forbidden in shared contracts).
- Every impl enforces `timeoutMs` (default 30s in adapters) and `maxOutputBytes` (default 1 MB) — bytes beyond cap get dropped and `truncated: true`.
- `capabilities` declares support statically. v1 sandboxes declare `['exec']`. Future ones add `'isolated-code'` which unlocks the 5th catalog tool.
- `Sandbox` takes a `Workspace` in `init()` so exec `cwd` defaults to workspace root. Prevents escape-via-cwd.
- `onHeartbeat(elapsedMs)` fires every 2s while a command is still running. Stream adapter uses this to emit `data-status` heartbeat chunks.

**Tests:** Type-level stub + conformance-test requirement noted for infra.conformance-tests.

## Acceptance Criteria

`src/shared/sandbox.ts` exports `Sandbox`, `SandboxCapability`, `ExecOptions` (with `onHeartbeat`), `ExecResult` (stdout/stderr as `Uint8Array`), `IsolatedCodeInput/Output`. Type-level stub passes. Conformance test harness imports this interface.
acceptance_criteria: `src/shared/sandbox.ts` exports `Sandbox`, `SandboxCapability`, `ExecOptions`, `ExecResult`. `stdout`/`stderr` are `Uint8Array` (not `Buffer`). Type-level stub passes.
close_reason: shipped — cross-reviewed by Codex (gpt-5.3-codex): ship

title: M1: createPiCodingAgentHarness
description:
**Goal:** Wrap `@mariozechner/pi-coding-agent` as our `AgentHarness`. File: `src/server/harness/pi-coding-agent/createHarness.ts`.

**Reference:** Old `packages/agent/src/server/harnesses/pi/runtime.ts` — IMPORTANT the old code uses `pi-agent-core`; v2 uses `pi-coding-agent` (which wraps pi-agent-core + plugins + sessions). API surface differs.

**Shape:**
```ts
export function createPiCodingAgentHarness(opts: { tools: AgentTool[] }): AgentHarness {
  return {
    id: 'pi-coding-agent', placement: 'server',
    sessions: new PiSessionStore(),   // in-memory in M1; JSONL-backed in M3a
    async *sendMessage(input, ctx) {
      const session = createAgentSession({ tools: opts.tools })   // pi API
      const piStream = session.runTurn({
        sessionId: input.sessionId, message: input.message,
        model: input.model, thinkingLevel: input.thinkingLevel,
        abortSignal: ctx.abortSignal,
      })
      yield* piEventsToUIMessageChunks(piStream)                   // see m1.stream-adapter
    },
  }
}
```

**Invariants:**
- **Pi built-ins are skipped.** We pass only our `tools: [...]`. Verified by M1 acceptance test (`m1.test.pi-builtin-opt-out`).
- TypeBox → JSON Schema adaptation at the tool boundary: our tools declare JSON Schema; pi receives TypeBox; adapter converts one-way.
- Model registry adaptation: our `'sonnet' | 'haiku' | 'opus'` → pi's model IDs.

**Tests:** one round-trip: send a message with a no-op tool, assert at least one chunk arrives; assert no pi default tool (like pi's bash) is registered.

## Acceptance Criteria

createAgentSession receives our tools array only; no pi built-ins in catalog (verified by `research.pi-builtin-optout`). Single turn roundtrip works.
acceptance_criteria: createAgentSession receives our tools array only; no pi built-ins in catalog (verified by `research.pi-builtin-optout`). Single turn roundtrip works.

title: M3b: Heartbeat events during long tool execution
description:
**Goal:** Every 2s during an in-flight tool call, emit a synthetic `data-status` UIMessage part so `<Tool />` shows elapsed time. Files: stream adapter (~10 LOC) + Tool renderer (~10 LOC).

**Server-side:** wrap the tool execution promise; every 2s if not yet resolved, emit `{type:'data-status', data:{toolCallId, elapsedMs}}`. Flush on resolve/reject.

**Client-side:** `<Tool>` component displays "Running… (Ns)" updating from latest status part.

**Why:** long `bash` calls feel frozen without heartbeats; users ctrl-C unnecessarily.

**Tests:** a 6s bash command emits 3 heartbeats; UI shows ticking counter; resolve clears heartbeat.

## Acceptance Criteria

6s bash emits 3 heartbeats. UI shows ticking counter. Resolve clears heartbeat.
acceptance_criteria: 6s bash emits 3 heartbeats. UI shows ticking counter. Resolve clears heartbeat.

title: Phase 4: Bombadil Property-Based Testing
description:
## Goal
Property-based exploration using Antithesis Bombadil. Random UI exploration validating invariants.

## Properties (invariants)
- Layout has no overlapping panels (no negative widths/heights)
- Essential panels cannot be closed
- Sidebar collapse/expand is reversible
- Layout serialization round-trips: toJSON → fromJSON → toJSON is idempotent
- No React error boundary triggers during normal exploration
- Bridge state matches visible UI (open panels list = actual rendered panels)
- Active file in bridge = file shown in editor

## Actions (random sequences)
- Open/close panels, resize sashes, collapse/expand sidebar
- Click files in tree, switch tabs, trigger shortcuts
- Send bridge commands (openFile, openPanel, closePanel, showNotification)
- Toggle theme, switch layouts

## CI integration
- Nightly run: 10-30 min exploration
- Reports to Slack/GitHub issue if failures found
- Not blocking PRs (too slow)

## Success criteria
- Bombadil finds no property violations
- At least 7 invariants validated
- Runs for 30 min without crash

title: Phase 4: Theme System
description:
## Goal
Light/dark mode via shadcn CSS variables. CodeMirror + dockview theme synced. ThemeProvider + useTheme() hook.

## Background
Workspace's ThemeProvider wraps core's useTheme() and extends it to sync CM6 and dockview CSS variables. Apps use workspace's useTheme() (re-exported), not core's directly. This avoids theme divergence between code editors and workspace chrome.

## What to implement
### theme/ThemeProvider.tsx + useTheme.ts
- ThemeProvider wraps core's useTheme, extends to sync CM6 + dockview CSS vars
- useTheme(): { theme: 'light' | 'dark', setTheme, toggleTheme } hook
- Persisted to boring-ui-v2:preferences (separate from layout, never reset)
- System preference detection (prefers-color-scheme)
- ThemeProvider adds data-theme attribute to workspace container element

### theme/codemirror-theme.ts
- CM6 theme reading shadcn CSS variables at runtime (--background, --foreground, --muted, etc.)
- Auto-updates on theme change (EditorView.theme reconfigured via compartment)
- Maps shadcn variables to CM6 theme spec: { "&": {}, ".cm-content": {}, ".cm-gutters": {}, etc. }

### Dockview theme sync
- dockview-overrides.css maps --dv-* variables to shadcn vars:
  - --dv-activegroup-visiblepanel-tab-background-color → var(--background)
  - --dv-tabs-container-scrollbar-color → var(--muted)
  - etc. (~15 variable mappings)
- Auto-follows theme toggle, no JS needed (pure CSS variable cascade)

## v1 references
- useTheme.jsx — light/dark toggle, localStorage persistence, system preference detection

## Unit tests (vitest + @testing-library/react)

### ThemeProvider
1. Renders children with default theme (system preference)
2. useTheme().theme returns 'light' when prefers-color-scheme: light
3. useTheme().theme returns 'dark' when prefers-color-scheme: dark
4. setTheme('dark') updates theme and applies data-theme="dark" to container
5. toggleTheme() switches from light to dark and vice versa
6. Theme persists to boring-ui-v2:preferences localStorage key
7. On mount, reads persisted theme (overrides system preference)
8. Clearing localStorage reverts to system preference

### CodeMirror theme sync
9. CM6 theme compartment reconfigured when theme changes
10. Dark mode: CM6 gutters use --muted CSS variable (verify computed style)
11. Light mode: CM6 background matches --background CSS variable

### Dockview theme sync
12. dockview-overrides.css loaded (verify CSS rules exist in document.styleSheets)
13. Theme toggle changes dockview tab background (via CSS variable cascade)

## Success criteria
- Toggle between light/dark mode
- CodeMirror theme matches workspace theme
- Dockview chrome matches workspace theme
- Theme persists across reloads (preferences key)
- System preference respected as default
- All 13 tests pass
close_reason: Theme system shipped: toggleTheme, data-theme, system preference detection, CM6 compartment reconfiguration, 13 tests

title: Phase 4: CSP Compatibility
description:
## Goal
Workspace works under strict CSP. Validate with Playwright test.

## Target CSP
default-src 'self'; script-src 'self'; style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline'; connect-src 'self'; img-src 'self' data: blob:; font-src 'self'

## What to implement
- style-src 'unsafe-inline' required by CM6 style-mod (no nonce support upstream)
- Tiptap HTML paste: configure HTMLSanitizer to strip script, on*, javascript: URLs, iframe
- No eval() anywhere. No 'unsafe-eval'.
- Playwright test: set strict CSP header, verify workspace loads and functions (open file, edit, save, bridge command) without violations

## Success criteria
- Playwright CSP test passes
- No CSP violations in console
- Tiptap paste sanitized

title: M1: Path helpers + realpath validation (port)
description:
**Goal:** Port + adapt the path validation primitives. File: `src/server/workspace/paths.ts`.

**Functions to port (from old boring-ui `packages/workspace/src/server/workspace/paths.ts` ~80 LOC):**
- `validatePath(workspaceRoot, relPath): string` — rejects `..`, absolute paths, null bytes; returns joined absolute path.
- `assertRealPathWithinWorkspace(workspaceRoot, absPath): Promise<void>` — `realpath()` on the resolved path; rejects if symlink escapes root.
- `ensureExistingWorkspacePath(workspaceRoot, relPath)` — validate + realpath + existence check.
- `ensureWritableWorkspacePath(workspaceRoot, relPath)` — validate + dir-exists check on parent.

**Why port, not rewrite:** battle-hardened, already fuzzed in production. Copy verbatim where possible; only adapt imports.

**Security invariants:**
- Rejects `..` segments before resolve.
- Rejects absolute paths.
- Rejects null bytes.
- Follows symlinks via `realpath` and checks final resolved path starts with `realpath(workspaceRoot)`.

**Tests:** path traversal attack corpus (~20 vectors: `../../etc/passwd`, `foo/../../../bar`, `/etc/passwd`, `foo\x00bar`, symlink-to-outside). Every vector must throw.

**Logging:** on reject, log the vector + reason (`path-escape`, `absolute-path`, `null-byte`, `symlink-escape`) for debugging forensics.

## Acceptance Criteria

All 20+ path-traversal vectors in `infra.path-traversal-corpus` rejected. Helpers throw with categorized reason codes (`path-escape`, `absolute-path`, `null-byte`, `symlink-escape`).
acceptance_criteria: All 20+ path-traversal vectors in `infra.path-traversal-corpus` rejected. Helpers throw with categorized reason codes (`path-escape`, `absolute-path`, `null-byte`, `symlink-escape`).

title: UI-shadcn subpath: ship Vercel-grade styled ChatPanel via @boring/agent/ui-shadcn
description:
**Goal:** ship @boring/agent/ui-shadcn with a Vercel-chatbot-grade styled ChatPanel. Same hook, same public API as bare @boring/agent/ui — just plugs ai-elements (Tailwind+shadcn) primitives under the hood.

**Source strategy:** vendor ai-elements via their CLI. DO NOT hand-roll primitives.
```
cd packages/agent
npx ai-elements@1.9.0 add conversation message response reasoning tool code-block prompt-input loader
```
These drop into src/front-shadcn/components/ui/. We own them afterward (add VENDORED.md attribution).

**Architecture:**
@boring/agent/ui-shadcn/ChatPanel.tsx
  - useAgentChat() — unchanged, same hook as bare ChatPanel
  - <Conversation><ConversationContent>
      - <Message role=...>
          - <MessageContent> styled bubble
          - <Response> markdown renderer (for text parts)
          - <Reasoning> collapsible (for reasoning parts)
          - <Tool> card (for tool parts, via toolRenderers override)
    </ConversationContent></Conversation>
  - <PromptInput><PromptInputTextarea/><PromptInputSubmit/></PromptInput>

**Reference for hook + props contract (v1, not v2):**
/home/ubuntu/projects/boring-ui/packages/workspace/src/front/layouts/chat/ChatStage.jsx shows the Stage+empty-state+scroll pattern. We do NOT port the full Stage+Wings shell — just the chat column. But reading ChatStage is useful for the empty state + scroll behavior.

**6 atomic commits — one per step, run tests + typecheck + invariants between:**

1. feat(agent): scaffold ui-shadcn subpath export
   - package.json exports map adds ./ui-shadcn (types + import + prebuilt-css subpath)
   - tsup.config.ts new entry src/front-shadcn/index.ts
   - peerDependencies: tailwindcss@^4, lucide-react
   - new files: src/front-shadcn/index.ts, ChatPanel.tsx (placeholder exporting a stub that throws 'not implemented'), styles.css (Tailwind @tailwind directives + @boring/agent theme layer)
   - tsup builds clean, existing @boring/agent/ui unchanged

2. feat(agent): vendor ai-elements into ui-shadcn
   - npx ai-elements@1.9.0 add conversation message response reasoning tool code-block prompt-input loader
   - lands into src/front-shadcn/components/ui/
   - add VENDORED.md with source + copy date + re-port procedure
   - no ChatPanel wiring yet — just the primitives build clean

3. feat(agent): wire ChatPanel to ai-elements primitives
   - Replace placeholder ChatPanel with full implementation using vendored primitives
   - Identical public API to bare ChatPanel (same sessionId, bridge, toolRenderers, extraCommands, onSessionReset props)
   - useAgentChat hook unchanged
   - Default tool renderer = <Tool/> from ai-elements; toolRenderers overrides win
   - Text parts → <Response/>; reasoning → <Reasoning/>; user/assistant → <Message role=/>
   - 18+ vitest snapshot tests covering same cases as bare ChatPanel

4. feat(agent): shadcn example app
   - packages/agent/examples/with-shadcn/
   - Tailwind config + @tailwindcss/vite + shadcn globals
   - Same reverse-tool demo as with-custom-tool
   - HOST env var binds 0.0.0.0 for Mac access
   - Vite config transformIndexHtml for React preamble
   - Visual target: matches https://vercel.com/templates/next.js/chatbot

5. feat(agent): prebuilt CSS fallback
   - tsup processes styles.css → dist/ui-shadcn/styles.css (Tailwind compiled, self-contained)
   - Consumers without Tailwind just: import '@boring/agent/ui-shadcn/styles.css'
   - Document the two consumption modes in README

6. docs(agent): ui-shadcn README + gallery
   - packages/agent/docs/UI-SHADCN.md — two APIs, when to pick which, peer deps, theming
   - Update packages/agent/README.md with a 'Two UI flavors' section + screenshots
   - Cross-review verdict required before close

**Non-goals this bead:**
- Stage+Wings shell (NavRail/BrowseDrawer/SurfaceShell) — separate future bead
- Markdown editor pane, file tree pane — separate beads
- Removing or deprecating @boring/agent/ui — NEVER

**Ship criteria:**
- examples/with-shadcn/ visually matches Vercel chatbot template
- examples/with-custom-tool/ still works unchanged (bare primitives path regression-free)
- pnpm --filter @boring/agent build clean, 0 type errors
- Cross-review: at least one ship verdict from other agent

**Dependencies resolved:** AI SDK v6 stream protocol fix already landed (commit 26fdb1e). boring-ui-v2-4c9k (contract-test follow-up) is independent.

title: M4: Name-collision precedence (document + runtime enforcement)
description:
**Goal:** When a plugin registers `bash` and our default `bash` is also present, which wins? File: `src/server/catalog/mergeTools.ts`.

**v1 decision:** last-registered wins. Plugin tools registered AFTER defaults → plugin overrides. Document this.

**Future consideration:** namespace ours (`fs.bash`) — deferred.

**Log a warning:** on collision: `[catalog] Tool "bash" overridden by plugin <name>`.

**Tests:** register a conflicting tool; assert which wins; assert warning logged.

## Acceptance Criteria

Collision precedence documented. Warning logged on collision. Test asserts which wins.
acceptance_criteria: Collision precedence documented. Warning logged on collision. Test asserts which wins.

Wired createAgentApp to use mergeTools and plugin-name labeling. Added mergeTools tests for merge order + collision override/warn behavior and documented precedence in docs/PLUGINS.md. Validation: vitest (mergeTools + createAgentApp), package typecheck, invariants all pass. Cross-review (claude-latest) verdict: ship. Commit: 9a9118d.

title: Phase 4: Storybook Visual Regression
description:
## Goal

## What to implement

- CodeEditor (JS, Python, JSON, large file, read-only)

### Stories for dockview chrome

- TabBar (multiple tabs, active tab, overflow)
- GroupChrome (locked, collapsible)

### Stories for panes in isolation (mocked data)

### Visual regression
- Screenshot comparison via Chromatic (preferred) or Percy
- Catches: CSS regressions, theme inconsistencies, responsive breakpoints
- Run on every PR (~3 min)

## Success criteria

- Light + dark mode variants
- Visual regression catches theme/CSS changes

title: M3b: E2E — chat feels like a real chat in both modes
description:

**Scenarios (Playwright):**
1. Send "list files" → bash tool card renders via `<Tool>` with terminal output.
2. Send "edit foo.md to fix a typo" → DiffView renders unified diff.
3. Trigger `/help` → command list shows.
4. Trigger `/reset` → new session created, old session appears in toolbar.
5. Type `/model haiku` → switch confirmed.

title: Infra: E2E logging harness (browser console + server logs → artifacts)
description:
**Goal:** Playwright fixture + reporter that captures ALL logs (browser console, network, server stdout) per test into structured artifact files. File: `e2e/fixtures/loggingHarness.ts` (~80 LOC).

**Why needed:** the user explicitly asked for "great, detailed logging so we can be sure that everything is working perfectly after implementation." E2E failures in CI with no log context = impossible to debug.

**Captures per test:**
- **Browser console:** all `console.log/warn/error` + page errors.
- **Network:** all requests/responses (method, URL, status, duration, bytes). Redact Authorization headers.
- **Server stdout/stderr:** piped from spawned backend process.
- **Test timeline:** structured events (`test.start`, `page.goto`, `assertion.fail`, etc.).
- **DOM snapshot on failure:** HTML dump for layout debugging.
- **Screenshot on failure:** native Playwright feature + our metadata overlay.

**Artifact layout (on failure):**
```
e2e-artifacts/
  <test-name>/
    browser-console.log           # timestamped lines
    network.har                    # HAR format for replay
    server.log                     # backend stdout/stderr
    dom.html
    screenshot.png
    timeline.json                  # structured events
```

**Fixture usage:**
```ts
test('mode flip', async ({ page, backend, logging }) => {
  logging.mark('starting mode flip test')
  await page.goto(backend.url)
  // ... test body
  logging.mark('verifying response')
  // ...
})
// If test fails → entire artifact dir written + linked in reporter output.
// If test passes → artifact dir deleted (unless BORING_E2E_KEEP=1 set).
```

**Reporter hooks:**
- On `test.afterEach` failure → flush artifacts + print artifact path to stderr.
- On test pass with `BORING_E2E_KEEP=1` → keep artifacts anyway.
- On `test.afterAll` → zip all artifacts into `e2e-artifacts.tar.gz` for CI upload.

**CI integration:**
- GitHub Actions: upload `e2e-artifacts.tar.gz` as workflow artifact (retained 7 days).
- Debug workflow: download artifact + local replay.

**Tests:**
- Passing test: artifacts not written (unless keep env var set).
- Failing test: all 6 artifact files present; tarball created.
- Server log capture: intentional backend error appears in `server.log`.
- HAR file: openable in Chrome DevTools.

**Logging (of the harness itself):** `[e2e-logging]` prefix. Log artifact dir path + flush events.

## Acceptance Criteria

Every e2e test that fails produces a full artifact bundle in `e2e-artifacts/<test-name>/`. CI uploads the bundle on failure. A developer can download + explore the bundle to understand exactly what happened. Log redaction works for Authorization headers. Artifacts auto-clean on pass (keep-env override available).

title: fix(agent): resolve @babel/core bundling failure in pnpm build
description:
**Problem:** `pnpm --dir packages/agent run build` fails at the tsup stage.

```
ERROR: Could not resolve "@babel/preset-typescript/package.json"
  ../../node_modules/.pnpm/@babel+core@7.29.0/node_modules/@babel/core/lib/config/files/module-types.js:165:36:
    165 │ ...t packageJson = require("@babel/preset-typescript/package.json");
```

**Cause:** tsup bundles `@vitejs/plugin-react` → `@babel/plugin-transform-react-jsx-self` → `@babel/core`, which tries to `require('@babel/preset-typescript/package.json')` at build time. preset-typescript isn't in the dep tree.

**Options:**
1. Add `@babel/preset-typescript` as devDependency.
2. Mark `@babel/core` and `@vitejs/plugin-react` as external in `tsup.config.ts` (they shouldn't be bundled into the published CLI anyway).
3. Strip Vite plugin imports from server/CLI bundle entrypoint.

**Recommended:** #2 — server/CLI bundle has no business pulling in vite plugins. Mark them external + any other dev-only deps.

**Impact:** blocks `npx @boring/agent` publish. Tests + typecheck still green; only prod build breaks.

**Verification:** `pnpm --dir packages/agent run build` exits 0 and emits `dist/bin/boring-agent.js`.

## Acceptance Criteria

pnpm build succeeds end-to-end (tsup + vite build + build:assert). dist/ contains: dist/shared.js, dist/server.js, dist/front.js, dist/bin/boring-agent.js. npx-like invocation works.
close_reason: Fixed tsup bundling failure by marking dev-only @vitejs/plugin-react and @babel/core as externals in packages/agent/tsup.config.ts (plus shared external constants). Verification: pnpm --dir packages/agent run build now succeeds end-to-end (tsup + vite build + build:assert). dist/bin/boring-agent.js emitted and build-artifacts check passes. Cross-review (claude-latest) verdict: ship. Commit: fab7bfc.

title: Infra-Test: Path-traversal attack corpus (fixture)
description:
**Goal:** Central test fixture of known path-traversal attack vectors. Every Workspace adapter runs it.

**Vectors (minimum 20):**
```
../etc/passwd
../../etc/passwd
..%2Fetc%2Fpasswd                          # URL-encoded
..\etc\passwd                             # Windows-style
/etc/passwd                                 # absolute
//etc//passwd                               # double-slash
./../etc/passwd
foo/../../etc/passwd
foo/../bar/../../etc/passwd
foo\x00/etc/passwd                         # null byte
foo\n/etc/passwd                           # newline injection
foo/../../../../../../../etc/passwd        # many levels
.../...//                                   # ninja
C:\Windows\System32                        # Windows absolute
/proc/self/environ                          # sensitive proc file
/dev/null
~/.ssh/id_rsa                               # tilde expansion
$HOME/.ssh/id_rsa                           # var expansion
<symlink-outside-workspace>                 # symlink escape (set up in test)
<path-ending-with-spaces>
```

title: M3a: Stream resumption — ring buffer + cursor route
description:
**Goal:** Survivable streaming across client disconnects. Files: `src/server/http/routes/chat.ts` (extended), `src/server/http/streamBuffer.ts`.

**Architecture:**
- **Transport-owned.** Harness has no `reconnect?()`. Any future harness inherits resume automatically.
- Per `(sessionId, turnId)`, keep an **in-memory ring buffer** of emitted chunks + monotonic `chunkIdx`.

**Ring buffer details:**
- Max chunks per buffer: 2000 (tuneable). Older chunks evicted; resume requests for evicted cursors get a 'replay from persistent store' fallback.
- TTL: 60s idle after turn completion. Dropped thereafter.
- Memory bound: ~2000 chunks × ~1KB avg = ~2MB per active turn. Acceptable for v1.

**Flow:**
1. On `POST /api/v1/agent/chat`: allocate buffer, stream chunks to client AND append to buffer.
2. On turn complete: mark buffer complete, hold for TTL, then GC.
3. On `GET /api/v1/agent/chat/:sessionId/:turnId?cursor=<n>`:
   - If buffer exists + turn in-flight: replay chunks `> cursor`, continue live stream.
   - If buffer exists + turn complete: replay from buffer, close.
   - If buffer evicted: fall back to `PiSessionStore.load(sessionId)` + serialize from `UIMessage[]` (lossy — no exact chunk-level replay, but semantically correct).
   - If turn not found: 404.

**Client-side (useChat):**
- Wired with `experimental_resume: true`.
- On page reload mid-stream: useChat calls the resume endpoint with last known cursor.

**Tests:**
- Disconnect mid-stream → reconnect → receives chunks `> cursor` only (no duplicates).
- Completed turn replay returns full transcript.
- Buffer eviction: stale buffers dropped after 60s (assert memory release).
- Evicted buffer resume: serves from SessionStore fallback.
- Cursor > buffer length → 416 Range Not Satisfiable.

**Logging:** `[resume]` prefix; log cursor + buffer size + eviction events.

## Acceptance Criteria

Disconnect mid-stream → reconnect receives chunks > cursor only (no duplicates). Completed replay returns full transcript. Buffer eviction after 60s. Evicted buffer falls back to SessionStore. Cursor > buffer → 416.
acceptance_criteria: Disconnect mid-stream → reconnect receives chunks > cursor only (no duplicates). Completed replay returns full transcript. Buffer eviction after 60s. Evicted buffer falls back to SessionStore. Cursor > buffer → 416.
close_reason: shipped

title: M3a: Abort propagation (useChat.stop → pi → sandbox.exec)
description:
**Goal:** `useChat.stop()` cleanly halts the full pipeline. Files: chat route + harness + sandbox adapters.

**Chain:**
1. Client `stop()` → fetch abort.
2. Route's AbortController signals.
3. Passes to `harness.sendMessage(..., {abortSignal})`.
4. Harness passes to pi's `runTurn({abortSignal})`.
5. Pi propagates to any in-flight `tool.execute({abortSignal})`.
6. Our tools pass to `sandbox.exec({abortSignal})`.
7. Sandbox adapter kills the process (bwrap) or aborts the Vercel runCommand.

**Accepted risk:** pi's abort propagation is unproven for all cases. Test each mode in M1 and M2.

**Tests:** mid-stream abort halts within 200ms end-to-end; assert no orphaned bwrap child processes; assert sandbox.exec promise rejects with AbortError.

## Acceptance Criteria

Mid-stream abort halts within 200ms end-to-end. No orphaned bwrap child processes. sandbox.exec rejects with AbortError.
acceptance_criteria: Mid-stream abort halts within 200ms end-to-end. No orphaned bwrap child processes. sandbox.exec rejects with AbortError.
close_reason: shipped

title: Phase 3a: Workspace Bridge — State Store + Commands
description:
## Goal
WorkspaceBridge implementation: command dispatcher, typed event system, selector-based subscriptions, usePanelActions hook, and bridge command validation. Bridge reads/writes the single Zustand store.

## Full Type Definitions

### WorkspaceBridge interface
\`\`\`typescript
interface WorkspaceBridge {
  // Read (agent sees what user sees)
  getOpenPanels(): PanelState[]
  getActiveFile(): string | null
  getDirtyFiles(): string[]
  getVisibleFiles(): string[]

  // Write (agent acts like a user) — all return Promise<CommandResult>
  openFile(path: string, opts?: { mode?: 'view' | 'edit' | 'diff' }): Promise<CommandResult>
  openPanel(config: DynamicPaneConfig): Promise<CommandResult>
  closePanel(id: string): Promise<CommandResult>
  expandToFile(path: string): Promise<CommandResult>
  showNotification(msg: string, level?: 'info' | 'warn' | 'error'): Promise<CommandResult>
  navigateToLine(file: string, line: number): Promise<CommandResult>
  markDirty(path: string): void
  markClean(path: string): void

  // Subscribe — typed events with discriminated union
  subscribe<K extends keyof BridgeEventMap>(
    event: K,
    handler: (data: BridgeEventMap[K]) => void
  ): Unsubscribe

  // Selector-based subscription (fires only when selected slice changes)
  select<T>(selector: (state: WorkspaceState) => T, handler: (value: T) => void): Unsubscribe

  // Connectivity
  onDisconnect(handler: () => void): Unsubscribe
  onReconnect(handler: () => void): Unsubscribe
}
\`\`\`

### CommandResult
\`\`\`typescript
interface CommandResult {
  seq: number
  status: 'ok' | 'error'
  error?: { code: string, message: string }
}
\`\`\`

### BridgeEventMap (exhaustive, no stringly typing)
\`\`\`typescript
interface BridgeEventMap {
  'panel:opened': { panelId: string; params: Record<string, unknown> }
  'panel:closed': { panelId: string }
  'panel:activated': { panelId: string; previousPanelId: string | null }
  'file:opened': { path: string; mode: 'view' | 'edit' | 'diff' }
  'file:saved': { path: string }
  'file:dirty': { path: string; dirty: boolean }
  'sidebar:toggled': { collapsed: boolean }
  'notification:shown': { message: string; level: 'info' | 'warn' | 'error' }
  'pane:error': { panelId: string; error: string; stack?: string }
}
// Apps extend via module augmentation:
// declare module '@boring/workspace' {
//   interface BridgeEventMap { 'myapp:custom': { payload: string } }
// }
\`\`\`

### UIStatePut (PUT body format)
\`\`\`typescript
interface UIStatePut {
  v: 1                                    // protocol version
  causedBy: 'user' | 'agent' | 'restore' // prevents agent echo loops
  openPanels: PanelState[]
  activePanel: string | null
  activeFile: string | null
  visibleFiles: string[]                  // file paths shown in tree
  dirtyFiles: string[]                    // files with unsaved changes
}
\`\`\`

## What to implement

### bridge/commands.ts — command definitions + dispatcher
- Command definitions with per-kind dispatch:
  - openFile(path, opts?) → file-type routing via PanelRegistry.resolve(), then addPanel via DockviewShellApi
  - openPanel(config) → addPanel with explicit component. Idempotent: duplicate ID activates existing (prefer_existing: true)
  - closePanel(id) → removePanel. Reject if essential flag is set.
  - showNotification(msg, level) → add to notifications slice in store
  - navigateToLine(file, line) → openFile + set cursor position via panel params
  - expandToFile(path) → expand tree to path, scroll to file
  - markDirty(path) / markClean(path) → update dirtyFiles map in store
- All write commands return Promise<CommandResult>
- Fire-and-forget works (just don't await)
- Max 50 open panels per workspace (hard cap, not rate)

### bridge/useWorkspaceBridge.ts — hook for panes
- Returns WorkspaceBridge interface
- Backed by the single Zustand store
- Local commands (from user) → immediate Zustand update + PUT state
- Agent commands (from SSE) → same Zustand update code path
- causedBy field distinguishes source on PUT

### hooks/usePanelActions.ts — exported public hook
- open(path): resolve file type → open in correct editor, activate panel
- close(panelId): close panel, activate next tab
- activate(panelId): bring panel to front
- openToSide(path): open file in a new panel beside current
- Thin wrapper around DockviewShellApi + bridge methods
- Extracted from v1's usePanelActions.js (16 KB). v2 is much smaller — only core open/close/activate. File-specific logic stays in panes.

### Bridge State Scope
- Bridge sends: openPanels, activePanel, activeFile, visibleFiles (file tree paths), dirtyFiles
- Bridge does NOT send: full file tree state (agent queries files via its own tools)
- This eliminates bandwidth problem of broadcasting 10K+ entries on every panel change

### dirtyFiles slice
- Map<string, { panelId: string, savedAt: number | null }>
- Updated by editors via bridge.markDirty/markClean
- Exposed to agents via bridge.getDirtyFiles()
- Enables auto-save-before-commit workflows

## SECURITY-CRITICAL: Bridge Command Validation

Every bridge command passes through a per-kind Zod validator before execution. Unknown kinds are rejected. This runs client-side on SSE commands AND is enforced server-side by @boring/agent.

| Kind | Params | Constraints |
|------|--------|-------------|
| openFile | path: string, mode?: 'view'\|'edit'\|'diff' | path max 1024 chars, no path traversal (../) |
| openPanel | id: string, component: string, params?: Record<string, JsonSerializable> | id alphanum+dash max 64, component must exist in registry, params max 16KB |
| closePanel | id: string | must be open, must NOT be essential |
| showNotification | msg: string, level?: 'info'\|'warn'\|'error' | msg max 500 chars, plain text only (NO HTML) |
| navigateToLine | file: string, line: number | file path validated same as openFile, line positive integer |
| expandToFile | path: string | path validated same as openFile |

### Path parameter safety
openFile, navigateToLine, expandToFile paths MUST NOT shortcircuit to direct file reads. All path params go through same validation as agent-side fileRoutes.

### Text parameter safety
showNotification msg, panel titles: rendered as React text nodes ({msg}), NEVER via dangerouslySetInnerHTML. Truncated to max length.

### Rate limiting
Deferred to post-launch. Bridge commands are infrequent (~1 per agent turn). Max 50 open panels hard cap enforced.

## Risk 14: Bridge command lifecycle (async but returns Promise)
bridge.openFile('/file.ts') is async internally (needs file-type resolution + DockviewShellApi.addPanel). Agent calls openFile then immediately reads getOpenPanels() — file isn't there yet.

Mitigation: All commands return Promise<CommandResult>. Agent can await:
\`\`\`typescript
const { seq, status } = await bridge.openFile('/file.ts')
const panels = bridge.getOpenPanels()  // now includes the file
\`\`\`

## v1 References
- frontendState.js (2.6 KB) — current UI state bridge: panel snapshots, command queuing
- uiStateImpl.ts (201 lines) — state shape: { openPanels, activeFile, commands }
- usePanelActions.js (16 KB) — open/close/activate, file-to-panel routing

## Success Criteria
- All bridge commands work and return CommandResult
- Events fire for all state changes (each event type tested)
- Selector subscriptions only fire when selected slice changes
- dirtyFiles accurately tracks unsaved changes
- Module augmentation works for custom events
- usePanelActions hook: open, close, activate work correctly
- Validation rejects invalid commands (bad paths, essential panels, oversized params)
- Text params rendered safely (no XSS)
- Max 50 panels enforced
- causedBy correctly set on PUT body for user vs agent vs restore

## Unit Tests
- Each bridge command: happy path + error path (bad params, essential, max panels)
- BridgeEventMap: each event type fires correctly
- select() subscription: fires on slice change, NOT on unrelated changes
- dirtyFiles: markDirty adds entry, markClean removes, getDirtyFiles returns correct list
- usePanelActions: open resolves file type correctly, close activates next tab
- Zod validation: each command kind validated with correct constraints
- Path validation: ../ rejected, max length enforced
- Text validation: >500 chars truncated, HTML not interpreted
- causedBy: user actions get 'user', SSE commands get 'agent', restore gets 'restore'
- Idempotency: openPanel with duplicate ID activates existing (no second panel)
close_reason: done
